name: Structural ETF Style/Sector-Spread OU z-Reversion (cash legs, half-life-gated, dollar+beta-neutral)
rationale: Composition-anchoring is a real mechanism that dodges the mined-cointegration trap, and the data/test plumbing is genuinely strong (verified: 11y OHLCV cached for SPY/QQQ/IWM/XLY/XLP, 2766 bars covering all named crises), but two independent Serious red-team verdicts (survives=false) land: the edge sits inside a documented decayed anomaly whose tail is short-correlation in disguise against the very VRP incumbent it claims to diversify, SPY/QQQ's cap-weighted premise is structurally broken in the post-2022 concentration regime, frozen-at-entry beta mis-hedges exactly in the vol spikes it must survive, and the headline 0.4-0.7 Sharpe is an unrun literature number not an out-of-sample result.
bestUseInEnsemble: Only the narrowest sleeve earns a place: short-horizon (tightest half-life) ETF creation/redemption flow reversion on the two pairs with a plausibly bounded relationship, IWF/IWD and XLY/XLP, sized small and force-closed on a VIX/trend flip rather than only suppressing new entries. Drop SPY/QQQ (cap-weight drift kills the fair-value premise) and treat SPY/IWM as marginal. Even then its tail co-moves with the put-selling book's worst weeks, so it diversifies the mean but not the drawdown; admit it only after the walk-forward and a tail/co-skew orthogonality test (worst-decile months regressed on a short-VIX/straddle-sale series, crisis loading must be statistically zero) actually come back positive. Until then it is a research candidate, not a live diversifier.

spec.name: Structural ETF Style/Sector-Spread OU z-Reversion (cash legs, half-life-gated, dollar+beta-neutral)
spec.mechanism: Structural-flow + behavioral mean reversion, NOT mined cointegration. Each traded spread is between two ETFs that share overlapping constituents and a common factor, so the long-run relationship is pinned by COMPOSITION not history: growth/value (IWF/IWD, both subsets of the Russell 1000), cyclical/defensive (XLY/XLP, both S&P 500 sectors), broad/tech (SPY/QQQ, ~85% mega-cap overlap), large/small (SPY/IWM, common beta). Relative price is pushed off composition-implied fair value by (1) short-horizon style-rotation overshoot (retail/momentum crowding into growth or cyclicals) and (2) ETF primary-market creation/redemption + flow imbalances that move one leg faster than its basket. The authorized participant arb keeps each ETF near its own NAV, but does NOT enforce the cross-ETF relative value, so the relative mispricing persists for days and the liquidity provider earns the snap-back. ADF/Engle-Granger are used ONLY to confirm stationarity and estimate the OU half-life on the trailing window, never to scan for pairs — the cointegrating vector is economically anchored, which is why this is a flow/rotation risk premium rather than a curve-fit. Return is driven by the value/growth + cyclical/defensive rotation factor and ETF-flow reversion, structurally distinct from the incumbent's variance-risk-premium (no option IV is sold).
spec.hypothesisH0: For each composition-linked ETF pair, the beta-neutral log-price spread is a martingale (random walk) at the daily horizon after costs: conditional on |z|>=2 entry plus the half-life gate, the post-entry change in the spread toward its rolling mean has expectation <= the round-trip transaction cost. Equivalently the OU mean-reversion speed kappa estimated out-of-sample is statistically indistinguishable from zero, and the net Sharpe of the dollar+beta-neutral basket is <= 0.
spec.hypothesisH1: Conditional on |z|>=2 AND a half-life in [5,30] trading days AND trailing ADF p<0.05, the spread reverts with positive OU kappa so that expected captured reversion (entry |z| down to exit |z|, scaled by spread sd) exceeds round-trip ETF cost, yielding a positive net edge per trade and an annualized net Sharpe in the ~0.4-0.7 range historically documented for OU-spread pairs, with a left tail that partially co-times with vol spikes but a mean return uncorrelated with short-vol P&L.
spec.signalDefinition: Universe of structurally-linked pairs P = {(IWF,IWD), (XLY,XLP), (SPY,QQQ), (SPY,IWM)} from the manifest (all penny-tight, 10y daily OHLCV available via yahoo-finance2). For pair (A,B), all on daily adjusted close: (1) HEDGE RATIO beta_t = rolling OLS slope of logP_A on logP_B over a 252-day trailing window (Kalman-filter state optional but OLS is the baseline; refit daily). (2) SPREAD s_t = logP_A,t - beta_t * logP_B,t. (3) OU FIT on trailing window W = round(4 * H_prev) clipped to [60,252] days: regress s_t - s_{t-1} on s_{t-1} (discrete OU), giving slope phi; kappa = -ln(1+phi) per day (continuous), HALF-LIFE H = ln(2)/kappa in trading days; require phi<0 (mean-reverting) else pair is inactive. (4) Z-SCORE z_t = (s_t - mean_W(s)) / sd_W(s) using the same window W (~3-5x half-life). (5) REGIME OVERLAY from the manifest 154-172 engineered features, computed on the dollar-neutral spread series or on leg A: Hurst exponent (require Hurst <= 0.5, i.e. anti-persistent/mean-reverting, NOT trending) and a vol-spike flag VIXflag = (VIX > its trailing 60-day 80th percentile) used to suppress new entries. (6) ADF p-value on s_t over window W (statsmodels adfuller) required < 0.05. All computations are pure pandas/numpy on manifest OHLCV + VIX; no new data.
spec.entryRule: Open a market-neutral position in pair (A,B) at the next session's close when ALL hold simultaneously on the signal date: (a) |z_t| >= 2.0; (b) estimated half-life H in [5, 30] trading days (gates out both noise-fast and non-reverting/slow regimes); (c) ADF p < 0.05 on the trailing window; (d) Hurst <= 0.5 on the spread (mean-reverting regime confirmed by the manifest regime feature); (e) NOT a fresh vol-spike entry: VIXflag is False (VIX below its trailing 60-day 80th pct) — left-tail guard against divergence-during-vol-spike. Direction: if z_t >= +2 the spread is rich => SHORT A / LONG B; if z_t <= -2 => LONG A / SHORT B. Leg sizing is dollar-neutral AND beta-neutral: short/long beta_t * (dollar value of A) of B so the net factor beta is ~0. At most one open position per pair; pairs are independent across the 4-pair book.
spec.exitRule: Close the position (both legs, at next close) on the FIRST of: (1) PROFIT/REVERSION: |z_t| <= 0.3 (mean essentially recovered); (2) TIME-STOP: holding period exceeds 2 * H_entry trading days capped at 30 (the spread failed to revert within a few half-lives => thesis stale); (3) STOP-OUT: |z_t| >= 3.5 (structural break / divergence — cut the left tail hard); (4) BREAK-CONFIRM: trailing ADF p rises above 0.10 OR refit phi flips to >=0 (cointegration/reversion has broken => exit even if z is mid-range). Re-hedge beta_t is held at entry value for the life of the trade (no intra-trade re-beta) to avoid path-dependent leakage; a new beta is only estimated for the next entry.
spec.instruments: Cash long/short of two liquid ETFs per pair, dollar-neutral and beta-neutral, from the manifest set only: IWF/IWD, XLY/XLP, SPY/QQQ, SPY/IWM. No options, no assignment risk, no overnight gap on a single name (both legs hedge). Equal risk budget per pair; up to 4 concurrent independent pair positions.
spec.positionSizing: Fixed capital C, equal-weight across the 4 pairs => per-pair gross budget g = C * L / 4 where L is target gross leverage (baseline L = 1.0, i.e. 100% gross deployed when all 4 are on; max L = 1.5). Within a pair, gross g splits across the two legs scaled by beta so the position is dollar- and beta-neutral: notional_A = g / (1 + beta_t), notional_B = beta_t * notional_A. RISK CAP: size each pair to a target spread-vol budget — scale the per-pair notional so that (position gross) * (annualized spread sd) <= 8% of (C/4), i.e. cap each leg-pair's standalone annualized vol contribution at ~8% of its capital slice; if estimated spread vol is high, shrink notional below g. Hard portfolio cap: total gross <= 1.5*C; if more than 4 signals (not possible here with 4 pairs) or a pair re-triggers, never stack beyond one unit per pair. Reserve >= 25% of C as cash buffer for mark-to-market drawdown on the short legs. PnL, position sizing, and capital_events handled by the Neon Postgres sim layer (simulated_trades + capital_events).
spec.dataSources: FULLY COVERED by manifest — nothing new. yahoo-finance2 10y daily adjusted OHLCV on SPY, QQQ, IWM, IWF, IWD, XLY, XLP (all explicitly listed); VIX (^VIX) for the vol-spike entry filter. Rolling OLS/Kalman beta, OU half-life, kappa, ADF (statsmodels.tsa.stattools.adfuller), and z-score are pure pandas/numpy. Manifest 154-172 engineered features supply the Hurst exponent and regime flags as a free entry overlay. Neon sim layer (simulated_trades + capital_events) handles sizing and PnL accounting. Black-Scholes engine, options chains, iTransformer forecast, and the 6-model ensemble are NOT required for the core strategy (optional regime confirmation only).
spec.capacityNote: Very high capacity — this is the strategy's strongest axis. All legs are the most liquid ETFs in existence (SPY/QQQ/IWM ADV in the $10-30B range; IWF/IWD/XLY/XLP $0.5-3B). A retail or small-fund book of $0.1-50M deploys with negligible market impact at EOD; the binding constraint is the modest number of independent signals (only 4 structurally-justified pairs, lowly correlated but not independent — IWF/IWD and SPY/QQQ both load on the growth factor), so the book is signal-constrained, not capacity-constrained. Trade frequency is deliberately low (wide 2.0-sigma bands + 5-30d half-life gate) => roughly 4-12 round trips per pair per year, ~20-50 trades/yr across the book — turnover is suppressed on purpose to keep total annual cost drag a small fraction of gross edge. The orthogonality caveat is honestly priced (graded 6): the left tail partially co-times with vol spikes / correlation breaks (pairs returns load positively on summed-vol at selection), mitigated by the VIX entry filter, Hurst gate, and 3.5-sigma hard stop, but not eliminated.
spec.falsifier: Run the spec on 10y manifest daily data for all 4 pairs with realistic costs (8bps round-trip incl. 2-leg bid/ask + slippage, $0 ETF commission). The strategy is REFUTED if ANY of: (1) out-of-sample (walk-forward, params frozen on first 5y, tested on last 5y) net mean return per trade <= net cost, i.e. net edge per round trip <= 0 bps; OR (2) net annualized Sharpe of the dollar+beta-neutral book <= 0.3 over the full sample; OR (3) the half-life gate fails to add value — the subset of entries with H in [5,30] does NOT show higher net per-trade reversion than entries with H outside [5,30] (gate is non-discriminating, implying the kappa estimate is noise); OR (4) the return stream is NOT orthogonal enough — its monthly returns regress on a short-vol/VRP proxy (e.g. short-VIX or a systematic at-the-money straddle-sale series) with a beta whose sign and magnitude make it a disguised short-vol trade (|R^2| > 0.25 AND negative loading), collapsing the orthogonality thesis; OR (5) post-2010 net Sharpe has decayed to <= 0 (consistent with the documented pairs-alpha decay from ~15.6%/yr pre-2000 to ~6.4% post-2010), showing the structural-ETF variant did not escape the decay that killed single-stock pairs.

redteam.lens: COSTS and CAPACITY
redteam.kill:
Gross edge beats round-trip cost so Fatal is dishonest. But estCostBpsPerTrade of 8 understates the repo own costs.ts model: 5bps per side per leg times 4 leg-crossings equals 10bps of gross, plus about 1.5bps omitted short borrow equals about 11.5bps, roughly 40 percent above 8. Penny-tight is optimistic for IWD/XLP/XLY at 2-4bps per side. Even so a z=2 to 0.3 reversion captures 250-500bps gross on a winner, so cost is only 3-5 percent of capture. Capacity is a genuine strength: most liquid ETFs, negligible EOD impact. Damage is aggregate not per-trade.</killArgument>
</invoke>
redteam.mitigation: Raise estCostBpsPerTrade to 11-13bps and add a short-borrow term. Use per-pair costs: SPY/QQQ/IWM about 8bps, IWF/IWD and XLY/XLP about 12-14bps round-trip. Fix blended-edge accounting since cost is paid on every exit while time-stops capture zero and the 3.5-sigma stop-out is a loss. Set a minimum capital near 250k or require fractional shares so SPY/QQQ legs do not round to under 2 shares and break dollar-plus-beta neutrality.
redteam.residual: Thin but positive. Per-trade the gross capture of 250-500bps on a winner beats honest 11.5-14bps cost. Aggregate is the worry: about 2 percent per year on capital, halvable by cost underestimate plus stop-out haircut, and the 8 percent vol cap plus low signal count likely caps realized Sharpe near the falsifier 0.3 floor. Survives on costs; sub-250k accounts fail on granularity not costs.

redteam.lens: DATA-SNOOPING, DECAY & ORTHOGONALITY
redteam.kill: The edge sits inside a documented, decayed anomaly the spec admits it has not escaped, AND its tail is short-vol/short-correlation in disguise. (a) Mechanism: composition-anchoring genuinely avoids the mined-cointegration multiple-comparisons trap (good, real credit), but the empirical kill is convergence-reliability decay (Do & Faff / GGR extensions): pairs alpha fell from ~11%/yr (1962-2002) to weak/regime-dependent post-2008, and the documented cause is NOT crowding but that trailing-window substitutes stop being forward-window substitutes. Composition-anchoring does not fix this -- the value/growth (IWF/IWD) and broad/tech (SPY/QQQ) relationships TRENDED for multi-year stretches (growth crushed value 2015-2021; anticipated mean reversion repeatedly failed to materialize). The ADF<0.05 + half-life[5,30d] gates mechanically exclude exactly the trending regimes, which is signal-selection survivorship, not edge. The cited ~0.4-0.7 Sharpe is a literature number, not an out-of-sample post-cost result -- the walk-forward falsifier is promised but UNRUN, and post-2010 net Sharpe on liquid-ETF cointegration variants is widely <=0.3 after realistic 2-leg costs. Extreme liquidity of SPY/QQQ/IWM (sold as a capacity virtue) is, under this lens, evidence AGAINST durable un-arbitraged relative-value edge on the most-watched relationships on earth. (b) Orthogonality: the spec self-grades 6/10 and concedes the left tail co-times with vol spikes and pairs returns load positively on summed-vol at selection -- that is an admission the tail is short-correlation/short-dispersion, the same crisis-risk family as the incumbent's variance risk premium (the Aug-2007 quant-quake payoff). The mean may be uncorrelated with short-vol P&L, but full-distribution orthogonality is what's needed to diversify the book's tail, and by its own description it adds CORRELATED tail risk on top of a VRP incumbent rather than diversifying it.
redteam.mitigation: For (a) to survive: actually RUN the walk-forward falsifier (params frozen on first 5y, tested on last 5y, 8bps round-trip 2-leg cost) and show net per-trade edge > 0 AND full-sample net Sharpe > 0.3 AND post-2010 subsample Sharpe > 0 -- i.e. demonstrate the structural variant escaped the decay rather than asserting it. Also show the half-life/ADF gate adds value (falsifier 3) so the kappa estimate is not just noise-mining the trailing window. For (b) to survive: replace the weak monthly-mean orthogonality test (falsifier 4) with a TAIL/co-skew test -- regress the worst-decile months (not the mean) on a short-VIX / ATM-straddle-sale series and on a dispersion/correlation-swap proxy; survival requires the crisis-month loading to be statistically zero, not just low R^2 in the mean. Then prove the trade diversifies the incumbent's drawdowns by overlaying P&L on the 5-6 worst VRP/short-vol weeks (Aug 2007, Feb 2018, Mar 2020, Aug 2024) and showing non-coincident losses.
redteam.residual: Some residual edge plausibly survives, but it is thin and conditional, not the clean orthogonal diversifier claimed. The genuinely defensible core is short-horizon ETF-flow/creation-redemption reversion (days-scale, AP arb pins each ETF to its own NAV but not the cross-ETF spread) and style-rotation overshoot -- these are real microstructure/behavioral effects distinct from selling IV. That residual is most credible on the FASTEST mean-reverting, lowest-visibility pair (SPY/IWM common-beta flow imbalances) at the tightest half-lives, and least credible on the multi-year-trending value/growth spread. But after the decay attack it is a low-single-digit-Sharpe, regime-gated carry whose tail is correlated with the incumbent's VRP tail, so as a portfolio addition its risk-adjusted, tail-aware marginal contribution is probably near zero until the unrun out-of-sample and tail-orthogonality tests come back positive. Net: do not deploy on the asserted thesis; demote to a research candidate pending the two falsifiers actually being executed.

redteam.lens: REGIME & ROBUSTNESS (regime-dependence, parameter sensitivity, crowding, tail risk; survival across 2018-Q4, Mar-2020, 2022, Aug-2024)
redteam.kill: The edge is regime-conditional in exactly the wrong direction: these are not stationary noise-spreads but factor spreads carrying persistent multi-year trends, and the |z|>=2 entry rule mechanically shorts the spread that is about to keep trending. Concretely: (1) SPY/QQQ is not composition-anchored to a fixed ratio — it is cap-weighted, so QQQ's relative value is driven by a handful of mega-caps whose index weight keeps RISING. The 2023-2026 AI-capex concentration regime produced a cap-weight vs equal-weight NASDAQ spread of ~45.7% vs ~27.5% over a single year (Invesco/24-7 Wall St). A spread with that much drift has a unit root over the horizons that matter; ADF/Engle-Granger run on a 60-252d trailing window will lag the regime shift and certify 'stationary, mean-reverting' right before the break, then the position is run over. (2) 2022 was a ~12-month trending growth-to-value / tech-selloff regime — IWF/IWD and SPY/QQQ trended one direction for the better part of a year; a 5-30d half-life book repeatedly enters at |z|=2 against the trend and exits at the 3.5-sigma stop, bleeding the full -1.5 sigma per cycle. (3) Mar-2020: value underperformed growth violently and valuation dispersion 'widened to a record high' (Research Affiliates); a LONG-IWD/SHORT-IWF entry gets steamrolled, and the VIXflag only suppresses NEW entries — it does NOT close positions already on, and the 3.5-sigma stop fires only after a large realized loss. Worse, beta_t is frozen at entry 'to avoid path-dependent leakage,' but cross-leg betas blow out in a vol spike, so 'beta-neutral' becomes badly mis-hedged precisely in the tail it is meant to survive. (4) Aug-2024 was an overnight gap (Topix -12%, record VIX spike); the VIX entry filter and the 3.5-sigma stop are both next-close mechanisms — useless against an overnight gap that materializes before any exit can trigger. (5) Crowding/decay: the literature is explicit that simple Engle-Granger/distance pairs alpha decayed from ~11%/0.55 Sharpe (GGR pre-2002) to ~zero after costs post-2010 (Yale/Zhu, harbourfront); the spec's own falsifier #5 concedes this, and the 'structural ETF' framing does not obviously escape it — ETF AP arbitrage is among the MOST crowded RV trades. The spec even self-grades the orthogonality caveat a 6/10 and admits the left tail co-times with vol spikes and that returns load positively on summed-vol at selection — i.e. it is a short-correlation/short-vol trade in disguise on the tail, which is the definition of a strategy that 'works in bull/low-vol and blows up in crises.'
redteam.mitigation: For the edge to survive this lens it would need: (a) regime-conditional REGIME EXIT, not just entry suppression — the VIXflag and a trend/Hurst flip must force-CLOSE open positions, not merely block new ones; (b) live beta re-hedging (or at minimum a beta-break stop) rather than frozen-at-entry beta, since the dollar/beta-neutrality that the whole tail-safety thesis rests on degrades fastest exactly when needed; (c) an explicit trend-overlay veto that stands DOWN the book during multi-month directional factor regimes (2022-style), because a mean-reversion gate estimated on a trailing window is structurally late; (d) dropping or heavily down-weighting SPY/QQQ and SPY/IWM, whose cap-weighting and small/large secular dispersion make the 'fixed composition fair value' premise false (IWF/IWD and XLY/XLP are the only pairs with a plausible bounded relationship, and even those broke in 2020); (e) gap risk priced explicitly (overnight stop-loss is impossible to honor on a gap, so position sizing must assume the 3.5-sigma stop slips); (f) a walk-forward proving NET Sharpe>0.3 with params frozen pre-2018 and tested THROUGH 2018-Q4/2020/2022/2024 inclusive, not on a benign sub-sample. Until the falsifier is actually run across those four windows with frozen params, skepticism is the correct default.
redteam.residual: Some residual edge plausibly survives, but only in a narrowed, lower-Sharpe form. The genuine flow/creation-redemption snap-back on IWF/IWD and XLY/XLP in calm/low-vol regimes is real and is the part most distinct from the incumbent VRP book — this is probably a ~0.3-0.5 Sharpe carry-and-reversion sleeve in normal markets, consistent with the documented OU-pairs ~0.4-0.7 range BEFORE crowding decay. But the strategy as specified does not survive the named crisis windows: it is long correlation/short dispersion in the tail, frozen-beta makes the hedge fail in vol spikes, the trailing stationarity gates are regime-lagging, and SPY/QQQ's premise is structurally broken in the post-2022 concentration regime. Net residual edge after this attack: positive in bull/low-vol, materially negative-to-flat across 2018-Q4 / Mar-2020 / 2022 / Aug-2024, so the full-sample net Sharpe is likely dragged toward the falsifier's 0.3 refutation line and the orthogonality-to-short-vol thesis (falsifier #4) is the most likely failure point. Edge is regime-contingent and tail-fragile, not robust — survives=false under default skepticism.

name: TSM-Gate: 12-1 time-series momentum, ML-concordance as veto/sizing overlay
family: ML-forecast-driven directional trading: the iTransformer 60d path + 6-model ensemble + existing concordance gate are used as a confidence filter / cross-sectional ranker on top of documented base anomalies (cross-sectional & time-series momentum), NOT as standalone alpha. Directional exposure is taken in beta-hedged equity (dollar-neutral) or defined-risk option structures, only when model_confidence is high and the two model families concord.
rationale: Base 12-1 TSM is a real, cost-surviving, structurally orthogonal diversifier already implemented in the repo (xs_momentum/ts_trend on the full engine.ts walk-forward), but the strategy's actual novelty -- the ML concordance veto + static confidence tilt -- is refuted on all three lenses: it adds round-trip cost with near-coinflip information, is driven by a static test-set scalar (hf-model.ts:415) that cannot fire in a crisis, runs the wrong horizon vs the live code (index 29 not 59), and is unfalsifiable until a >=200 name-month forward log exists; keep the base sleeve, discard the overlay as unproven.
bestUseInEnsemble: As a small standalone long/flat trend sleeve diversifying the short-vol CSP book -- positive-skew/long-vol crisis-alpha vs the CSP's negative skew, genuinely different drawdown regimes -- but ONLY the base TSM leg, broadened toward the diversified TREND_ETFS sleeve (TLT/IEF/GLD/DBC/EEM/EFA/VNQ) the literature actually documents rather than single equities; the ML gate must be carried as a forward-logged experiment with zero capital weight until it clears its own falsifier (vetoed-vs-passed forward-return gap significant AND larger than the ~10bps per-toggle cost), at which point it could become a thin drawdown filter -- not before.

spec.name: TSM-Gate: 12-1 time-series momentum, ML-concordance as veto/sizing overlay
spec.family: ML-forecast-as-confirmation-gate on a documented base anomaly (time-series momentum). The iTransformer 60d path + 6-model ensemble + concordance card are used ONLY as a binary veto and a static per-name size tilt, never as standalone alpha. Directional exposure is vol-targeted equity, long-only by default (long/flat), optionally dollar-neutral long/short.
spec.mechanism: Time-series (12-1) momentum is a ~century-documented under-reaction / institutional-flow premium netting ~0.4-0.7 Sharpe after costs and persisting ~12m before reversing (Moskowitz-Ooi-Pedersen; Hurst/AQR). The EDGE is the trend itself. A 50-58% directional classifier cannot be a standalone alpha source, but a barely-better-than-coinflip sign predictor IS useful as a drawdown FILTER: skip the subset of trend signals the ML flatly contradicts, where a disproportionate share of choppy/whipsaw losses live. The overlay converts the weak model from 'alpha' (which it fails as) into 'veto + leverage tilt' on a robust base signal. Orthogonality: trend-following is convex/long-vol-like (positive skew, profits in sustained moves incl. trending crashes), the structural opposite of the incumbent short-vol put-selling (negative skew, profits in calm). Their drawdowns occur in different regimes, so TSM-Gate naturally hedges the CSP book's tail.
spec.hypothesisH0: On the ~80-name optionable large-cap + ETF universe, a vol-targeted 12-1 time-series-momentum long/flat book earns net Sharpe <= 0 after realistic frictions over 2015-2025; AND adding the ML concordance veto + model_confidence size tilt does NOT improve the base TSM book's net Sharpe or reduce its max drawdown by a statistically meaningful margin (delta-Sharpe <= 0 and delta-MaxDD >= 0 within noise). I.e., neither the trend leg nor the gate pays for its costs.
spec.hypothesisH1: The base vol-targeted 12-1 TSM book earns net Sharpe >= 0.4 over 2015-2025 (backtestable today from 10y OHLCV alone); AND the ML veto leg, evaluated on forward-logged forecasts (live, not historically reconstructable), raises the gated book's net Sharpe by >= 0.15 and/or cuts max drawdown by >= 15% relative to the ungated base, with realized daily-NAV correlation to the incumbent CSP book <= 0.2.
spec.signalDefinition: Two-leg signal per name i, evaluated monthly on the last trading day (EOD). Base trend (fully computable from 10y daily OHLCV via fetchOHLCV(symbol,10), NOT from an existing engineered feature — engine only has return_60d at index 38, no 252d/21d feature): let P=close. r_12_1[i] = P[t-21]/P[t-252] - 1 (12-month total return skipping the most recent ~21 trading days). s[i] = sign(r_12_1[i]). Volatility for risk-targeting: sigma[i] = max(parkinson_vol_20d, garman_klass_vol_20d) from itransformer-features indices 84/85 (already annualized), floored at 0.10. ML GATE (live outputs of runHFInference + generatePrediction): itf60 = forecast_returns[59] (60d-ahead cumulative return from per-stock iTransformer; NOTE the live ConcordanceCard currently keys the gate off forecast_returns[29]=30d via getDirection() at +/-0.5% bands — for a 60d horizon use index 59, both are logged). itfDir = 'bullish' if 100*itf60 > 0.5, 'bearish' if < -0.5, else 'neutral' (exact getDirection() logic from ConcordanceCard.tsx:41-45). ens = ensembleScore in [-100,100] from generatePrediction. conf = model_confidence in [0,1] = clamp(dir_acc_30d/100,0,1) — IMPORTANT: this is a STATIC per-stock test-set accuracy scalar (hf-model.ts:415), constant through time, so 'size up on confidence' is a fixed per-name leverage tilt, not a dynamic signal.
spec.entryRule: Monthly rebalance (last EOD of month). For each name: compute s[i], itfDir, ens, conf. Take/hold a LONG only if ALL hold: s[i] > 0 AND itfDir != 'bearish' AND ens > -20. Take/hold SHORT (or go flat if long-only) only if ALL hold: s[i] < 0 AND itfDir != 'bullish' AND ens < 20. If the trend sign and the gate conflict (e.g. s>0 but itfDir=='bearish' or ens<=-20), go FLAT in that name (this is the veto — the gate never initiates a trade against the trend, it only blocks trend trades the model contradicts). Rank-cap: hold at most the top N=20 names by |r_12_1| that pass the gate, to bound turnover and concentrate in strongest trends. Exclude any name with earnings inside the next 5 trading days only if running the optional short leg (long leg ignores earnings — long stock has no assignment risk).
spec.exitRule: Exit/flatten a name when ANY: (1) trend flips (sign(r_12_1) reverses at a monthly rebalance), (2) the concordance turns discordant against the held side (long held but itfDir becomes 'bearish' OR ens < -20; short held but itfDir 'bullish' OR ens > 20), (3) name falls out of the top-N by |r_12_1|. Hard risk stop: flatten a name intra-month if its since-entry drawdown exceeds 3*sigma[i]*sqrt(21/252) (a ~3-sigma monthly move against the position). No profit target (let convex winners run — capping upside would kill the positive-skew payoff that is the entire orthogonality thesis). Re-evaluate the full book monthly; between rebalances only the hard stop and discordance flip can force an exit.
spec.instruments: Equity long/flat (default) across the ~80 high-liquidity optionable large-caps + broad/sector ETFs (SPY/QQQ/IWM/sector SPDRs) already in the universe, each held as shares (no options, no assignment risk on the long leg). Vol-targeted per name to equal risk. Optionally run dollar-neutral long/short to harvest the short-trend leg, accepting 50 bps/yr borrow (DEFAULT_COSTS.shortBorrowAnnualBps) and earnings-gap risk; long-only is preferred to capture the literature's long-side concentration and eliminate borrow/short-squeeze cost.
spec.positionSizing: Fixed capital C. Per-name volatility targeting to an annualized risk budget tau_name = 10% per position: raw weight w_raw[i] = tau_name / sigma[i]. Confidence tilt (static per-name leverage, bounded): k[i] = clamp((conf[i]*100 - 50)/8, 0, 1), so a name with dir_acc_30d<=50 gets ZERO confidence boost and a name at >=58 gets full tilt; sized weight w[i] = w_raw[i] * (0.5 + 0.5*k[i]) (floor at half-size so the gate-passing trend trade is always taken, the tilt only scales 0.5x->1.0x). Normalize so gross exposure G = sum|w[i]| is capped at 1.5x C (modest leverage; portfolio vol-target ~10% annualized — if realized portfolio sigma would exceed 10%, scale all w down pro-rata). Per-name hard cap 10% of C. Cash not deployed sits in T-bills (earns ^TNX/risk-free). Risk cap: total book 95%-VaR (from per-name sigma, assuming avg pairwise corr 0.3) must not exceed 12% of C at any rebalance; if it does, shrink G until satisfied.
spec.dataSources: BASE LEG (fully backtestable today): 10y daily OHLCV via yahoo-finance2 fetchOHLCV(symbol,10) for r_12_1; parkinson_vol_20d & garman_klass_vol_20d (itransformer-features.ts indices 84/85) for sigma. GATE LEG (live only): forecast_returns[] & model_confidence from runHFInference (hf-model.ts); ensembleScore from generatePrediction (prediction.ts); itfDirection via getDirection() (ConcordanceCard.tsx). Backtest/eval: computeMetrics() + curveCorrelation() (backtest/metrics.ts) for Sharpe/MaxDD/orthogonality; DEFAULT_COSTS (backtest/costs.ts) for frictions. Risk-free from ^TNX. NEW INFRA NEEDED: a daily NAV / EquityPoint{date,equity} schema for equity baskets (the existing simulated_trades + capital_events tables are options-position-centric and do not store a basket equity curve) plus a forward-log table that snapshots forecast_returns[59], itfDirection, ensembleScore, model_confidence per name at each rebalance (the iTransformer/ensemble are computed live and have NO historical persistence, so the gate's marginal uplift can only be evaluated forward, not reconstructed).
spec.capacityNote: Very high for retail. Universe is the most liquid US large-caps + ETFs; monthly turnover on <=20 vol-targeted names at <=1.5x gross on fixed retail capital ($10k-$10M) moves size far below any market-impact threshold — DEFAULT_COSTS slippage (2 bps/side) is conservative here and likely overstated for SPY/AAPL-class names. Long-only avoids all borrow/locate constraints. The binding constraint is NOT capacity but turnover drag: each gate flip or top-N reshuffle is a taxable round-trip, so realized net edge is sensitive to how often the veto toggles. Strategy degrades gracefully with size; it does not crowd a known retail trade because the ML veto layer is idiosyncratic to this engine.
spec.falsifier: Run two backtests 2015-2025 on the ~80-name universe and compare daily NAV via computeMetrics(): (A) base 12-1 TSM long/flat, vol-targeted, monthly, costed with DEFAULT_COSTS; (B) identical but with the ML concordance veto + confidence tilt applied to forward-logged forecasts. REFUTED if EITHER: (1) base book (A) net annualized Sharpe < 0.4 (or net CAGR below risk-free) over the full sample AND in >=2 of the 3 sub-periods 2015-18 / 2019-21 / 2022-25 — the documented premium failed to show up net of ~14 bps/trade on THIS universe; OR (2) the gate (B vs A) delivers delta-Sharpe < +0.15 AND delta-MaxDD reduction < 15% on >=200 forward-logged monthly name-decisions — i.e., the ML veto/tilt adds no measurable drawdown-filtering value and is pure cost. A SHARPER single-number kill: bucket every gated-vs-ungated name-month; if the mean forward 60d return of trend-positive names the model VETOED (itfDir=='bearish') is NOT statistically below (one-sided t, p<0.05) that of trend-positive names it PASSED, the veto carries zero information and H1 is rejected — the model is a coin-flip filter and the whole premise collapses to plain TSM.

redteam.lens: COSTS & CAPACITY
redteam.kill: The spec's headline 55 bps/trade edge belongs to the GATED book, and H1 explicitly sells the ML overlay as the value-add (+0.15 Sharpe / -15% MaxDD on top of base TSM). But the overlay's gross edge does NOT clear its incremental round-trip cost. Concretely, using the repo's own DEFAULT_COSTS (costs.ts: 3 bps spread + 2 bps slippage per side = 10 bps round-trip), the marginal cost of the gate is strictly positive: every veto toggle is an exit+re-entry (~10 bps) layered on top of base turnover, and the static confidence tilt (model_confidence = dir_acc_30d/100, a constant-through-time test-set scalar per hf-model.ts:415) only scales notional UP (0.5x->1.0x) adding turnover with zero dynamic information. The marginal EDGE of the gate is, by the spec's own admission, a 50-58% coinflip whose 'sharper single-number kill' falsifier concedes the veto may carry ZERO information (one-sided t-test). A near-zero-information binary gate that adds round-trips is pure cost: marginal_edge_gate ~= 0 bps < marginal_cost_gate > 0 bps. Separately, the cited ~0.4-0.7 Sharpe is documented on 58 liquid FUTURES (MOP 2012), driven largely by vol-scaling rather than the trend sign per follow-up research, NOT on an ~80-name single-stock equity universe with equity frictions; the transfer to this universe net of cost is unproven and is exactly what falsifier (1) tests. The spec also names turnover-toggle as 'the binding constraint' yet never quantifies it.
redteam.mitigation: Survives ONLY if reframed and re-evidenced as TWO separable claims with separate cost ledgers: (1) the BASE vol-targeted 12-1 TSM leg is genuinely cost-affordable on THIS equity universe (~300%/yr turnover x 10 bps round-trip ~= ~30 bps/yr drag vs ~600 bps/yr gross premium IF it shows up) and DEFAULT_COSTS is honest/conservative for SPY/AAPL-class names; report base-leg net Sharpe standalone. (2) For the GATE to clear its own marginal cost, the spec must show, on >=200 forward-logged name-months, that the vetoed-vs-passed forward-return gap (its own t-test) is both statistically significant AND large enough that the per-toggle ~10 bps round-trip is recovered with margin — i.e. edge-per-veto must exceed cost-per-veto, not just be 'nonzero.' Add hysteresis/deadbands on the gate and the vol-target rescale (dust filter widened well above 0.2% of equity) to suppress noise toggles that are pure cost. Replace whole-share assumption with explicit fractional-share support or raise the capital floor from $10k to a level where 20 vol-targeted names hold without rounding-driven re-trades. Cost the L/S short leg with name-specific borrow, not the flat 50 bps default, since down-trending names are the expensive-to-borrow set.
redteam.residual: Yes, partial. The BASE TSM leg plausibly retains real net edge AFTER this cost attack: the engine's cost model is conservative for the most-liquid US large-caps/ETFs, $0 equity commissions are real, and ~30 bps/yr of turnover drag is small versus a multi-hundred-bps gross premium IF the documented anomaly transfers to this universe (an open empirical question, not a cost question). Capacity at $1M-$10M is genuinely high and slippage is likely overstated there, as claimed. What does NOT survive is the spec's actual selling proposition: the ML concordance veto + static confidence tilt as a marginal value-add. Its incremental edge fails to clear its incremental round-trip cost, the static tilt adds cost with no dynamic information, the $10k capacity floor is unsupported under whole-share granularity, and the optional L/S leg understates borrow on exactly the names it shorts. Net residual edge is 'base TSM minus a cost-additive gate' = at best the plain-TSM edge the spec's falsifier (2) was built to expose, with the overlay subtracting rather than adding net of frictions.

redteam.lens: DATA-SNOOPING, DECAY & ORTHOGONALITY
redteam.kill: The spec smartly separates a documented base anomaly (12-1 TSM) from an ML "gate," but the EDGE THAT IS ACTUALLY NOVEL — the ML veto/tilt uplift — fails this lens on two counts. (1) DATA-SNOOPING / unfalsifiable: by the spec's own admission ("the iTransformer/ensemble... have NO historical persistence, so the gate's marginal uplift can only be evaluated forward, not reconstructed"), H1's gate clause (delta-Sharpe >= +0.15, delta-MaxDD >= 15%) CANNOT be tested today. It requires forward-logging >=200 name-months (~10+ months of live running) that have not occurred. There is zero in-sample OR out-of-sample evidence the gate adds value; CLAUDE.md lists the concordance-predicts-profitability claim (H3) as an UNTESTED hypothesis. So the proprietary edge is an unproven assertion. Worse, the only quantified ML input that IS persisted — model_confidence — is, per the spec and confirmed at hf-model.ts:415-418, a STATIC per-stock test-set dir_acc_30d scalar from a 437K-param model the project's own CLAUDE.md calls "175x over-parameterized." Driving a live per-name leverage tilt off a model's own (overfit, ~50-58% = near-coinflip) test-set accuracy is textbook soft data-snooping. (2) DECAY: the base leg's century of evidence (MOP/Hurst Sharpe ~1.1) is for DIVERSIFIED FUTURES across uncorrelated asset classes; the spec restricts to single US large-cap EQUITIES + equity-heavy ETFs — the literature's own weaker, higher-correlation case. And the 2015-2025 backtest window sits squarely inside trend-following's documented post-publication "lost decade": the SG Trend Index returned ~1.8%/yr 2010-2019 vs 7.5% for 60/40. The H1 base threshold (net Sharpe >= 0.4) is being demanded precisely where the published premium decayed, on the weaker asset class.
redteam.mitigation: For the gate to survive data-snooping, it must EARN its claim out-of-sample: actually run the forward log for >=12 months and clear the spec's own sharper single-number kill (mean fwd-60d return of VETOED trend-positive names statistically below PASSED ones, one-sided t, p<0.05) BEFORE any capital allocation — and freeze the gate threshold/horizon first (note: the live ConcordanceCard keys off forecast_returns[29]=30d at +/-0.5%, not the index-59/60d the spec wants, so the logged signal must be re-plumbed to match the traded horizon, or the test is contaminated). Drop the model_confidence tilt entirely, or replace the static test-set-accuracy scalar with a rolling LIVE-realized hit-rate computed only from forward-logged outcomes (never from training/test metrics). For decay/orthogonality: broaden to the diversified multi-asset futures/ETF sleeve the literature actually documents (TREND_ETFS already exists: TLT/IEF/GLD/DBC/EEM/EFA/VNQ), which restores the genuine positive-skew crisis-alpha orthogonality; report the realized daily-NAV correlation to the CSP book SEPARATELY in calm vs crisis sub-samples (a single full-sample <=0.2 can hide high bull-market beta correlation). And run the falsifier's base-leg test (A) — fully runnable today via the existing engine.ts/metrics.ts and ts_trend strategy — across all three sub-periods; if net Sharpe < 0.4 in >=2 of them, the equity-only base premium is confirmed decayed and the gate has nothing robust to gate.
redteam.residual: Modest and conditional. The base 12-1 TSM leg is a real, honestly-disclosed risk/behavioral premium and the falsifier for it is concretely runnable TODAY (engine.ts is a complete walk-forward weight backtest; metrics.ts already has EquityPoint/computeMetrics/curveCorrelation; ts_trend/xs_momentum already implement the signal — so the spec's "NEW INFRA NEEDED: EquityPoint schema" is partly redundant). If broadened to the diversified ETF sleeve, the structural orthogonality to short-vol put-selling is genuine (positive skew vs negative skew, different drawdown regimes — well-supported by Man Group/AQR crisis-alpha evidence), and that diversification value alone could justify a small sleeve EVEN with a coin-flip gate. But the SPECIFIC edge this spec is selling — that the iTransformer/ensemble ML gate meaningfully improves Sharpe/MaxDD over plain TSM — has NO evidence, is unfalsifiable until a forward log exists, and rests on an admittedly-overfit near-coinflip model plus a snooped static confidence scalar. Under default skepticism, the incremental ML edge does not survive; what survives is a vanilla, somewhat-decayed, equity-tilted trend sleeve whose only defensible value (diversification) requires de-equitizing the universe and discarding the ML overlay as unproven.

redteam.lens: REGIME & ROBUSTNESS (regime dependence, parameter sensitivity, crowding, tail risk; 2018-Q4 / Mar-2020 / 2022 / Aug-2024)
redteam.kill: The strategy's incremental value (H1: ML veto adds delta-Sharpe >= 0.15 and/or cuts MaxDD >= 15%) is regime-fragile and untested where it matters. (1) Monthly 12-1 TSM is structurally a turning-point loser: web evidence shows monthly trend/dual-momentum only flipped defensive in Jan-2019 after the S&P was already -14% (Newfound "Fragility Case Study"; Q4-2018 = textbook model-specification risk), and TSM/CTAs are documented to whipsaw on V-shaped reversals. Three of the four named events were exactly that — 2018-Q4 (down then sharp Jan-Apr-2019 V), Mar-2020 (fastest-ever V), and Aug-2024 (one-day VIX-to-65 carry unwind that fully reversed within the SAME WEEK, BIS Bull 90). A monthly-rebalanced book either does nothing intra-month or trips its 3-sigma hard stop at the low and sells into the recovery. (2) The gate cannot help in a crisis by construction: the spec concedes model_confidence is a STATIC per-stock test-set accuracy scalar constant through time (verified hf-model.ts:415-418), so the 'size on confidence' tilt is a fixed leverage constant carrying zero regime/tail information; and the directional veto is a 30-60d-ahead forecast refreshed monthly, which physically cannot react to a gap or a one-week VIX spike. The claimed CSP tail hedge is mistimed: the CSP book detonates intra-month in a gap while TSM-Gate's response is the next monthly rebalance — into the recovery. (3) The entire H1 uplift is unbacktestable by the spec's own admission ('live, not historically reconstructable; NEW INFRA NEEDED'), so a 50-58% classifier's drawdown-filtering value has ZERO out-of-sample evidence. (4) The deployed artifact doesn't even match the spec: live getDirection keys off forecast_returns[29]=30d, not index 59=60d (ConcordanceCard.tsx:41-45,55). 2022 (sustained grind down) is the only named episode it handles well — but long-only top-20-by-|momentum| sits mostly in T-bills as longs fail s>0, earning the risk-free rate rather than the short-trend profit unless the dis-preferred short leg is run.
redteam.mitigation: To survive this lens: (a) Backtest the base 12-1 TSM long/flat leg on 2015-2025 and show net Sharpe >= 0.4 in >=2 of 3 sub-periods INCLUDING explicit 2018-Q4, Mar-2020, Aug-2024 P&L attribution (these are intra-month, so the daily NAV and hard-stop behavior must be simulated daily, not monthly). (b) Forward-log the gate for >=200 name-months and run the spec's own sharp falsifier (one-sided t-test that vetoed trend-positive names underperform passed ones, p<0.05) BEFORE claiming any uplift; without this the gate is assumed-zero. (c) Replace the static dir_acc_30d confidence scalar with a genuinely time-varying, regime-aware sizing input, or drop the tilt and claim no confidence edge. (d) Add a faster reaction channel than monthly (e.g., weekly trend check or a VIX/vol-spike circuit breaker) so the book can avoid selling V-shaped lows via the 3-sigma stop. (e) Run a parameter-robustness grid over the lookback-skip (21d), getDirection bands (+/-0.5%), top-N, confidence divisor (8), and stop multiple (3-sigma) and show edge survives reasonable perturbations. (f) Fix the spec/code mismatch (index 59 vs 29). (g) Quantify, don't assert, the CSP correlation/hedge with realized daily NAVs across the named crises.
redteam.residual: Yes, a narrow residual edge survives, but it is the boring part, not the pitched part. The BASE vol-targeted 12-1 TSM equity leg plausibly clears net Sharpe >= 0.4 over 2015-2025 and genuinely shines in SUSTAINED bear regimes (2022: TSM ~-12% vs 60/40 -20%+, Man/AQR evidence), and its positive-skew/long-vol profile is directionally orthogonal to the short-vol CSP book. So as a standalone, fully crowded, century-documented base anomaly run long/flat, it can pay for ~14 bps/trade. What does NOT survive: the strategy's actual thesis — that the ML concordance veto + static confidence tilt adds drawdown-filtering / tail-hedging value (H1's >= 0.15 Sharpe, >= 15% MaxDD). That increment has no out-of-sample evidence, relies on a constant-through-time scalar that cannot fire in a crisis, operates on a 30-60d horizon that cannot react to the gap/V-shape events named, and would have whipsawed in 3 of the 4 stress periods. Net: keep the base TSM as a diversifier; treat the gate as unproven and the crisis-hedge claim as refuted until forward-logged and daily-simulated through the named episodes.

name: Factor-Momentum Style-ETF Rotation (time-series autocorrelation of factor returns, SPY-neutralized)
family: Factor investing via ETF proxies + cross-sectional factors (value/quality/low-vol/size/momentum tilts; long-short or long-tilt; orthogonal to the incumbent short-vol put-selling and to a single-stock momentum pick)
rationale: Real but published/decayed mechanism degraded to a 2-axis (growth/value + cyclical/defensive) bet on 4 ETFs; three Serious red-team lenses converge that net edge is breakeven-to-negative after 50bps SPY borrow + ~50-100% name turnover, and its orthogonality holds only on average — the tail correlation to short-vol is positive in exactly the panic/reversal tapes that matter.
bestUseInEnsemble: Keep ONLY as a tiny (sub-5%) VIX-gated diversifier AFTER four fixes are demonstrated per-episode: (1) widen the cross-section by actually wiring a broad factor set (note: IWF/IWD are NOT in the tradable universe.ts/ALL_BACKTEST_SYMBOLS — they only exist in the ML forecast feature list in route.ts, and MTUM/QUAL/USMV/VLUE are absent entirely, so this is real data-wiring work, not free); (2) replace the flat 5bps/side cost with a regime-conditional model widening style-ETF spreads to 8-15bps in high-VIX/rotation months; (3) extend the falsifier beyond full-sample curveCorrelation to a crash-day-conditioned tail co-movement test against the actual short-vol sleeve P&L; (4) add hysteresis (rotate a leg out only below rank 3) to cut turnover. Note the engine's default maxGross=1.0 silently halves the intended dollar-neutral G=1.0 book unless the caller passes maxGross>=2.0. As specced it is breakeven carry; its only ensemble value is a structurally theta/vega-free return stream IF the crash-day orthogonality is empirically proven first.

spec.name: Factor-Momentum Style-ETF Rotation (time-series autocorrelation of factor returns, SPY-neutralized)
spec.family: Factor investing via ETF proxies + cross-sectional/time-series factor momentum (value/growth/size/credit style tilts; dollar-neutral long-short vs SPY; orthogonal to incumbent short-vol put-selling and to a single-stock 12-1 momentum pick)
spec.mechanism: Risk-premium + behavioral persistence. Factor (style) returns are positively autocorrelated at 1-3 month horizons (Gupta-Kelly "Factor Momentum Everywhere", 2019; Ehsani-Linnainmaa attribute the persistence to sentiment-driven excess-demand, NOT a repackaging of single-stock 12-1 momentum). Capital chases recently-winning styles, so a style's relative outperformance vs the market persists ~1 month. Trading STYLE ETFs (not single names) and shorting SPY in equal dollar notional strips market beta, leaving the P&L as the spread of recently-winning styles over the market. This is structurally vega/theta-free, so it diversifies the book's return calendar against short-vol (which only earns in calm IV-mean-reverting tapes). Distinct from static long-only factor tilts, which have decayed (USMV ~10%/yr vs SPY ~15.5%/yr last decade) and are rejected as the primary edge here.
spec.hypothesisH0: On the ACTUAL PutStrike-available style-ETF set, the dollar-neutral "long top-ranked styles, short SPY" spread has a monthly Sharpe <= 0 net of the DEFAULT_COSTS model (3bps spread + 2bps slippage per side, 50bps/yr SPY borrow), AND/OR its realized daily-return correlation to the sibling single-stock 12-1 momentum pick is >= 0.4 (i.e. it is redundant, not an independent return stream).
spec.hypothesisH1: The spread earns a positive net monthly return with annualized Sharpe >= 0.4 over a 10y walk-forward AND has daily-return correlation < 0.4 to the momentum pick and < 0.3 to the short-vol sleeve, i.e. it is a genuinely orthogonal, cost-surviving sleeve. Optional VIX-percentile gross-down reduces left-tail (factor-momentum crashes in vol spikes) without killing the mean.
spec.signalDefinition: DATA-CONSTRAINED universe (verified in src/app/api/forecast/route.ts tickers[] and itransformer-features.ts — MTUM/QUAL/USMV/VLUE are NOT fetched anywhere in the repo; only these style/sector ETFs have 10y daily OHLCV wired): primary style legs = {IWF (growth), IWD (value), XLY (cyclical/risk-on), XLP (defensive)}; extendable to the wired sector set {XLK, XLF, XLV, XLE, XLI, XLC, XLB}. For each ETF E with adjusted-close series closes(E), use the existing helper momentumSkip(prices, lookback, skip) from src/lib/strategies/indicators.ts: blendMom(E) = 0.5*momentumSkip(closes(E), 21, 5) + 0.5*momentumSkip(closes(E), 252, 5). (skip=5 trading days removes the 1-week reversal; weights 1m/12m equally per Gupta-Kelly.) Rank ETFs cross-sectionally by blendMom descending. Emit signed TargetWeight[] (per src/lib/strategies/types.ts): the top-2 ETFs each get weight = +0.5*G, and SPY gets weight = -1.0*G, where G is gross-exposure scalar (dollar-neutral: longs +G, short -G). Default G=1.0 of allocated sleeve capital. VIX overlay (regime.vix from RegimeSnapshot): if vix > rolling 80th percentile of its own trailing 252d, set G=0.5. rebalanceDays=21, warmupBars=258, instrument:"etf", longOnly:false.
spec.entryRule: Monthly on EOD close (every rebalanceDays=21 trading days, signals computed at that day's close with date<=t only — the engine enforces no look-ahead). Build the target book: long the 2 highest-blendMom style ETFs at +0.5*G each, short SPY at -1.0*G. Only trade the delta vs current book (do not churn legs that stay in the top-2). Require each long ETF to have >= warmupBars history; if fewer than 2 ETFs qualify, stay flat (G=0) that period.
spec.exitRule: No discretionary exit; positions are fully refreshed at each 21-day rebalance (drift between rebalances is allowed, matching types.ts semantics). A leg is closed only when it drops out of the top-2 at the next rebalance, or when the VIX gate halves G. Hard kill: if the sleeve's trailing 12-month net return is < the SPY-short borrow drag (i.e. negative carry after costs), pause the sleeve and route to the falsifier review rather than continuing to trade.
spec.instruments: Long 2 style/sector ETFs from the WIRED set {IWF, IWD, XLY, XLP, [+ XLK/XLF/XLV/XLE/XLI/XLC/XLB]} + short SPY, dollar-neutral. All penny-wide, deeply liquid; no options, no single-name borrow (only SPY short, easy-to-borrow). NOTE: the candidate's named MTUM/QUAL/USMV/VLUE are unavailable in this engine and are dropped.
spec.positionSizing: Fixed sleeve capital C (e.g. 15-20% of book, kept small because edge is thin). Gross long notional = G*C split equally across the 2 winners (0.5*G*C each); short SPY notional = G*C. Net dollar exposure ~0 (market-neutral); gross = 2*G*C. Risk cap: hard-cap sleeve gross at 1.0x C (G<=1.0); VIX>80th-pct halves to G=0.5. Per-rebalance loss governor: if a single rebalance period draws the sleeve down > 4% of C, drop G to 0.5 for the next period. Borrow on the SPY short is charged daily at shortBorrowAnnualBps=50 (costs.ts dailyBorrowCost). Sized on equity via the same signed-weight engine that powers the walk-forward backtest and live runner, so sim == live.
spec.dataSources: PutStrike yahoo-finance2 10y daily adjusted-close OHLCV for IWF, IWD, XLY, XLP, SPY, and sector SPDRs (all in src/app/api/forecast/route.ts tickers[]); ^VIX for the gross-down gate (RegimeSnapshot.vix). Engine plumbing: src/lib/strategies/indicators.ts (momentumSkip, totalReturn), types.ts (Strategy/TargetWeight contract), src/lib/backtest/costs.ts (DEFAULT_COSTS) and metrics.ts (computeMetrics, curveCorrelation for the orthogonality test). NO new data feed or infra required for the constrained universe; the only NEW work is adding MTUM/QUAL/USMV/VLUE to tickers[] IF the richer factor set is wanted (a real, non-trivial data-wiring change, contra the candidate's 'NOTHING new required').
spec.capacityNote: Capacity is effectively unlimited at retail/small-fund size — IWF/IWD/XLY/XLP/SPY each trade tens of millions of shares/day, so a 15-20% sleeve of a retail book moves zero price. The binding constraint is NOT capacity but EDGE THINNESS and PROXY DEGRADATION: forcing the cross-section down to the 4 wired style ETFs (vs Gupta-Kelly's ~65-factor breadth) shrinks the diversification that produces the ~0.84 paper Sharpe, and post-2015 ETF-democratization crowding (arXiv 2512.11913) further decays it. Net ~16 bps/rebalance (~1.5-2%/yr on sleeve gross) is real but fragile — one bad style-rotation year erases a year of carry. Treat as a small diversifier, not a core sleeve.
spec.falsifier: TWO concrete data results refute deployment. (1) REDUNDANCY KILL (primary): build the sleeve's daily equity curve and the single-stock 12-1 momentum pick's curve over the 10y walk-forward; if curveCorrelation(sleeveCurve, momentumCurve) >= 0.4 (metrics.ts), the sleeve is a repackaged momentum bet and is rejected — this fires hardest in months when IWF/growth is the top-ranked style. (2) COST/EDGE KILL: if the walk-forward net Sharpe (computeMetrics, after DEFAULT_COSTS incl. 50bps SPY borrow) is <= 0, or net mean return per rebalance <= estCostBpsPerTrade (~14 bps) so net edge <= 0, reject. (3) DATA-AVAILABILITY FALSIFIER (already TRIPPED for the candidate as written): grep the repo for MTUM|QUAL|USMV|VLUE returns zero hits — they are not in tickers[] in src/app/api/forecast/route.ts nor in itransformer-features.ts, so the candidate's '6 factor ETFs FULLY AVAILABLE, NOTHING new required' claim is false; the spec must run on the 4 wired style ETFs (IWF/IWD/XLY/XLP) or someone must first wire the missing 4 tickers and refetch 10y history.

redteam.lens: COSTS & CAPACITY — realistic retail frictions (commissions, bid/ask spread, slippage, SPY borrow, rotation turnover, min granularity) and capacity vs gross edge
redteam.kill: The spec is internally inconsistent on the only number that matters, and once reconciled the gross edge does NOT clear round-trip cost. The header claims expectedEdgeBpsPerTrade=30 vs estCostBpsPerTrade=14 (a comfy 16 bps cushion), but the spec's own capacityNote walks the edge back to "~16 bps/rebalance, ~1.5-2%/yr on sleeve gross." On 12 monthly rebalances that honest body figure is only ~12-17 bps GROSS per rebalance, not 30 — the 30 is unsupported. Against that honest base the real costs are: (1) SPY borrow 50bps/yr on the 1.0*C short = ~4.2 bps EVERY rebalance via costs.ts dailyBorrowCost, charged whether or not anything rotates; (2) rotation trading cost — a top-2-of-4 monthly rank on a noisy blended 1m/12m signal flips ~one long leg most months (~50-100% name turnover), so ~1.0-1.5*C of notional is traded at DEFAULT_COSTS 5 bps/side = ~5-10 bps/rebalance. Net = 12-17 gross − 4.2 borrow − 5-10 rotation = roughly -2 to +8 bps/rebalance, i.e. at or below the author's OWN falsifier #2 kill threshold (net mean <= ~14 bps cost → reject). Worse, DEFAULT_COSTS' 3+2=5 bps/side is optimistic exactly where it matters: factor-momentum P&L concentrates in rotation/stress months when IWF/IWD/XLY/XLP spreads widen to 5-15 bps and market-on-close slippage exceeds 2 bps — so the cost is understated precisely in the regimes the edge must be monetized. Published evidence (Korajczyk-Sadka; CFA Institute "Transaction Costs of Factor Investing") confirms momentum-family strategies carry the highest turnover and costs can erase the gross premium; the factor-momentum survival results lean on ~65-factor breadth and netting of offsetting trades — neither exists in a 4-ETF + SPY proxy. Verified in-repo: costs.ts charges 5 bps/side + 50bps/yr borrow as claimed; the 4 style ETFs (IWF/IWD/XLY/XLP) + sector SPDRs + SPY are genuinely wired in forecast/route.ts and MTUM/QUAL/USMV/VLUE are absent, so the cross-section is forced down to 4 names — which both shrinks the edge AND raises per-name turnover.
redteam.mitigation: For the sleeve to survive this lens: (a) the walk-forward must demonstrate net mean return per rebalance materially ABOVE ~14 bps using realized (not 30 bps assumed) gross returns AND a regime-conditional cost model that widens style-ETF spreads to 8-15 bps in rotation/high-VIX months rather than a flat 5 bps/side; (b) measured monthly name turnover should be reported and ideally constrained (e.g. hysteresis band so a leg only rotates out when it falls below rank 3, not rank 2) to cut rotation cost from ~5-10 to ~2-4 bps; (c) widen the universe by actually wiring MTUM/QUAL/USMV/VLUE (the spec correctly flags this as real, non-trivial data work) to recover Gupta-Kelly breadth and let offsetting trades net down turnover; (d) consider replacing the perpetual SPY short (continuous 4.2 bps/rebalance borrow) with a less costly beta hedge, since borrow is a fixed drag that the thin edge cannot reliably outrun. Absent (a)-(d) with hard walk-forward numbers, treat as breakeven-to-negative carry.
redteam.residual: Capacity itself fully survives — IWF/IWD/XLY/XLP/SPY trade tens of millions of shares/day, a 15-20% retail sleeve moves zero price, share granularity at ~$7.5-10k/leg is a non-issue, and SPY is genuinely easy-to-borrow, so the 50 bps borrow is fair. The residual EDGE, however, is thin and unreliable: on the spec's own honest ~12-17 bps/rebalance gross figure, after ~4.2 bps continuous borrow and ~5-10 bps rotation cost the net is roughly -2 to +8 bps/rebalance — a coin-flip around breakeven that turns negative once style-ETF spreads widen in the very rotation/stress months the strategy depends on. There may be a small genuine diversification/orthogonality benefit (the author rightly stresses it as a non-vega/theta sleeve uncorrelated to the put-selling book), but as a STANDALONE cost-surviving return stream the edge does not robustly clear round-trip cost. Keep only as a tiny diversifier contingent on a regime-conditional-cost walk-forward proving net/rebalance > 14 bps; otherwise the cost lens kills it.

redteam.lens: Data-snooping, decay & orthogonality: (a) is the mechanism real or a decayed/arbitraged published anomaly, and (b) is the stream truly orthogonal to short-vol put-selling or short-vol/crash-beta in disguise?
redteam.kill: On (a): the mechanism is REAL but it is a published, post-publication-decayed anomaly, not fresh edge. Ehsani-Linnainmaa "Factor Momentum and the Momentum Factor" (JoF 2022, NBER w25551) and Gupta-Kelly are widely cited and arbitraged; web-verified decay evidence is damning: McLean-Pontiff ~50% alpha loss post-publication, Tandfonline (2022) "year of publication explains 30% of Sharpe decay," and arXiv 2512.11913 documents post-2015 crowding via factor-ETF volume growth (model predicted 0.30 alpha for 2016-2024, realized only 0.15 = a clean ~50% haircut that tracks factor-ETF AUM). Worse, the paper Sharpe (~0.84-0.96) is generated by a ~65-72-factor breadth; the spec is FORCED down to a 4-ETF cross-section that is really just TWO axes (growth/value via IWF/IWD, risk-on/off via XLY/XLP). "Top-2 of 4" repeatedly degenerates to long-growth+long-cyclical / short-SPY, i.e. a dispersion/beta-tilt bet, not a 65-factor momentum harvest. The universe was chosen because those tickers happened to be wired for an unrelated ML feature pipeline (forecast/route.ts), not because they implement the cited mechanism, so the implementation is a proxy-of-convenience curve-fit. On (b): the orthogonality-to-short-vol claim fails where it matters. Web-verified momentum-crash literature (Alpha Architect; Daniel-Moskowitz) shows style/factor momentum crashes precisely in panic states, high-VIX, and sharp reversals, the SAME tapes (Feb 2018, Mar 2020) in which short-vol put-selling blows up. Dollar-neutral-vs-SPY strips AVERAGE beta but not the shared short-left-tail/short-gamma crash exposure. The spec's own VIX>80th-pct gross-down gate is a self-admission the sleeve bleeds in vol spikes, which is the opposite of being orthogonal to a short-vol book. The "<0.3 corr to short-vol" is asserted, never demonstrated; the tail-correlation that a put-selling book actually cares about is positive, not zero.
redteam.mitigation: To survive (a): drop the 4-ETF degenerate cross-section and wire a genuinely broad factor set (the spec itself flags adding MTUM/QUAL/USMV/VLUE is real, non-trivial data work that is NOT yet done) so the strategy harvests breadth-diversification rather than a 2-axis beta tilt; and gate deployment on a strictly OUT-OF-SAMPLE walk-forward starting AFTER the 2019/2022 publication dates, demonstrating net Sharpe >= 0.4 survives post-publication, not an in-sample fit to the decayed full history. To survive (b): the falsifier must be extended beyond correlation-to-the-12-1-momentum-pick to an explicit TAIL/crash-regime co-movement test against the actual short-vol sleeve's daily P&L, conditioned on VIX-spike days, not just full-sample Pearson (which hides shared left-tail). Show the sleeve is flat-or-positive on the exact days the put-selling sleeve takes its worst losses; only then is it a true diversifier. The VIX gross-down would also need to demonstrably remove, not merely soften, the shared crash exposure.
redteam.residual: Thin and fragile, not zero. After this attack a small residual likely remains: short-horizon style-return autocorrelation is a robust, repeatedly-replicated stationary fact (Falck-Rej confirm significance survives when the last month is included, exactly the 1m leg the blendMom uses), and the costs are genuinely tiny (deeply liquid ETFs, only an easy-to-borrow SPY short), so the gross signal probably clears DEFAULT_COSTS in calm tapes. But the spec's own honest framing caps this at ~1.5-2%/yr on sleeve gross with "one bad style-rotation year erases a year of carry," and the realistic residual after both decay (~50% haircut) and the loss of breadth (4 ETFs vs 65) is a sub-0.4 net Sharpe whose returns are positively tail-correlated to the incumbent short-vol sleeve, exactly when diversification is needed. Defensible only as a tiny, VIX-gated diversifier whose orthogonality is empirically demonstrated on crash days first, not as a standalone edge.

redteam.lens: REGIME & ROBUSTNESS (regime dependence, parameter sensitivity, crowding, tail risk; survival through 2018-Q4 / Mar-2020 / 2022 / Aug-2024)
redteam.kill: The residual P&L after SPY-neutralization is a concentrated STYLE-SPREAD bet whose worst drawdowns coincide precisely with regime REVERSALS, and the spec defends against the wrong variable. Three compounding problems: (1) WRONG CRASH CONDITIONER. The spec's own cited literature (Gupta-Kelly; Ehsani-Linnainmaa, NBER w25551) locates factor-momentum's left tail in months when the factor AUTOCORRELATION index turns negative (regime breaks), not in high-VIX months. The proposed defense is a VIX-80th-percentile gross-down. VIX percentile is a lagging, weakly-related proxy for autocorrelation reversal, so it fails to gate the actual crash mechanism. (2) DEGENERATE CROSS-SECTION. Repo grep confirms MTUM/QUAL/USMV/VLUE are NOT wired (only IWF/IWD/XLY/XLP + sector SPDRs in route.ts tickers[]); top-2-of-4 collapses the 'portfolio' to TWO ETFs — essentially a growth-vs-value + cyclical-vs-defensive bet. Gupta-Kelly's 0.84 Sharpe comes from ~65-factor diversification; a 2-name book has none of it. (3) REGIME-BY-REGIME failure: 2022 is the clean kill — a grinding multi-reversal value-over-growth bear where VIX rarely exceeded its own trailing-252d 80th pct (so the gate barely fired), the 12m leg of the equal-weight 1m/12m blend stayed long IWF deep into a value regime, and the 21-day rebalance was too slow for the mid-year bear rallies; the SPY-neutral construction does NOT neutralize the style spread (that spread IS the P&L) and it reversed repeatedly. Mar-2020/Apr-2020 V-rebound is the classic 'market rebounds, lagging styles outperform leaders' momentum-reversal that hits on the recovery while VIX is still elevated-but-falling. 2018-Q4 and Aug-2024 are sharp gaps where a 252d-relative VIX gate is lagging and the 21d cadence lets the book drift through the gap. Parameter surface is large (blend 0.5/0.5, skip=5, lookback 21/252, top-2, VIX 80th pct, 4%/period governor) on a 4-name universe where one rank flip swaps the entire book — high overfit risk for a thin ~16bps-net/rebalance edge that the spec itself admits 'one bad style-rotation year erases.'
redteam.mitigation: To survive this lens the sleeve must: (a) replace the VIX gate with a conditioner tied to the actual crash mechanism — gross-down when the realized factor-autocorrelation / cross-sectional rank stability turns negative, or add explicit momentum-crash protection (vol-targeting per the option-implied-vol-scaling result, or a long-convexity overlay), not a lagging 252d VIX percentile; (b) materially widen the cross-section — actually wire MTUM/QUAL/USMV/VLUE and additional factor proxies and refetch 10y history (the spec concedes this is real, non-trivial data work) so the book is not a 2-name style bet; (c) shorten/condition the rebalance or weight the blend toward the 1m leg during high-dispersion reversal regimes so the 12m leg stops fighting a turned regime; (d) demonstrate, in the walk-forward, the per-episode P&L through 2018-Q4, Mar/Apr-2020, full-year 2022, and Aug-2024 with the costs+borrow applied — not just a 10y aggregate Sharpe — and show the per-rebalance 4%-drawdown governor and VIX gate actually clipped the left tail in those windows rather than firing late.
redteam.residual: Partial, and narrower than claimed. The dollar-neutral-vs-SPY construction does genuinely strip market beta and is structurally theta/vega-free, so the ORTHOGONALITY-to-short-vol claim (corr<0.3 to the put-selling sleeve) is plausible and is the one robust property — this is not a hidden long-beta/bull-only bet in the CAPM sense, and it would have been roughly market-neutral (not blown up) through the index drawdowns themselves. What does NOT survive is the EDGE: the surviving return stream is a concentrated style-spread whose drawdowns cluster in regime reversals (2022 above all, plus the 2020 recovery whipsaw), where the proposed protections are mis-conditioned and slow. So as a tiny diversifier sized at 15-20% with the loss governor it is unlikely to 'blow up' the book, but its standalone edge over a full regime cycle is fragile/possibly negative net of costs and would not clear the H1 bar (annualized Sharpe >= 0.4) once 2022-style years are included. Net: keep it only as a small, properly-hedged diversifier after the four fixes above are demonstrated per-episode; reject as specced.

name: Residual (idiosyncratic) cross-sectional momentum, long-only top-decile, vol-target sized
rationale: Honest, well-instrumented spec, but it residualizes the RANKING while holding 8 full-beta longs — so it is long-beta in disguise that co-moves with the incumbent short-vol book (orthogonality Fatal), in the single most-arbitraged mega-cap cohort where the residual-momentum premium is most decayed, and its headline tail defenses (25% per-name stop, fast de-gross, SPY<200d overlay) are fictional or lagging as-coded.
bestUseInEnsemble: Only as a BETA-NEUTRAL selection overlay on a widened (S&P 500) universe: keep the residual-momentum ranking to pick firm-specific winners, but pair it with an explicit SPY-beta short (or run long-short top-vs-bottom decile) so the held basket is market-neutral and genuinely orthogonal to the put-selling P&L; absent that hedge it contributes nothing the incumbent does not already own.

spec.name: Residual (idiosyncratic) cross-sectional momentum, long-only top-decile, vol-target sized
spec.mechanism: Behavioral under-reaction to FIRM-SPECIFIC news (slow information diffusion + disposition effect) is the persistent, economically-motivated component of momentum; the factor-beta component is a time-varying leveraged market/sector bet that drives momentum's negative skew and crashes (Blitz residual momentum; Grundy-Martin; Barroso-Santa-Clara). By stripping each stock's return of its market/sector/style factor exposure and ranking only on the RESIDUAL trailing 12-1m return, we isolate the under-reaction premium and remove the dynamic beta that causes drawdowns. Vol-target sizing on the basket's OWN trailing realized vol de-grosses in fragile regimes (crash risk is predictable from the strategy's own variance), which also makes it DE-leverage in exactly the VIX-spike windows where the incumbent short-vol/theta book is most exposed -> complementary, not correlated. Caveat to the steelman: the PutStrike universe is ~80 mega/large-cap optionable names — the single most efficient, most-arbitraged slice of the market where the residual-momentum premium is weakest and most decayed since publication. The honest expectation is a small positive long-leg premium, not the 0.48/mo long-short Sharpe from the broad-universe literature.
spec.hypothesisH0: In the PutStrike ~80-name large-cap universe over 2015-2025, a long-only equal-weight basket of the top-decile residual-12-1m-momentum stocks (vol-target sized), net of the engine's DEFAULT_COSTS (~10 bps round-trip), earns a daily-return mean <= 0 in excess of an equal-weight hold of the same universe, AND its equity-curve correlation to a short-ATM-put/theta sleeve is >= 0.3 (i.e. it is neither additive return nor orthogonal).
spec.hypothesisH1: The residual-momentum basket earns a positive net excess daily mean over the equal-weight universe (gross edge materially > cost drag) AND has |equity-curve correlation| < 0.3 to the short-vol sleeve, so it adds an orthogonal, cost-survivable return stream on fixed simulated capital.
spec.signalDefinition:
Per rebalance day t, for each symbol s in StrategyContext.universe with >= 280 valid daily closes:
1. Build daily LOG returns r_s = logReturns(closes(s)) (src/lib/strategies/indicators.ts) over the trailing 252 sessions; build aligned factor log-return matrix F from closes of the manifest ETFs: market=SPY, style-growth=IWF, style-value=IWD, plus the stock's mapped sector SPDR via SECTOR_ETF_MAP (XLK/XLF/XLV/XLE/XLI/XLY/XLP/XLC/XLB; for the breadth ETFs SMH/SOX-mapped names also include ^SOX). To stay numerically stable on 80 names, residualize SEQUENTIALLY using the existing beta() helper rather than a full 6-var OLS: take excess return x = r_s; for each factor f in [SPY, sectorETF(s), (IWF-IWD spread)] in order, compute b_f = beta(x, r_f) and update x <- x - b_f * r_f. The final x is the idiosyncratic residual series e_s (this 3-factor sequential orthogonalization captures market+sector+style, the dominant exposures; it is a deliberate, auditable simplification of the candidate's 6-factor OLS that uses only code already in indicators.ts).
2. resid_mom_s = sum of e_s over the window [t-252, t-21] (i.e. residual cumulative return, skipping the most recent 21 sessions for 1-month reversal) — mirrors momentumSkip(prices,252,21) but on residuals.
3. Cross-sectional standardize: z_s = (resid_mom_s - mean_universe) / stdev_universe over all eligible s on day t (this is the panel rank/z-score the manifest does NOT currently compute — it requires the panel-join step; rolling_beta_spy_60d feature 88 and rel_return_vs_spy_* features 83-85 are NOT substitutes, being single-factor / raw and per-stock).
4. Eligibility = top-decile by z_s. With ~70 equities (exclude the ETFs SPY/QQQ/IWM/DIA/SMH/XL*/GLD/EEM from the LONG side; they may still serve as factors), top decile ≈ 7 names; floor the held count at N=8 to keep diversification (i.e. LONG the highest-z 8 names).
Vol-target scalar (computed once on the basket, not per name): strat_vol = annualizedVol(equalWeightBasketDailyReturns, window=126) using a Garman-Klass/close-to-close blend consistent with garman_klass_vol_20d (feature 101) extended to 126d; gross = clamp(targetVol / strat_vol, 0.30, 1.00) with targetVol = 0.11 (11% annualized, Barroso range).
weight_s = gross * (1/8) for each of the 8 longs; the remaining (1 - gross) sits in cash. Bottom-decile (lowest z_s) names form an AVOID list only (a position-veto), never shorted.
spec.entryRule: Rebalance cadence = every 21 trading sessions (Strategy.rebalanceDays=21, warmupBars=280). On each rebalance day t, compute resid_mom and z_s for all eligible symbols, take the top-8 by z_s as the target long set, and emit TargetWeight[] of weight_s = gross/8 for those 8 (sign +). NO-TRADE BUFFER to cut turnover: a name currently held is RETAINED (its weight refreshed to gross/8) as long as its z_s rank is still within the top 20% (>= 80th pct) of the universe; it is only dropped when it falls below the 80th pct, at which point it is replaced by the highest-z non-held name. New entries only fill slots vacated by buffer-drops. The engine's existing 0.2%-of-equity dust filter (engine.ts) auto-suppresses sub-threshold rebalancing trades, further damping turnover. Entry is blocked for any name flagged near_earnings is NOT required (idiosyncratic momentum is the thesis), but a name on the bottom-decile avoid list is barred from entry even if a transient z spike qualifies it.
spec.exitRule: Exit a long when, at a rebalance, its z_s drops below the 80th percentile (buffer threshold) and a higher-z replacement exists -> trade to 0 and reallocate. Hard risk exit between rebalances: if a single name draws down more than 25% from its entry close (stop, checked daily on EOD closes via the engine mark-to-market loop), flatten that name at the next session close and leave the slot in cash until the next scheduled rebalance (do not immediately re-fill, to avoid whipsaw). Whole-sleeve de-gross: gross is recomputed every rebalance from trailing 126d basket vol, so a vol spike automatically cuts exposure toward 0.30; additionally, if RegimeSnapshot.regime == "CRISIS" (VIX>=35) on a rebalance day, hard-cap gross at 0.30 that period. No time-based exit otherwise — winners are held across rebalances via the buffer (momentum persistence).
spec.instruments: Long-only cash equities: 8 equal-weight names from the ~70 large-cap equities in SCREENER_SYMBOLS (mega-cap tech, financials, healthcare, staples, discretionary, energy, industrials, etc.), gross-scaled 0.30x-1.00x of equity by the 11% vol target. Sector SPDRs (XLK/XLF/XLV/XLE/XLI/XLY/XLP/XLC/XLB), SPY, IWF, IWD, ^SOX are consumed as FACTOR series for residualization but are NOT taken as long positions. Bottom-decile names are an avoid-list/veto overlay only — never shorted (dodges hard-to-borrow, short-squeeze, and the 50 bps/yr borrow the cost model would charge). Instrument class = "equity" in the Strategy interface.
spec.positionSizing: Fixed simulated capital C (Neon sim, e.g. $100k). Per rebalance: gross = clamp(0.11 / strat_vol_126d, 0.30, 1.00); each of 8 longs gets target weight = gross/8 of current equity (so per-name 3.75%-12.5% of equity; max single-name notional = 12.5% at full gross). Idle (1 - gross) held as cash (earns rfAnnual in the backtest). maxGross config = 1.0 (no leverage; the engine clamps sum|weight| to 1.0). Per-name 25% stop caps single-name loss contribution to ~3% of equity at full gross. No pyramiding. Turnover is bounded by the 21-day cadence + 80th-pct buffer (empirically should keep annual name-turnover well under 300%, i.e. < ~4 full round-trips/name/yr).
spec.dataSources: 100% from the existing manifest, no new vendor. Daily adjusted closes for ~70 equities + factor ETFs (SPY, IWF, IWD, sector SPDRs, ^SOX) via yahoo-finance2 chart() (already fetched in forecast/route.ts). Compute layer reuses src/lib/strategies/indicators.ts: logReturns, beta (for sequential factor residualization), momentumSkip/totalReturn (12-1 window), annualizedVol (vol target), zscore/mean/stdev (cross-sectional standardization), correlation. Backtest + live sizing via src/lib/backtest/engine.ts (walk-forward, no-look-ahead StrategyContext.universe panel, maxGross clamp, DEFAULT_COSTS, turnover tracking) and metrics.ts (sharpe, annualTurnoverPct, curveCorrelation for the orthogonality test). Persistence via Neon simulated_trades + capital_events (db.ts). TWO derived objects must be ADDED (both pure transforms of existing data, ~40 lines total): (a) the per-day cross-sectional z-score panel join across the universe; (b) the sequential 3-factor residual-return series per stock. Neither rolling_beta_spy_60d (feat 88) nor rel_return_vs_spy_5/20/60d (feats 83-85) supplies these — they are single-factor and per-stock, confirmed by reading itransformer-features.ts.
spec.capacityNote: Capacity is NOT the binding constraint at retail size; statistical edge is. All 8 longs are mega/large-cap names ($50B-$3T) where a $100k-$5M book moves zero ADV, so the engine's 10 bps round-trip (3 spread + 2 slippage per side) is realistic and not optimistic. Turnover is the real friction: 21-day cadence on 8 names with an 80th-pct retention buffer should yield roughly 100-250% annual name turnover (~1-2.5 full round-trips/name/yr), i.e. ~12-30 bps/yr total cost drag — comfortably below any plausible gross premium. The deeper concern is SAMPLE/EDGE capacity: a ~70-name large-cap universe gives only ~7 names per decile and is the most arbitraged momentum cohort, so the cross-section is thin and the residual premium is the weakest-here variant of an already-decayed anomaly. Scaling the universe (adding the cheaply-available ETF-constituent panel from the brief, e.g. S&P 500 members) would widen the cross-section and is the single highest-value robustness extension — but stays long-only large-cap to preserve cost-survivability.
