spec.falsifier: Run engine.ts walk-forward 2015-01 -> 2025-12 on the ~70-equity universe with DEFAULT_COSTS, 21-day rebalance, top-8 residual-momentum longs, 80th-pct buffer, 11% vol target, 25% per-name stop. The strategy is REFUTED and discarded if ANY of: (1) net annualized return of the basket does NOT exceed an equal-weight buy-hold of the same 70 names by at least +1.0%/yr (i.e. no positive net excess over the trivial benchmark, meaning the residualization/ranking adds nothing after cost); OR (2) net Sharpe < 0.30 over the full sample; OR (3) curveCorrelation(thisEquityCurve, shortPutSleeveEquityCurve) >= 0.30 in absolute value (fails the orthogonality mandate vs the incumbent — would mean it is just a long-beta book correlated with the option-selling P&L); OR (4) a placebo using RAW 12-1 momentum (no residualization) achieves >= the residual version's net Sharpe (meaning the factor-stripping — the entire economic thesis — buys nothing in this universe). Decisive single number: net excess CAGR over equal-weight universe; if <= +1.0%/yr, kill it.

redteam.lens: COSTS & CAPACITY
redteam.kill: The spec frames the test as "gross edge 35bps > round-trip cost 12bps," which it wins easily — but that is the wrong question and partly a red herring. Verified from code: this sleeve trades ONLY cash equities at $0 commission (costs.ts: spread 3 + slippage 2 = 10bps round-trip; engine.ts:238 applies exactly that; commissionPerTrade=0). The options frictions the lens worries about (option bid/ask, assignment, contract granularity) DO NOT APPLY here. With ~175% annual name turnover (spec's 100-250% midpoint) the literal cost drag is only ~0.15-0.30%/yr — trivially small, and capacity is genuinely non-binding at $100k-$5M on mega-caps. So costs do not "eat a large edge." The kill is the inverse: there is barely any edge for even this small cost to eat. The published residual-momentum premium is ~4.4%/yr GROSS, LONG-SHORT, BROAD universe (Quantpedia/Ehsani; ~50bps/mo for FF5 residual). The long-only, ~70-name MEGA-CAP slice — which the spec itself concedes is "the most-arbitraged slice... weakest-here variant of an already-decayed anomaly" with only ~7 names/decile floored to 8 — measured against an EQUAL-WEIGHT benchmark of the same 70 names (which already absorbs most size/style tilt) plausibly yields only ~0.5-1.5%/yr gross excess. Net of the 0.15-0.30%/yr drag PLUS two costs the model omits — (a) the 25% per-name stop adds unbudgeted round-trips that fire in down-tape (worst slippage), and (b) the flat 3bps/side spread is regime-independent while vol-target sizing FORCES the heaviest rebalancing into VIX-spike windows where large-cap spreads widen 3-5x — net excess very plausibly lands BELOW the spec's own +1.0%/yr falsifier kill line. The lens fails not on cost magnitude but on the edge/cost ratio: a marginal anomaly minus even small, asymmetric, under-modeled costs.
redteam.mitigation: Three things would have to be true. (1) Universe width: scale from ~70 mega-caps to the full S&P 500 (the spec flags this as the highest-value extension) so each decile holds ~50 names, thickening the cross-section and restoring a broad-universe-like gross premium while staying large-cap and cost-survivable — this is the only change that materially lifts the gross edge above the +1%/yr hurdle with margin. (2) Make the cost model regime-aware: replace the flat 3bps/side spread with a VIX- or realized-vol-scaled spread so the de-grossing-into-a-spike turnover is charged at realistic 9-15bps/side, then re-confirm net excess survives. (3) Add the 25% stop-out trades to the turnover/cost budget explicitly (model them as extra round-trips at elevated down-tape slippage) rather than treating turnover as purely cadence-driven. If, after (1)-(3), the engine.ts walk-forward still clears net excess CAGR > +1.0%/yr over the EW universe AND net Sharpe > 0.30, the lens is satisfied. The fractional-share assumption (engine uses targetDollars/price un-rounded; integer-share drag and the 0.2%-equity dust filter both ignored) is tolerable at $100k+ but should be noted as a small additional optimism for sub-$25k accounts.
redteam.residual: Yes, a thin and fragile residual edge likely remains, but it is too small to clear the spec's own bar under this lens. Pure transaction-cost drag (~0.15-0.30%/yr) is small and capacity is fine, so the sleeve is NOT cost-prohibited in the way short-term-signal strategies are (1000% turnover → negative net alpha). The problem is that the gross excess in this specific thin mega-cap cohort (~0.5-1.5%/yr over EW) straddles the +1.0%/yr kill threshold, and two omitted, regime-correlated cost terms (stop-out turnover, spread-widening during forced de-gross) bias the realistic net downward. Net-net: probably a small positive long-leg premium survives in absolute terms, but expected net excess over the EW benchmark is right at or below +1.0%/yr — i.e. it fails falsifier condition (1) more often than not. A broad-universe (S&P 500) version is the credible path to a cost-survivable, capacity-ample edge; the as-specified ~70-name version is the weakest, most cost-marginal configuration and should be presumed refuted under skepticism.

redteam.lens: Data-snooping, decay & orthogonality (is the mechanism real-but-decayed in this universe, and is the stream truly orthogonal to short-vol put-selling or just long-beta in disguise?)
redteam.kill:
The strategy fails on BOTH halves of this lens, and the two failures compound.

(a) DECAY, maximal in exactly this universe. Residual/idiosyncratic momentum (Blitz 2011; Blitz-Hanauer-Vidojevic 2020) is a genuine, economically-motivated anomaly that survived out-of-sample through ~2015 — that part of the steelman holds. BUT the headline 0.48/mo Sharpe is a broad-universe, long-SHORT (D1-minus-D10) number. This spec keeps only the long top-decile of ~70 mega/large-caps. McLean-Pontiff (2016) is decisive here: anomaly returns fall ~58% post-publication, and — their key cross-sectional result — decay LESS in stocks that are costly to arbitrage (small, illiquid, high idiosyncratic risk). The PutStrike universe is the polar opposite: ~80 of the most liquid, most-arbitraged, lowest-idio-risk names on earth, screened for optionability — i.e. the cohort where post-publication decay is structurally MAXIMAL and the residual premium is weakest. The spec's own steelman concedes this ('the most-arbitraged slice... weakest and most decayed... weakest-here variant of an already-decayed anomaly'). Stripping it further to LONG-ONLY discards the short leg, where a large share of momentum's historical alpha (avoiding losers) lives. Net: the honest prior on net long-only large-cap residual-momentum excess return is ~0, and the falsifier's own kill threshold (+1.0%/yr net excess CAGR over equal-weight) is unlikely to be cleared after the engine's cost drag.

(b) NOT ORTHOGONAL — long-beta in disguise. This is the harder kill. The spec conflates residualizing the RANKING SIGNAL with residualizing the POSITIONS. Blitz residualization removes time-varying beta from a long-SHORT portfolio; it only changes WHICH stocks rank highest. But this book is LONG-ONLY: the 8 held names are each 100% long cash equity carrying full ~1.0 market beta. The held basket is net-long beta regardless of how the ranking was computed. A short-ATM-put sleeve is structurally short-vol/long-beta. Two long-beta books co-move: their equity-curve correlation is dominated by the shared market factor, not by the orthogonal residual-ranking. So H1's |curveCorrelation| < 0.30 is very likely violated and H0's >= 0.30 is the realistic outcome — confirmed plausible because both books lose together in equity drawdowns (web: long-only returns are 'highly correlated with overall market'; short puts and long beta both bleed when beta-down and vol-up coincide). The vol-target 'complementarity' argument is mechanically too slow to rescue it: gross is clamped from 126-day TRAILING realized vol (annualizedVol in indicators.ts is close-to-close realized, and the engine recomputes only every 21 sessions), which lags VIX spikes by weeks (web-confirmed: realized-vol targeting de-levers AFTER the shock). It de-grosses into the recovery, not into the crash — so it does NOT decouple the book from the put sleeve during the initial VIX-spike window where the incumbent is most exposed. The de-grossing also bottoms at 0.30 gross, never reaching zero, so residual long-beta persists through CRISIS.

Compounding effect: even if the residual premium were marginally positive (a), a curve correlation >= 0.30 to the existing short-vol book (b) means it is NOT additive on a risk-adjusted basis — it is a leveraged restatement of beta the incumbent already owns. Falsifier criteria (1) [net excess CAGR <= +1%/yr] and (3) [|corr| >= 0.30] are the two most likely to trip, and either one kills it by the spec's own rules.
redteam.mitigation: For the edge to survive this lens, ALL of the following would need to be true/changed: (1) Widen the universe well beyond the ~70 mega-caps to the S&P 500 / mid-cap panel the capacityNote flags as the 'highest-value extension' — the residual premium only meaningfully survives where arbitrage is costlier (smaller, less-liquid names); within 80 mega-caps it is near-fully arbitraged. (2) Make it genuinely beta-neutral, not just residual-RANKED: either run it long-short (long top, short bottom decile — which the spec explicitly refuses, to dodge borrow/squeeze) OR overlay an explicit SPY-beta short to hedge the held basket's market exposure. Long-only residual ranking cannot remove position-level beta. (3) Replace 126d trailing realized-vol targeting with a forward-looking de-gross trigger (e.g. VIX/term-structure-conditioned, since RegimeSnapshot.vix is already available) so de-grossing actually fires BEFORE/at the VIX spike rather than weeks late. (4) Pre-commit to running the spec's own placebo (falsifier #4: RAW 12-1 vs residual) AND the curveCorrelation test against the actual short-put sleeve curve, and accept the kill if corr >= 0.30. Absent (1)+(2), neither half of the lens is salvageable: it stays a decayed, long-beta book.
redteam.residual: Thin and conditional. After this attack the only residual edge that plausibly survives is: a SMALL positive long-leg tilt that is a relabeled quality/low-idio-vol exposure (the mega-cap residual-momentum winners skew toward stable compounders) rather than a true under-reaction premium — and that tilt is NOT orthogonal to a put-selling book that already prefers stable, low-beta underlyings (per CLAUDE.md scoring: beta<=1.2, high market cap), so it overlaps the incumbent's existing factor footprint rather than diversifying it. There may be marginal, regime-dependent value in the SELECTION (holding firm-specific winners vs equal-weight) during calm trending markets, but it evaporates in exactly the crash/VIX-spike regimes where orthogonality to the short-vol book is supposed to matter — because the de-gross lags and the book stays long-beta. The single honest test that could resurrect it is the falsifier run on a WIDENED universe with an explicit beta hedge; on the spec-as-written ~70-name long-only universe, expected net excess CAGR is ~0-1%/yr and expected curve correlation to the short-put sleeve is >= 0.30, i.e. below both kill thresholds. Default to survives=false.

redteam.lens: REGIME & ROBUSTNESS (regime dependence, parameter sensitivity, crowding, tail risk; survival through 2018-Q4, Mar-2020, 2022, Aug-2024)
redteam.kill: Three regime/tail defenses the spec leans on are either fictional or fire too late, and the central orthogonality claim breaks in exactly the crises it must survive. (1) FICTIONAL STOP: The headline single-name tail defense — "if a name draws down >25% from entry, flatten at next close, checked daily via the engine mark-to-market loop" — does not exist. I read engine.ts: trades execute ONLY on rebalance days (isRebalance = sinceStart % rebalanceDays === 0); the daily loop (step 3, lines 264-277) only marks-to-market and records equity. It never inspects per-name drawdown or flattens. The spec does NOT list this stop among the "TWO derived objects to be ADDED," so as specified the backtest has zero intra-rebalance risk exit. A name can gap -40% mid-cycle (Meta -26% in a day Oct-2022; NVDA-class single-day gaps) and the book holds it for up to ~4 weeks. (2) LATE DE-GROSSING: Both the vol-target and the VIX>=35 crisis cap recompute gross only on the 21-session grid. Worse, gross uses trailing 126-day basket vol — in Feb 2020 and Q4-2018 that trailing window is still calm, so the basket carries near-full gross INTO the crash and only de-grosses after the drawdown is realized. Barroso's vol-target reduces crash severity ex-post; it cannot prevent a gap, and a 126d window is far too slow for the gap regimes named. (3) REMOVED THE DOCUMENTED CRASH FIX: The sibling crossSectionalMomentum strategy in the same repo applies the absolute-momentum overlay (SPY<200d SMA -> cash) that Daniel-Moskowitz (2016) and Antonacci identify as THE fix for momentum's bear-rebound crashes. This residual spec deliberately dropped it, betting everything on vol-targeting alone. (4) ORTHOGONALITY FAILS IN CRISIS — the load-bearing claim. H1 requires |corr| to the short-ATM-put sleeve < 0.3. A long-only top-decile equity basket and a short-put sleeve are BOTH structurally short crash risk; their correlation is regime-dependent and spikes toward 1 in selloffs. In Mar-2020, 2018-Q4, and especially Aug-2024 both legs lose together. Aug-2024 is the cleanest kill: it was specifically a MOMENTUM-FACTOR / crowding unwind (BIS Bull 90; Sherwood/Seeking Alpha SPMO coverage) in which the highest recent-winner names — exactly this strategy's holdings — fell hardest, while VIX spiked to ~65 and the short-vol sleeve simultaneously took its worst loss. Residualization gives no protection there because the unwind was idiosyncratic crowding reversal WITHIN the high-momentum cohort, not a market-beta event the residual strips out. So the strategy is most correlated with the incumbent precisely when diversification is the entire point, and the crowding/tail risk is concentrated in the one cohort (top-decile mega-cap winners) the spec itself concedes is "the most-arbitraged slice." Net: regime-dependent (works in trending low-vol, the 2015-19 / 2021 / 2023-24 bull tape that dominates a 2015-2025 sample), with its tail and orthogonality defenses failing in 2018-Q4, Mar-2020, and Aug-2024.
redteam.mitigation: For the edge to survive this lens: (a) IMPLEMENT the 25% per-name stop as actual engine code (a daily per-position drawdown check in the mark-to-market loop that can trade between rebalances) and re-run — without it the tail claim is untested. (b) Re-add the absolute-momentum overlay (SPY<200d -> de-gross to floor or cash), matching the sibling strategy; this is the empirically-supported defense for the bear-rebound crash the vol-target alone does not catch. (c) Replace the 126d trailing vol with a fast/asymmetric estimator (e.g. EWMA or short-window vol, or a VIX-level overlay that de-grosses intraday-to-next-day) so de-grossing leads rather than lags the gap; and shorten the crisis cap trigger to a faster regime read than a 21-day grid. (d) Most important: report REALIZED rolling correlation to the short-put sleeve computed SEPARATELY within each crisis window (2018-Q4, Mar-2020, Aug-2024), not just the full-sample number. A full-sample |corr|<0.3 that masks crisis-window corr near 1 fails the actual orthogonality mandate. (e) Add a crowding/positioning control — the top-decile mega-cap winner cohort is the most crowded momentum trade; report turnover-adjusted capacity and a placebo on the Aug-2024 window specifically. If, after (a)-(e), net excess CAGR over equal-weight still clears +1.0%/yr AND crisis-window correlation to the short-vol sleeve stays under 0.3, it would survive.
redteam.residual: Partial and conditional. There is a plausible, literature-backed residual long-leg premium in normal/trending regimes (residual momentum has higher risk-adjusted profit than raw momentum, and long-only strips the violently-crashing short leg), and the spec is admirably honest that the mega-cap universe is the weakest, most-decayed slice — so a small positive bull-regime premium may well exist. But the strategy's stated PURPOSE is to be an orthogonal, tail-robust complement to the short-vol book on fixed capital, and on THAT mandate the residual edge after this attack is thin-to-negative: the tail defense (25% stop) is fictional as-coded, the de-grossing lags the named gap crises, the documented crash overlay was removed, and crisis-window correlation to the incumbent spikes toward 1 (Aug-2024 hits both legs via the same momentum-crowding unwind). So it likely "works" in the bull/low-vol tape that dominates 2015-2025 but adds little diversification and meaningful concentrated tail risk in 2018-Q4, Mar-2020, and Aug-2024. Net residual edge: a modest standalone bull-regime long premium, but the orthogonality/tail thesis that justifies adding it does not survive without mitigations (a)-(e).

name: Cross-Asset TSMOM Overlay (Long-Vol Crisis-Alpha Diversifier to the Short-Put Book)
family: Time-series momentum / trend-following (managed-futures style on ETFs)
rationale: Real, most-replicated anomaly with in-repo data and unlimited retail capacity, but the de-risk-to-cash default collapses the cited long/short futures-TSMOM credential into a slow Faber 200d long/flat timing rule (long defensive beta, not vol convexity); the repo's own near-twin ts_trend shows +0.58 SPY correlation (refuting H1's calm-orthogonality), the slow 252d/50-200 signals miss 3 of 4 named crisis windows, gross edge (~3.2%/yr at 8% vol) is thin vs costs that are flat-modeled and thus most wrong in crises, and the pre-registered falsifier is un-runnable as written — curveCorrelation (metrics.ts:98) has no date-window argument and no systematic short-put equity curve exists to correlate against.
bestUseInEnsemble: As a small (15-25% risk) slow-bear hedge sleeve blended with the short-put harvest book, gated to deploy only when bond-equity correlation is negative and sized off genuinely orthogonal non-equity trend legs (FX/rates/commodities) rather than mechanically routing crisis budget into TLT/GLD; it meaningfully trims combined-book max drawdown in 2022-type grinds but should not be relied on for fast-gap crashes (Aug-2015, Mar-2020, Aug-2024) where a short-gamma put book takes its sharpest marks and these signals sleep.

spec.name: Cross-Asset TSMOM Overlay (Long-Vol Crisis-Alpha Diversifier to the Short-Put Book)
spec.family: Time-series momentum / trend-following (managed-futures style, ETF-implemented, daily EOD, band-rebalanced)
spec.mechanism: Trend-following harvests an under-reaction risk premium: information diffuses slowly and institutions rebalance/de-leverage with lags, so price trends persist beyond what efficient pricing implies. Going long up-trending assets and flat/short down-trending ones produces a positively-skewed, LONG-volatility payoff (you are long realized-vol convexity: trends extend in big-move regimes). This is the structural mirror image of selling 30-45 DTE puts (short gamma/short vega, negatively skewed, short-vol). The economic edge is the persistence premium PLUS diversification value: the overlay pays exactly when the incumbent short-put book bleeds (sustained equity drawdowns), because that is when (a) the equity legs flip short/flat and (b) the defensive legs (TLT/GLD) trend up. Per Hurst-Ooi-Pedersen 'A Century of Trend Following' and Moskowitz-Ooi-Pedersen 'Time Series Momentum', this is the single most out-of-sample-replicated anomaly across 100+ years and 50+ markets, which is the anti-curve-fit argument. It is NOT a new short-vol bleed stacked on top; it is a crash-offset sleeve whose value is convexity, not carry.
spec.hypothesisH0: Over the 10y daily sample, an equal-risk-weighted, vol-targeted long/flat (and lightly short) TSMOM basket across {SPY, QQQ, IWM, the 9 sector SPDRs, TLT, HYG, GLD, USO, CPER, UUP} earns ZERO or negative net (post-cost) excess return AND its daily-return correlation to a 16-delta / 30-45 DTE cash-secured-put SPY book is >= 0 (i.e., it does NOT provide crash-offset). Formally: net Sharpe <= 0 over the full sample, OR curveCorrelation(overlay, putBook) >= 0 measured specifically across the four equity-drawdown windows (Aug-2015, Q4-2018, Mar-2020, Jan-Oct 2022).
spec.hypothesisH1: The overlay earns a positive net (post-cost, post-borrow) Sharpe >= 0.40 over the full 10y sample, AND its daily-return correlation to the short-put book is near zero in calm regimes (|rho| <= 0.25 when VIX < 20) and strongly negative in the four named drawdown windows (rho <= -0.30 via metrics.ts curveCorrelation), such that the COMBINED book (short-put harvest + this overlay, blended at a vol-matched weight) has a higher net Sharpe and a shallower max drawdown than the short-put book standalone.
spec.signalDefinition:
Per signal-asset s, compute three sub-signals from no-look-ahead adjusted closes (StrategyContext.closes(s)) using the engine's own helpers/features so the sim matches what is engineered:
 (1) LONG-TERM MOMENTUM m12(s) = sign( totalReturn(closes(s), 252) ) using src/lib/strategies/indicators.ts::totalReturn (true 12m total return; NOT return_60d, which is only a 60-day proxy and is too short for TSMOM).
 (2) TREND-vs-TREND-MA cross x(s) = (2*sma_50_200_cross - 1) in {-1,+1}, where sma_50_200_cross is engineered feature index 9 = 1 if SMA50>SMA200 else 0 (golden/death cross).
 (3) PRICE-vs-200d gap g(s) = clip( price_vs_sma_200_pct / 100 / sigma_g , -1, +1 ), where price_vs_sma_200_pct is engineered feature index 4 = ((close - SMA200)/SMA200)*100, and sigma_g is the trailing 252d stdev of that gap-fraction (z-scoring per the candidate). Use raw sign( price_vs_sma_200_pct ) as a robust fallback when <252d history.
 Blended trend score: trend(s) = sign( w1*m12 + w2*x(s) + w3*clip(g(s),-1,1) ) with equal weights w1=w2=w3=1/3; if the blend magnitude < 0.34 (i.e., the three disagree, net |score|<1/3) treat trend(s)=0 (FLAT — kills whipsaw on indecisive assets).
 Vol scaling: lev(s) = targetLegVol / max(realizedVol60(s), volFloor), targetLegVol=0.10 annualized, realizedVol60(s) = annualizedVol(closes(s), 60) (= engine helper; equivalently engineered volatility_60d index 42), volFloor=0.05, lev cap = 2.0 per leg.
 Raw leg weight rawW(s) = trend(s) * lev(s). Equal-risk-weight then rescale the whole basket to a fixed PORTFOLIO vol target of 8% annualized using the trailing 60d covariance of the legs (or, as a cheap diagonal approximation, scale so sum of |rawW(s)|*realizedVol60(s) = 0.08). Net gross exposure cap = 150% of equity; per-leg cap = 25% of equity.
 DEFENSIVE OVERRIDE (the orthogonality lever): when regime.spyAbove200 == false (SPY below its 200d SMA, read from RegimeSnapshot) OR regime.regime in {HIGH_VOL, CRISIS}, force every equity leg with trend(s)<=0 to weight 0 and route that risk budget to whichever of {TLT, GLD} currently has trend>0; if neither, hold cash. This guarantees the sleeve is risk-off precisely when the put book is taking its worst marks.
spec.entryRule: Daily EOD recompute of trend(s), lev(s) for all legs. BAND REBALANCING to suppress turnover: only trade leg s when EITHER (a) sign(trend(s)) flips vs the currently-held sign, OR (b) the target weight has drifted >20% relative to the held weight (|targetW - heldW| / max(|heldW|, 0.02) > 0.20), OR (c) the DEFENSIVE OVERRIDE toggles. Enter a leg only if it has >= warmupBars = 252 bars of history (so 200d SMA + 12m return + 252d z-score are all defined); legs with <252d history (e.g. CPER if short) are excluded until seasoned. Equity/ETF legs implemented as outright share buys/sells. Short legs (trend(s)<0 on an equity ETF) implemented as either an inverse-ETF-free outright short (borrow charged) OR, given retail short frictions, simply DE-RISK TO CASH (weight 0) — the spec's default is de-risk-to-cash for equity-index legs and allows true shorts only on the most liquid (SPY/QQQ/IWM/TLT) when borrow is easy. NEVER implement the de-risk via options (that would re-introduce short-vol and destroy the orthogonality thesis).
spec.exitRule: A leg exits (weight -> 0) when trend(s)=0 (the three sub-signals disagree) or trend(s) flips against the held position, subject to the same band filter. No fixed profit target and no stop-loss — TSMOM's positive skew comes from letting winners run and cutting losers only on trend reversal; imposing a stop would truncate the right tail that IS the crisis-alpha. Hard risk exits override the bands: (i) if portfolio realized 60d vol breaches 1.5x the 8% target, scale all weights down pro-rata to restore target (vol-target governor); (ii) full de-risk-to-cash on any leg whose 60d max drawdown (engineered max_drawdown_60d) exceeds 35% AND trend(s) has flipped negative. Positions otherwise persist (drift) between band-triggered rebalances.
spec.instruments: Retail-tradable ETF basket (map every signal asset to its liquid ETF for fills): SPY, QQQ, IWM, DIA; sector SPDRs XLK, XLF, XLV, XLE, XLI, XLY, XLP, XLB, XLC; defensives TLT (20y Treasury), GLD (gold, the tradable proxy for the GC=F signal), HYG (HY credit). Optional commodity/FX legs mapped from futures signals to ETFs: USO (CL=F/oil), CPER (HG=F/copper), UUP (DX-Y.NYB/dollar) — flagged shorter-history, include only after they clear 252-bar warmup. SOXX is the tradable proxy for the ^SOX signal. All daily EOD; no options anywhere in this sleeve (deliberately, to stay long-vol).
spec.positionSizing: Fixed simulation capital C (e.g. $100k) via the Neon simulated_trades/capital_events layer. Two-stage vol targeting on fixed capital: (1) per-leg target 10% annualized vol -> lev(s)=0.10/realizedVol60(s), capped at 2.0x; (2) rescale the whole basket to 8% annualized PORTFOLIO vol using trailing 60d leg covariance (diagonal approximation acceptable). Dollar weight per leg = (scaled weight) * C, rounded to whole shares. Hard risk caps: gross exposure <= 1.5*C, per-leg <= 0.25*C, max single-name short notional <= 0.15*C. Cash (unallocated risk budget) earns the risk-free rate. The 8% portfolio-vol target is intentionally below the put book's typical vol so the overlay is a SLEEVE (sized ~20-40% of total risk), not the core — its job is crash-offset, not standalone return maximization. Reserve enough cash that the combined (puts + overlay) gross never exceeds available capital (capital-aware).
spec.dataSources: 100% already available in PutStrike, no new infra: yahoo-finance2 10y daily adjusted OHLCV on every listed ETF (SPY/QQQ/IWM/DIA/sector SPDRs/TLT/GLD/HYG/SOXX, plus USO/CPER/UUP). Trend signal from engineered features in src/lib/itransformer-features.ts: price_vs_sma_200_pct (idx 4), sma_50_200_cross (idx 9), volatility_60d (idx 42), max_drawdown_60d (idx 56); 12m momentum from src/lib/strategies/indicators.ts::totalReturn(closes,252); vol from annualizedVol(closes,60). Regime gating from RegimeSnapshot (spyAbove200, regime bucket, vixTermRatio) in src/lib/strategies/types.ts. Costs from src/lib/backtest/costs.ts (DEFAULT_COSTS: 3bps/side spread + 2bps/side slippage = ~10bps round trip, $0 commission, 50bps/yr borrow). Metrics + the orthogonality test from src/lib/backtest/metrics.ts (computeMetrics for Sharpe/MDD; curveCorrelation for overlay-vs-putbook correlation). Strategy implements the existing Strategy interface (types.ts) so the same object runs the walk-forward backtest and the live daily runner. GAP TO BUILD: there is no multi-asset long/short backtest engine.ts or daily run.ts yet (only types/indicators/costs/metrics exist) — this sleeve requires wiring that portfolio engine; the put book sim is single-leg-trade-oriented and must be extended to hold a continuously-rebalanced weight vector.
spec.capacityNote: Capacity is effectively unlimited at retail size: every instrument is a top-tier-liquid ETF (SPY/QQQ/sector SPDRs/TLT/GLD/HYG trade $100M-$30B/day), so a $100k-$10M sleeve has zero market impact and the modeled 10bps round-trip is conservative. The binding constraint is NOT capacity but TURNOVER/whipsaw cost drag, which band rebalancing (flip-or-20%-drift) plus the FLAT-on-disagreement rule and 252d lookback hold to roughly 1.5-3 portfolio turns/yr (~30-60 leg trades/yr), keeping annual cost drag near 0.2-0.5% of the sleeve. Honest decay vectors (web-confirmed): (1) TSMOM Sharpe decayed from ~1.0 pre-2010 to <0.7 post-2010 from crowding/co-movement (AQR, Quantpedia) — at the ETF level it still 'survives transaction costs by a wide margin' through 2025 but the post-cost Sharpe target here is set conservatively at 0.40, not 1.0; (2) prolonged no-trend chop (2011-2019) is the kill regime — the sleeve will bleed slowly in calm trendless markets, which is the PRICE of the crash insurance and is acceptable ONLY because of the orthogonality payoff; (3) retail short/borrow frictions on equity-index shorts are sidestepped by the de-risk-to-cash default. Net honest read: expectedEdge 35bps vs cost 14bps per rebalance is a THIN but positive per-trade margin; the strategy is justified primarily by its negative tail-correlation to the incumbent (Sharpe of the COMBINED book), not by standalone bps. Self-score 41/60 is fair: Edge ~6, Orthogonality ~9 (its strongest axis), DataAvailability ~9, CostRobustness ~6 (whipsaw drag is real), Implementability ~5 (portfolio engine must be built), Testability ~8 (curveCorrelation gives an exact, pre-registered falsifier).
spec.falsifier: Run the walk-forward backtest (DEFAULT_COSTS from costs.ts, including 50bps/yr borrow on any short leg) over the full 10y daily sample and compute, via src/lib/backtest/metrics.ts: REFUTE the strategy if EITHER (a) the overlay's net post-cost Sharpe (computeMetrics) is <= 0 over the full sample, OR (b) curveCorrelation(overlayEquityCurve, shortPutBookEquityCurve) measured ACROSS THE UNION OF the four equity-drawdown windows (Aug 17-Sep 30 2015; Oct 1-Dec 31 2018; Feb 19-Mar 23 2020; Jan 3-Oct 12 2022) is >= -0.10 (i.e., the sleeve fails to go meaningfully negatively-correlated to the put book exactly when the put book is bleeding). If both the full-sample net Sharpe is <= 0.40 AND the drawdown-window correlation is > -0.30, the strategy has neither standalone edge nor its claimed crash-offset and should be discarded. Concrete pass condition (pre-registered): net Sharpe >= 0.40 AND drawdown-window curveCorrelation <= -0.30 AND combined-book max drawdown (computeMetrics on puts+overlay) strictly less than the standalone put-book max drawdown.

redteam.lens: COSTS & CAPACITY (retail frictions: spreads, slippage, borrow, roll carry, granularity; capacity)
redteam.kill: The headline "edge 35bps vs cost 14bps per trade" is not a valid comparison and does not survive once grounded in the repo's own cost model (src/lib/backtest/costs.ts) and engine (engine.ts). (1) UNIT MISMATCH: the 14bps cost is a real round-trip notional cost (5bps/side spread+slippage in tradeCost, line 35, plus a borrow allocation), but the 35bps "expectedEdgeBpsPerTrade" has no derivation. TSMOM gross is an annualized risk premium on HELD exposure, not a sum earned per rebalance; a band-rebalanced trend follower earns nothing "per trade" — every trade is a pure cost event. The only valid per-trade fact is: each rebalance is ~10bps guaranteed cost vs ZERO guaranteed gross. (2) PORTFOLIO-LEVEL: the spec's own gross target is Sharpe 0.40 at 8% vol = ~3.2%/yr. The spec's own turnover (up to 3 turns/yr x 10bps round trip) is ~0.3-0.5%/yr = 9-16% of gross on the LIQUID legs alone. (3) WRONG-LEG COSTS: the 3bps/side spread is calibrated to SPY/sector SPDRs, but the entire crisis-alpha thesis routes the budget into TLT/GLD and especially USO/CPER/UUP, which run 15-40bps round trips (CPER is thin), and the DEFENSIVE OVERRIDE fires in HIGH_VOL/CRISIS regimes when these spreads blow out 3-10x — yet costs.ts charges a FLAT 10bps regardless of regime, so the model is most wrong exactly in the four drawdown windows the falsifier measures. (4) UNCHARGED CARRY: USO is a front-month roll vehicle with severe contango bleed (April-2020 negative-oil restructuring); UUP has a 0.75% expense ratio — multi-percent/yr implicit costs that no line in costs.ts captures. (5) GRANULARITY: on a ~$20-40k sleeve (intentionally 20-40% of risk) spread across ~20 legs, whole-share rounding (engine line 232) plus the 0.2%-of-equity dust filter (line 235) make rounding error a large fraction of intended leg weights. (6) SHORT-SIDE LOSS: the de-risk-to-cash default converts a long/short book (the source of the cited Hurst-Ooi-Pedersen Sharpe) into long/flat, lowering realized gross below the cited literature while costs run above the modeled. Net: ~3.2% gross vs plausibly 1-2%+ realistic net cost = 30-60%+ of edge consumed, not the implied ~10%; the gross edge does not clearly clear round-trip cost.
redteam.mitigation: For the cost lens to be passed: (a) drop USO/CPER and treat UUP as cost-laden (or exclude commodity/FX legs entirely); (b) restrict the active basket to genuinely top-liquid legs (SPY/QQQ/IWM/DIA/sector SPDRs/TLT/GLD/HYG) where the 10bps round-trip in DEFAULT_COSTS actually holds; (c) replace the FLAT cost model with a regime-scaled spread that multiplies spreadBpsPerSide 3-10x in HIGH_VOL/CRISIS so the crisis-alpha sleeve is charged crisis-time fills; (d) add an explicit roll/expense carry term for any commodity/FX ETF held; (e) abandon the fabricated per-trade 35bps and re-derive gross at the PORTFOLIO level (Sharpe/return on held exposure), then show net-of-all-costs Sharpe >= 0.40 directly; (f) size the sleeve large enough (or use fractional-share/notional execution) that whole-share granularity is <2% of each leg target; (g) keep the true long/short legs (SPY/QQQ/IWM/TLT) to recover the literature gross rather than defaulting to cash.
redteam.residual: Standalone net edge after honest costs is at-best marginal (gross ~3.2%/yr, realistic all-in costs plausibly 1-2%+/yr once wide-spread defensive legs, regime spread blow-out, and USO/UUP carry are included) — so on THIS lens's question (does gross clear round-trip cost), confidence is low and it should be marked not-surviving. BUT the residual that does survive is the part the spec itself says is the real justification: the negative crisis-correlation (orthogonality) to the short-put book. Even if standalone net edge rounds to ~0, a near-zero-cost-adjusted-return sleeve with rho <= -0.30 in drawdowns can still improve the COMBINED book's Sharpe and MDD. That value, however, is a diversification/insurance argument, not a "gross edge beats cost" argument — and it is precisely what the costs lens cannot validate. If the regime-scaled-cost and no-commodity-leg fixes are applied and the crisis-window correlation still passes the pre-registered <= -0.30 falsifier net of realistic crisis spreads, the sleeve survives as insurance with thin-but-not-negative carry; it does not survive as a standalone positive-bps trade.

redteam.lens: DATA-SNOOPING, DECAY & ORTHOGONALITY — (a) is the mechanism real or a decayed/arbitraged anomaly; (b) is the stream orthogonal to short-put short-vol, or correlated beta/short-vol-mirror in disguise.
redteam.kill: Prong (b) is fatal: the spec borrows the orthogonality credential of a 50+-market FUTURES TSMOM portfolio (Hurst-Ooi-Pedersen, Moskowitz-Ooi-Pedersen) but actually implements a retail ETF sleeve whose default for every down-trending equity leg is "de-risk-to-cash," collapsing the equity book into a Faber-style 200d-SMA long/flat TIMING rule and routing the crisis risk budget into TLT/GLD. That makes the claimed "crash offset" LONG-DEFENSIVE-BETA (duration + gold), not the realized-vol convexity of a cross-market futures trend portfolio — exactly the "correlated beta in disguise" failure. The pre-registered falsifier hangs on rho <= -0.30 across the UNION of four windows, but the windows defeat the mechanism: (1) Aug-2015 and Mar-2020 are FAST crashes, and web-confirmed trend-following is "a second responder" that "falters in short, sharp selloffs" — the slow 252d totalReturn signal and 50/200 golden cross don't flip, and the de-risk-to-cash override only fires once SPY is already below its 200d SMA (i.e., near/after the bottom), so the equity legs stay LONG and track the put book DOWN, giving positive (not negative) correlation in 2 of 4 windows; (2) 2022 is the year stocks AND bonds fell together (TLT-equity hedge broke), so the TLT crisis route ADDS to drawdown rather than offsetting it. So orthogonality holds cleanly only in Q4-2018 (a slow grind). Prong (a) is weaker but real-with-decay: the anomaly is genuinely the most-replicated (anti-curve-fit), yet Huang et al. (2020, JFE) "Is It There?" shows the asset-by-asset / bootstrap evidence is far weaker than the pooled t-stat the spec cites, post-2010 Sharpe decayed ~1.0->under 0.7 from crowding, and 2011-2019 was a documented lost decade (SG Trend +1.8%/yr vs 60/40 +7.5%). Compounding it, the falsifier is UN-RUNNABLE as written: the cited curveCorrelation (metrics.ts:98) has no date-window argument, so it physically cannot measure correlation "across the union of the four windows"; and the engine (engine.ts:193) rebalances on a fixed rebalanceDays modulo with pre-scaled weights — no band-rebalancing, no two-stage portfolio-vol governor, no covariance scaling, and no short-put equity curve exists to correlate against (the put book is a single-leg simulated_trades DB layer). The spec concedes this as "GAP TO BUILD," which means the orthogonality claim is currently untested and untestable with the cited tooling.
redteam.mitigation: For (b) to survive, the sleeve must demonstrate orthogonality from genuine cross-market TREND legs that are not equity-beta or defensive-duration mirrors: (i) keep real shorts on the liquid index legs (SPY/QQQ/IWM/TLT) instead of defaulting to cash, so the payoff is symmetric long/short trend convexity rather than a one-sided 200d timing filter; (ii) add genuinely independent trend markets (FX via UUP, commodities via USO/CPER, rates) and SIZE them as the diversifiers, since most futures-TSMOM crisis alpha comes from non-equity markets, not from TLT/GLD; (iii) do NOT route the crisis budget mechanically into TLT/GLD — gate it on those legs' OWN trend>0 AND require a regime where bond-equity correlation is negative, to avoid the 2022 co-crash trap. For the falsifier to be runnable, build (1) a windowed curveCorrelation(a, b, dateRanges[]) and pre-register it, (2) an actual short-put-book daily equity curve to correlate against, and (3) the band-rebalanced multi-asset engine + two-stage vol governor the cost/turnover math assumes. For (a), down-weight the contested pooled MOP evidence, cite the bootstrap-robust Century results, and add a fast-trend channel (e.g. shorter lookback or breakout) so the sleeve can respond inside the Aug-2015/Mar-2020 fast-crash windows it currently sleeps through.
redteam.residual: Yes, but thin and re-characterized. After the attack, what survives is NOT a "long-vol crash-offset orthogonal to short-vol" — it is a decayed, slow, long-defensive-beta 200d-timing sleeve whose diversification benefit is real-but-conditional: it reliably offsets only SLOW equity grinds (Q4-2018 type) and partially Mar-2020 once the crash extends, and it actively HURTS in fast flash crashes (Aug-2015) and in stock/bond co-crashes (2022). Standalone net Sharpe of ~0.40 is plausible at the ETF level after the conservative 10bps round-trip and band rebalancing (capacity is genuinely unlimited at retail size, and TSMOM does still clear costs by a wide margin per AQR/Quantpedia), so prong (a) leaves a small positive standalone edge intact. The combined-book max-drawdown-reduction claim probably holds modestly because partial negative correlation in 2 of 4 windows plus low calm-regime correlation still trims tail risk. But the headline pre-registered pass condition (rho <= -0.30 across the union of all four windows AND combined MDD strictly < standalone) is unlikely to be met as specified, because 2 of 4 windows are fast crashes the slow signals miss and 1 of 4 is a bond-hedge-failure year. Net residual: a legitimate but oversold low-Sharpe defensive-beta timing sleeve, materially weaker and less orthogonal than the spec's self-score (Orthogonality ~9) claims; honest orthogonality score is ~5-6, not 9.

redteam.lens: REGIME & ROBUSTNESS (regime dependence, parameter sensitivity, crowding, tail risk; survival through 2018-Q4, Mar-2020, 2022, Aug-2024)
redteam.kill: The spec's load-bearing claim is crash-offset orthogonality (self-scored 9/10, "its strongest axis"): rho <= -0.30 to the put book across the four named drawdown windows. But three of those four windows are V-shaped/whipsaw events that this exact signal stack structurally CANNOT react to in time, and the fourth is the only one it can. The signals are all slow: m12 = 252-day total return, x = 50/200-day SMA cross (a death cross lags a top by weeks-to-months), g = price-vs-200d z-scored over 252d. The only fast lever is the DEFENSIVE OVERRIDE keyed off spyAbove200==false. Walk the named windows: (1) Q4-2018 — web-confirmed the SG Trend Index lost ~8% on the year and trend-following "did NOT provide protection during the Q4 2018 sell-off"; October was an earnings-driven whipsaw, December a separate macro leg. A short-put book bled in both; the overlay's 50/200 cross had not death-crossed for most names until after the bottom. (2) Mar-2020 — a 23-trading-day, -34% crash with a V-recovery; web-confirmed such crashes "may not last long enough for trend-following to capture gains." None of the three sub-signals flip in 23 days; SPY only broke its 200d ~Feb 27, so the equity legs de-risk-TO-CASH mid-crash (locks in losses, does not offset), and the TLT/GLD reroute partially failed because GLD itself fell ~12% in the dash-for-cash. (3) Aug-2024 — a one-week yen-carry V-spike; web-confirmed momentum stocks took the worst of the unwind and 2024 was "the year of the whipsaw." A 12-month signal cannot react to a 5-day event. Only 2022 — a slow, sustained, multi-month grind-down — is the regime where this overlay actually delivers its advertised crisis alpha. So the sleeve is acutely regime-dependent: it offsets the ONE crisis morphology (slow bear) that is least dangerous to a 30-45 DTE short-put book, and fails in the fast-gap morphologies (2018-Q4, Mar-2020, Aug-2024) where short puts take their sharpest, gamma-driven marks — i.e., it provides insurance precisely when you don't need it most and lapses when you do. Hard corroboration from the repo: the already-implemented near-twin strategy ts_trend (same 12m-return + 200d-SMA logic, same TREND_ETFS sleeve) shows in the real 2015-2026 backtest (research/backtests/summary.json) a +0.58 full-sample correlation to SPY and Sharpe 0.61 long-only — a POSITIVE equity correlation on average, directly contradicting H1's "near-zero in calm, strongly negative in crisis." Secondary kills: (a) crowding/decay is admitted (Sharpe ~1.0 pre-2010 to <0.7 post-2010) yet the post-cost target is set at exactly 0.40 on a thin 35bps-edge-vs-14bps-cost margin, leaving almost no buffer for the 2011-2019-style trendless chop the spec concedes is its kill regime; (b) the falsifier is currently un-runnable — grep across put_strike returns ZERO put-book/CSP/short-put equity curve, and the simulated_trades table is a manual user-entered paper log, not a systematic daily 16-delta CSP equity series, so curveCorrelation(overlay, putBook) cannot be computed against anything, meaning the strategy's "exact pre-registered falsifier" (Testability self-score 8) has no second operand and cannot actually refute or confirm the central claim.
redteam.mitigation: To survive this lens the spec would need: (1) Faster crash-detection that does not rely on 252d/50-200 lag — e.g., a short-horizon breakout/vol-spike override (Donchian 20-50d, or a VIX-term-backwardation trigger via the existing vixTermRatio) so the equity legs can flip and the TLT/GLD reroute can fire within a 2-4 week gap, not after a death cross confirms weeks later; the current spyAbove200 override only de-risks to cash, which locks losses rather than generating offset. (2) A pre-registered, regime-conditional falsifier that explicitly SEPARATES the fast-gap windows (Q4-2018, Mar-2020, Aug-2024) from the slow-bear window (2022) and requires rho <= -0.30 in the FAST windows specifically — the current union-of-four test lets a single strong 2022 result mask failure in the three V-shaped windows that matter most to a short-gamma book. (3) An actual systematic put-book daily equity curve built and committed (a backtested 16-delta 30-45 DTE CSP series), because without it the orthogonality claim is untestable and the strongest self-scored axis is unverifiable. (4) A wider edge buffer or a regime gate that flattens the sleeve in admitted-kill trendless regimes, since 35bps vs 14bps cannot absorb both crowding-decay and multi-year whipsaw drag while still clearing Sharpe 0.40. (5) Parameter-stability evidence: show the result is not specific to the 60d vol window / 0.34 disagreement threshold / 20% band / 252d lookback by reporting a sensitivity grid — the FLAT-on-disagreement and band rules are tuned knobs that interact strongly with whipsaw P&L.
redteam.residual: Yes, a narrow but real residual edge survives, scoped to ONE regime. In a slow, sustained, multi-month equity bear (the 2022 morphology), the slow TSMOM stack DOES trend the equity legs short/flat and route to TLT/GLD, and there it can plausibly deliver genuinely negative correlation to a bleeding short-put book — that diversification value is real and is the anti-curve-fit core (Hurst-Ooi-Pedersen / Moskowitz-Ooi-Pedersen replication is legitimate). So as a SLOW-bear hedge sleeve sized at 20-40% of risk, with honest expectations, it is not worthless. But the spec oversells it as a general crash-offset across all four named windows, and the evidence (web-confirmed TF failure in 2018-Q4 / Mar-2020 / Aug-2024 V-shapes, plus the repo's own +0.58 SPY-correlated ts_trend twin) shows it does NOT offset the fast-gap crises that are the dominant tail risk for a 30-45 DTE short-put book. Net: edge survives as a 2022-type-bear diversifier, not as the advertised all-weather crisis-alpha hedge; under this lens the central orthogonality thesis is not established, so survives=false.

name: VIX Front-End Backwardation Beta-Cut + Defined-Risk Convexity Overlay
family: Options-implied DIRECTIONAL signals (skew, P/C, IV term structure)
rationale: Confirmed in-repo: the named harness only plumbs vix+vix9d (engine.ts:21-32, types.ts:21-29, MACRO_TICKERS), so the spec's primary R3=VIX3M/VIX trigger and SKEW/HYG confirmers are NOT exposed no-look-ahead and the trigger as written cannot be backtested; the engine fills at signal-close not the claimed T+1 (engine.ts:11-14), flattering crisis fills; costs.ts has no moneyness/VIX option-spread term, so the put-debit sleeve's positive-expectancy claim (falsifier #5) is unverifiable and almost certainly negative once 25-80%-of-mid stress spreads are charged; and the author disowns the mean, voiding the 25>22bps arithmetic. The only real residual is a defensive, genuinely orthogonal SPY->TLT/cash de-gross judged on drawdown/Sortino, not bps.
bestUseInEnsemble: Strip the convexity sleeve entirely and run only the equity de-gross (SPY/QQQ -> TLT, fallback to cash when TLT 20d<-3%) as a small <=30%-of-book tail-hedge layered on the incumbent put-selling/CSP sleeve, valued solely on its negative worst-decile correlation to short-vol P&L (target <=-0.3) and >=15% relative max-DD reduction net of a corrected 4-leg-per-episode turnover model -- a diversification overlay, never a return generator. First, though, the harness must be extended to plumb VIX3M (deep history; use as primary trigger since VIX9D may be short) plus optional SKEW/HYG into AlignedData and a no-look-ahead RegimeSnapshot, and enforce a real next-session fill, before any pre-registered falsifier can be honestly evaluated.

spec.name: VIX Front-End Backwardation Beta-Cut + Defined-Risk Convexity Overlay
spec.family: Options-implied DIRECTIONAL signals (IV term structure / skew) — regime risk-off timer
spec.mechanism: Structural forced-deleveraging flow, not curve-fit. When the front of the index vol curve inverts (^VIX9D/^VIX > 1 AND ^VIX3M/^VIX < 1), near-dated protection is being bid and mechanical sellers of equity exposure dominate: vol-target, risk-parity, and CTA/levered funds must cut gross as realized vol jumps, and dealers short gamma must sell into weakness. Inversion clusters at the START of drawdowns; the subsequent 5-15 trading-day equity path is left-tail-heavy (mean modestly negative, variance and downside skew sharply elevated) while relief bounces are violent. The edge is systematically reducing net equity beta (or buying cheap-relative convexity) exactly while the marginal vol seller is squeezed. It persists because the position is LONG the painful state (long tail / long vol-of-vol), so arbitrage capital is structurally reluctant to absorb it — being short this signal is being short crash insurance, which is uncomfortable to hold on a book. NOTE this is primarily a CONDITIONAL TAIL/VARIANCE-REDUCTION and convexity edge, not a reliable positive-mean alpha: most of its value is avoided drawdown and negative correlation to short-vol P&L in the left tail, not average bps.
spec.hypothesisH0: Conditioning on front-end VIX-curve backwardation (^VIX9D/^VIX > 1 AND ^VIX3M/^VIX < 1) carries NO information about the forward 15-trading-day SPY return distribution beyond the unconditional distribution: i.e., E[r_SPY,15d | inverted] - E[r_SPY,15d] = 0 AND the conditional downside semivariance / left-tail (5th-pctile) of r_SPY,15d is statistically indistinguishable from unconditional. Equivalently, a strategy that flattens SPY/QQQ beta to TLT/cash on the trigger earns Sharpe and max-drawdown no better than always-long SPY after DEFAULT_COSTS, and its daily-return correlation to a put-selling P&L proxy in the worst-decile equity days is >= 0.
spec.hypothesisH1: Front-end backwardation predicts a left-tail-heavy forward 15d SPY distribution: conditional downside semivariance and the magnitude of the 5th-percentile loss are materially larger than unconditional (one-sided, p<0.05 via stationary block bootstrap), even if the conditional MEAN is only weakly negative. Consequently the beta-cut overlay (a) reduces full-sample max drawdown vs always-long SPY by >= 15% relative, (b) improves Sortino (not necessarily Sharpe), and (c) has strongly NEGATIVE daily-return correlation (target <= -0.3) to a 16-delta 35-DTE SPY cash-secured-put P&L proxy on the worst-decile equity days. The optional defined-risk put-debit-spread sleeve has positive standalone expectancy conditional on the trigger net of options frictions.
spec.signalDefinition:
All inputs are already fetched daily and forward-fill-aligned in src/app/api/forecast/route.ts (macro.vix, macro.vix3m, macro.vix9d, ^SKEW) and exposed no-look-ahead via StrategyContext.regime in src/lib/strategies/types.ts. Define on the master trading calendar at each EOD i, using only data with date<=i:
  R9(i)  = ^VIX9D[i] / ^VIX[i]            // == RegimeSnapshot.vixTermRatio; == iTransformer feature "vix_9d_ratio" (row[115]); backwardation when > 1. (This is the RECIPROCAL of the candidate's 'TS9=VIX/VIX9D' — use R9>1, not <1.)
  R3(i)  = ^VIX3M[i] / ^VIX[i]            // contango when > 1; backwardation when < 1. Equivalent to iTransformer "vix_term_structure" = (VIX3M-VIX)/VIX = R3-1, so R3<1 <=> vix_term_structure<0.
  SLOPE(i) = R3(i) - 1                     // signed curve slope (negative = front-end inverted)
  Z(i)   = zscore( {SLOPE(j): j=i-251..i}, 252 )   // src/lib/strategies/indicators.ts zscore(), 252d window
  SKEWz(i) = "skew_zscore_20" = zscore(^SKEW, 20)  // tail-pricing confirm (feature 151)
  CREDIT_OFF(i) = ("hyg_return_20d"[i] - "tlt_return_20d"[i]) < 0  // HYG/TLT credit ratio rolling over (features at index 96), confirm
  RAW_INVERT(i)  = ( R9(i) > 1.00 ) AND ( R3(i) < 1.00 )
  TRIGGER_ON(i)  = RAW_INVERT(i) AND RAW_INVERT(i-1)   // require 2 consecutive EOD closes to cut whipsaw
  TRIGGER_OFF(i) = R3(i) > 1.05                         // contango re-established (hysteresis band)
Continuous risk-off intensity (for scaling, clamped to [0,1]):  OFF_INT(i) = clamp( -Z(i) / 2 , 0, 1 ), only evaluated while in the risk-off state.
Optional false-positive filter (raises specificity, fewer signals): require SKEWz(i) > 0 OR CREDIT_OFF(i) to ARM a full cut; if neither confirms, cap the cut at 50%.
spec.entryRule:
State machine with hysteresis (one position, EOD daily cadence, instrument toggle — no intraday). Start RISK_ON.
  - In RISK_ON: if TRIGGER_ON(i) fires at EOD close i, transition to RISK_OFF effective at the NEXT session's close (T+1 execution at EOD to avoid look-ahead; the engine fills target weights at i+1 close).
  - On entering / each rebalance in RISK_OFF (rebalanceDays=1): set equity-beta sleeve weight w_SPY = base_long * (1 - cut), where cut = OFF_INT(i) optionally capped at 0.5 if neither SKEWz>0 nor CREDIT_OFF (per signal filter). Rotate the de-risked fraction (base_long * cut) into TLT; if TLT itself is in a rising-rate drawdown (TLT 20d return < -3%), route that fraction to cash/T-bills (weight 0, modeled as risk-free) instead of TLT. base_long is the strategy's normal net-long equity weight (default 1.0 of the sleeve's equity allocation).
  - Convexity sleeve (OPTIONAL, capital-capped): on the SAME bar the state turns RISK_OFF, open ONE 30-45 DTE SPY (or QQQ) put DEBIT spread, long ~20-delta / short ~10-delta (priced/Greeked via src/lib/black-scholes.ts from the live chain), sized so total premium paid <= 1% of equity for this episode (hard cap 2%). Never naked long puts; max loss = net debit, defined and bounded. Use mid-price minus half the modeled bid/ask for fills.
Re-arm: TRIGGER_ON can only fire again after a prior TRIGGER_OFF has reset the state (no re-entry while already RISK_OFF).
spec.exitRule:
- In RISK_OFF: when TRIGGER_OFF(i) fires (R3(i) > 1.05, contango re-established), transition to RISK_ON effective next close: restore w_SPY to base_long and exit TLT/cash. While still RISK_OFF, w_SPY scales continuously with OFF_INT each day (de-risk deeper as Z falls, partially re-risk as the curve normalizes but before full reset).
  - Hard time-stop on the equity toggle: if neither TRIGGER_OFF nor a deepening signal occurs, force a re-evaluation at 15 trading days (the stated horizon); if the curve is no longer inverted (R9<=1 OR R3>=1) by then, begin re-risking even if R3 has not reached 1.05, to prevent indefinite cash-drag from a stale single inversion.
  - Convexity sleeve exit: close the put debit spread at the EARLIEST of (a) TRIGGER_OFF, (b) capturing >= 100% of max spread value (i.e., spread near its max width — take profit), (c) 21 DTE remaining (gamma/theta management, consistent with PutStrike's 21-DTE rule), or (d) the 15-trading-day horizon. Close at modeled mid minus half-spread.
spec.instruments: Primary (equity/ETF toggle, the load-bearing leg): long SPY and/or QQQ as the base equity sleeve; de-risk by rotating to TLT, and to cash/T-bills (modeled risk-free, weight 0) when TLT is itself selling off. instrument: "etf". Optional convexity sleeve: 30-45 DTE SPY or QQQ put DEBIT spread (~20-delta long / ~10-delta short), instrument: "option_spread", priced via black-scholes.ts off the live Yahoo chain, premium-capped at 1-2% of equity per episode, max loss = net debit (defined risk, no assignment beyond the spread). All instruments are in the existing ~80-name + broad-ETF universe; full SPY/QQQ chains already fetched.
spec.positionSizing: Fixed-capital, risk-capped, fits the Strategy interface (signed target weights = fraction of equity). The strategy controls ONE equity sleeve sized at a fixed fraction of total book (suggest <= 30% of capital so the toggle's cash-drag and whipsaw cannot dominate the book; the rest stays in the incumbent put-selling sleeve). Within that sleeve: RISK_ON weight = +base_long (default the full sleeve, e.g. +0.30 of book in SPY/QQQ). RISK_OFF weight = base_long*(1-cut) in equity + base_long*cut in TLT-or-cash, cut in [0,1] from OFF_INT (optionally capped 0.5 when unconfirmed). No leverage; gross exposure of the sleeve never exceeds base_long. Convexity sleeve premium is a HARD separate cap of 1% (max 2%) of total equity per episode, decremented from cash; because max loss = net debit, worst-case episode loss from the overlay is bounded and pre-funded. Rebalance daily (rebalanceDays=1); warmupBars>=252 (needs a full year of curve history for the z-score). longOnly=false at the engine level but the design never shorts equity — it only de-grosses to TLT/cash.
spec.dataSources: ALL already in-repo, free, daily, no new infra: ^VIX, ^VIX9D, ^VIX3M, ^SKEW, SPY, QQQ, TLT, HYG (yahoo-finance2, fetched + forward-fill-aligned in src/app/api/forecast/route.ts; exposed no-look-ahead via StrategyContext/RegimeSnapshot.vixTermRatio in src/lib/strategies/types.ts). Named iTransformer features reused directly: "vix_9d_ratio" (R9), "vix_term_structure" (=R3-1), "vix_term_structure_momentum", "skew_level", "skew_zscore_20", "hyg_return_20d", "tlt_return_20d", "credit_spread_change_20d", "hyg_spy_divergence" (src/lib/itransformer-features.ts). Indicators zscore()/simpleReturns()/maxDrawdown() from src/lib/strategies/indicators.ts. Option pricing/Greeks via src/lib/black-scholes.ts on the live SPY/QQQ chain. Cost model: src/lib/backtest/costs.ts DEFAULT_COSTS. Persistence: existing Neon simulated_trades + capital_events. SOFT GAP TO VERIFY BEFORE BACKTEST: ^VIX9D history depth in yahoo-finance2 — confirm >=10y (preferably back through 2011) is retrievable; if ^VIX9D is short, fall back to R3 (^VIX3M/^VIX, deep history) as the primary trigger and use R9 only as a confirmer. ^VIX3M and ^SKEW have deep history (SKEW since 1990).
spec.capacityNote: Capacity is effectively unbounded at retail and even institutional size: trades only SPY/QQQ/TLT (the most liquid ETFs on earth, penny-wide spreads, billions/day) and SPY/QQQ index options (deepest option chains). DEFAULT_COSTS (6 bps round-trip spread + 4 bps slippage) is conservative for these names — true retail slippage is lower. Trade frequency is LOW (front-end inversions cluster a handful of times per year, with multi-day holds), so annual turnover and aggregate frictions are modest. The binding constraint is NOT capacity but (i) whipsaw on isolated 1-2 day inversions that revert (mitigated by the 2-consecutive-close trigger + hysteresis), and (ii) cash-drag/opportunity cost of being in TLT/cash during a false alarm or a V-shaped recovery. Signal decay is the real risk: front-end-backwardation timing is a published, somewhat-crowded signal whose standalone return has partially decayed — but its crash-avoidance / convexity leg rests on persistent structural forced-deleveraging flow, and its VALUE to THIS book is orthogonality to short-vol, which does not arbitrage away.
spec.falsifier: Run the walk-forward backtest (src/lib/backtest engine against the StrategyContext) over the deepest available ^VIX3M/^VIX9D history (>=2011 if VIX9D allows, else VIX3M-only) on SPY with DEFAULT_COSTS. The spec is REFUTED and discarded if, out-of-sample, ANY of: (1) E[r_SPY,15d | TRIGGER_ON] - E[r_SPY,15d unconditional] >= 0 (sign flips wrong) AND conditional 5th-pctile 15d loss is NOT more negative than unconditional at one-sided p<0.05 (stationary block bootstrap, block=21d) — i.e., the trigger carries no left-tail information; (2) the beta-cut overlay's full-sample max drawdown is NOT reduced by >= 15% relative vs always-long SPY net of costs; (3) the overlay's Sortino does not exceed always-long SPY net of costs; (4) the overlay's daily-return correlation to a 16-delta/35-DTE SPY CSP P&L proxy on the worst-decile equity days is >= -0.2 (fails the orthogonality/tail-hedge raison d'etre); or (5) the optional put-debit-spread sleeve has negative mean P&L per episode net of black-scholes mid-minus-half-spread fills + $0.65/contract over the trigger sample. Concretely: if conditional-vs-unconditional 5th-percentile 15d SPY return shows no statistically significant left-tail widening AND drawdown is not cut >=15%, kill the candidate.

redteam.lens: COSTS & CAPACITY
redteam.kill: The 25 vs 22 bps margin is incoherent and the dominant cost is unmodeled. (1) The spec itself disowns expectedEdgeBpsPerTrade: mechanism/H1/falsifier all state the value is "CONDITIONAL TAIL/VARIANCE-REDUCTION... not a reliable positive-mean alpha... not average bps." A 3 bps net margin built on a mean the author says is unreliable cannot clear a cost hurdle. (2) The equity toggle is understated: a full de-risk-and-restore cycle is 4 ETF legs (sell SPY/buy TLT on entry, reverse on exit) at ~10 bps round-trip each, plus daily OFF_INT rescaling for 15 days (rebalanceDays=1) generating repeated partial SPY/TLT round trips — realistic episode cost ~30-40 bps, not 22. (3) FATAL: the put-debit-spread sleeve — the only leg with a standalone positive-expectancy claim (falsifier #5) — has NO option bid/ask cost term anywhere. costs.ts (DEFAULT_COSTS) models only equity-grade 6+4 bps spread/slippage and $0.65/contract; tradeCost() applies equity bps to every instrument. black-scholes.ts yields a theoretical mid, not a quote. The spec's 'mid minus half the modeled bid/ask' invents a half-spread with no width parameter in the cost model. Web evidence: deep-OTM SPY put bid/ask runs ~25% of mid in calm tape and >80% of mid during stress (navnoorbawa/SpotGamma) — and this strategy fires ONLY during front-end backwardation, i.e. exactly the stress regime where OTM-put spreads blow out. 'Mid minus half-spread' on a 25-80%-of-mid market = paying 12.5-40% of premium in slippage per crossing, twice. Round-trip frictional drag on a 20/10-delta spread is plausibly 25-80% of the net debit, before ~$2.60/spread commission. The take-profit (>=100% of max spread value) rarely triggers, so the modal reverting-inversion episode eats the full debit plus both crossings — falsifier #5 (mean spread P&L > 0 net of fills) almost certainly fails on a realistic, not theoretical, spread.
redteam.mitigation: For the equity toggle to survive as a COST claim (not just a defensive claim): reframe — drop the bps-vs-bps framing entirely and validate only on drawdown reduction / Sortino net of a corrected turnover model that charges all 4 ETF legs per episode plus daily-rescaling round trips. For the options sleeve to survive: add a real option-bid/ask term to costs.ts parameterized by moneyness and VIX regime (e.g. spread = max($0.05, k% of mid) with k rising to 50-80% for deep-OTM puts when VIX>25), fill at NBBO not theoretical mid, and re-run falsifier #5 with that model plus integer-contract rounding and per-contract commission on the small-account capital base. If the spread's net-of-realistic-fill expectancy is still negative (very likely), drop the convexity sleeve and keep only the SPY->TLT/cash de-gross, justified solely on max-drawdown reduction >=15% relative (falsifier #2), which is a defensible non-bps claim.
redteam.residual: Yes, but only a DEFENSIVE residual, not a cost-clearing edge. The pure equity de-gross to TLT/cash plausibly survives on its own terms because it is rarely traded (handful of inversions/year), trades the most liquid ETFs on earth (penny spreads, capacity genuinely unbounded — that sub-claim is correct), and its value is measured as avoided drawdown / improved Sortino rather than positive mean bps — so a ~30-40 bps episode cost is small against a left-tail it is trying to dodge. That leg can clear falsifier #2/#3 even if it never clears '25 > 22 bps.' What does NOT survive: (a) the expectedEdgeBpsPerTrade=25 > estCostBpsPerTrade=22 arithmetic as written, since the author disowns the mean and understates turnover; and (b) the put-debit-spread sleeve's standalone positive expectancy, which is very likely negative once the omitted option bid/ask (25-80% of mid in the stress regime it trades) plus per-contract commissions and integer rounding are charged. So strip the convexity sleeve, keep the de-gross as a drawdown-reduction overlay, and stop calling it a bps edge.

redteam.lens: DATA-SNOOPING, DECAY & ORTHOGONALITY — (a) is the mechanism real or a curve-fit/known-arbitraged anomaly that has decayed; (b) is the stream orthogonal to put-selling short-vol or short-vol/beta in disguise?
redteam.kill: The load-bearing DIRECTIONAL/standalone-alpha half is a published, crowded, decayed anomaly. VIX term-structure/front-end-backwardation timing is textbook material (Quantpedia "Exploiting Term Structure of VIX Futures"; MDPI 2019 "VIX Futures as a Market Timing Indicator"; Macrosynergy; numerous Substacks) — the canonical published edge is "the END of backwardation is a contrarian BUY signal" and the roll-yield short-vol harvest, NOT a reliable negative forward equity mean at inversion onset. The spec itself concedes this (disclaims positive-mean alpha; falsifier (1) allows the mean sign not to flip), so the surviving claim collapses to a single conditional-tail statistic. That statistic is largely COINCIDENT/mechanical rather than predictive: front-end inversion IS the early vol spike (backwardation "preceded 100% of major drawdowns" because inversion and drawdown are the same event viewed two ways), so the raw conditional left-tail widening overstates the genuine FORWARD, post-2-consecutive-close, T+1, net-of-cost-and-cash-drag incremental edge — which is unproven. Under default-to-skepticism, a decayed-mechanism anomaly whose only residual claim is an unverified, near-tautological tail conditioner does not clear the bar.
redteam.mitigation: Prong (b) is NOT a kill: the overlay is genuinely orthogonal, not short-vol in disguise. It goes LONG the painful state (de-gross to TLT/cash, long put-debit convexity) exactly when put-selling P&L implodes (Volmageddon, "short convexity," correlations-to-1) — so the target <= -0.3 worst-decile correlation to a CSP P&L proxy is mechanically plausible and the insurance/negative-sign property does not arbitrage away (capital is structurally reluctant to be short crash insurance). To make the candidate SURVIVE overall: the pre-registered falsifier must actually PASS out-of-sample on the parts that matter — specifically (i) conditional 5th-pctile 15d SPY loss more negative than unconditional at one-sided p<0.05 via stationary block bootstrap AFTER the 2-day-confirm + T+1 lag (proving forward, not coincident, tail info), AND (ii) full-sample max-DD cut >=15% net of DEFAULT_COSTS despite cash-drag/whipsaw on false alarms and V-recoveries, AND (iii) worst-decile correlation to the CSP proxy <= -0.2. If the lag erases the tail significance or cash-drag eats the drawdown reduction, it is dead. Frame and size it purely as a tail-hedge/short-vol diversifier (the conceded role), not a return generator, and keep the sleeve <=30% so cash-drag cannot dominate.
redteam.residual: Yes, a real but narrow residual: the ORTHOGONALITY value survives intact — a de-grossing/long-convexity overlay that prints negative-to-zero in calm regimes but negatively correlates to put-selling losses in the left tail is a legitimate, non-decaying portfolio diversifier for a short-vol book, regardless of standalone alpha. What does NOT survive is any directional/standalone-alpha or "positive expectancy" claim (decayed, crowded), and the magnitude of the incremental FORWARD tail-reduction net of whipsaw + cash-drag is unproven and likely much smaller than the raw conditional statistic suggests. So: keep it ONLY if it passes its own falsifier as a pure insurance/orthogonality sleeve; discard any expectation of average-bps edge. Edge left = diversification/tail-hedge, conditional on the backtest confirming forward (not coincident) left-tail widening and a >=15% net DD cut.

redteam.lens: REGIME & ROBUSTNESS (regime dependence, parameter sensitivity, crowding, tail risk; survival across 2018-Q4 / Mar-2020 / 2022 / Aug-2024)
redteam.kill: The signal is structurally REACTIVE, not predictive, and its regime profile is the opposite of robust: front-end VIX backwardation does not lead drawdowns, it coincides with ones already underway. Cboe's own research ("Is VIX Backwardation Necessarily a Sign of a Future Down Market?") and the literature show backwardation clusters at/near bottoms, after the drop, and is followed by violent relief rallies — which the spec itself admits ("relief bounces are violent"). A de-grossed-to-cash + long-convexity book is by construction SHORT those bounces, so the edge depends entirely on catching the few days between signal and trough, a window the 2-consecutive-close confirm + T+1 execution + 252d z-score warmup all widen against it. Episode-by-episode: (1) Aug-2024 is a near-fatal counterexample — VIX gapped 180% to 65 pre-market and collapsed to 39 by the SAME close, SPY recouped the whole week by Friday; a 2-close-confirm timer arms at the Aug-5 close and (per the ACTUAL engine, which fills at close i, not the spec's claimed T+1) buys protection and de-grosses into the bottom of a one-day flush, then misses the rip. This is a textbook gap-and-reverse that the design is maximally exposed to. (2) 2022 breaks the mechanism the other way: a low-vol grind where SPY fell ~25% with NO sustained VIX spike; backwardation appeared on only ~63 of 250 days in recurring on-off WAVES (whipsaw), so the front-end was in contango for most of the decline and the trigger was simply OFF while the worst cumulative damage accrued — pure missed-signal regime dependence. Worse, the de-risk destination TLT fell ~30% in 2022, so the "route to cash when TLT 20d < -3%" branch fires persistently, converting the hedge into cash drag while the signal is mostly absent. (3) 2018-Q4 and Mar-2020 are the favorable cases (it would cut drawdown), but both featured the violent V-bounces (Dec-26-2018, Mar-13/24/26-2020 +6-9% days) that bleed the cash/TLT drag and decay the 30-45 DTE put-debit sleeve precisely when it is open. Parameter sensitivity compounds this: the thresholds (R9>1.00, R3<1.00, R3>1.05 hysteresis, OFF_INT=-Z/2 clamp, 2-day confirm, 15d time-stop, 3% TLT cutoff) are numerous, hand-tuned, and interact — small changes flip whipsaw vs lag. Crowding: the standalone directional version is a published, partially-decayed signal (the spec concedes this). Finally, an IMPLEMENTATION robustness failure undercuts the falsifier itself: the backtest harness the spec names (src/lib/backtest engine + RegimeSnapshot) only plumbs vix and vix9d — there is NO vix3m, ^SKEW, HYG, or TLT in AlignedData or StrategyContext.regime (engine.ts lines 21-32, 100-105; types.ts lines 21-29). The entire trigger hinges on R3=^VIX3M/^VIX and confirms on SKEW/HYG, none of which are exposed no-look-ahead in the stated engine, so the regime backtest as specified cannot even be run faithfully; and the spec's "T+1 execution" claim contradicts the engine, which fills at the signal close (engine.ts lines 11-14), flattering exactly the crisis fills that matter most.
redteam.mitigation: For survival under this lens it would need: (a) the harness extended to plumb ^VIX3M, ^SKEW, HYG, TLT into AlignedData + a no-look-ahead RegimeSnapshot, and a verified ENFORCED T+1 (next-session) fill so crisis-day gap fills are not flattered; (b) an explicit gap/whipsaw guard — e.g., do NOT arm on a single-session vol explosion that has already mean-reverted intraday (an Aug-2024 filter), and require the move to persist beyond one close on a NON-overlapping basis; (c) a regime-conditional study proving the edge survives in the 2022-style low-vol grind where backwardation is absent — i.e., evidence the overlay adds value when the trigger rarely fires, otherwise it is a pure bull/spike-regime tool blind to slow bears; (d) a TLT-destination stress check showing the cash fallback does not itself become persistent drag in rising-rate regimes; (e) parameter-stability evidence (grid/bootstrap over the threshold set) showing the drawdown-reduction and -0.3 tail-correlation results are not a knife-edge of the chosen constants; and crucially (f) honest accounting of the violent-relief-rally cost: a P&L attribution separating avoided-drawdown from give-back on the bounce, net of put-spread theta, across all four episodes — not just max-drawdown reduction, which is the metric most flattered by a reactive de-grosser.
redteam.residual: Yes, a NARROW residual edge survives, which is why this is Serious not Fatal. The spec is unusually honest in framing this as a CONDITIONAL tail/variance-reduction and convexity overlay (not positive-mean alpha), capping it at <=30% of book with hard-bounded (max-loss = net debit) convexity premium of 1-2%. In the two slow-onset, sustained-vol crises (Mar-2020 most clearly, 2018-Q4 partially) a reactive de-grosser plausibly does reduce drawdown and is genuinely negatively correlated to a short-vol/put-selling book in the left tail — its stated raison d'etre (orthogonality to the incumbent CSP sleeve) is real and does not arbitrage away, because being short crash insurance is structurally uncomfortable to warehouse. So as a small, defined-risk, accept-the-cash-drag tail hedge layered on a put-selling book, it can earn its keep via correlation, not bps. But the lens question — does it work across regimes and survive all four episodes — is answered NO: it is regime-dependent (helps in sustained-vol selloffs, hurts or is absent in gap-and-reverse like Aug-2024 and low-vol grinds like 2022), parameter-fragile, and built on a partially-crowded reactive signal. Under default skepticism, with the conditional-mean weakly negative, the drawdown-reduction metric flattered by reactivity, and the named backtest harness unable to run the trigger as specified, the edge is not robustly established and survives=false is the correct call.

name: VIX-conditioned cross-sectional 5-day reversal (liquidity-provision premium harvester)
rationale: Fatal orthogonality inversion — short-term reversal IS the same short-vol factor as the incumbent (loses contemporaneously on VIX spikes per Nagel 2012), so the "convexity hedge" pitch is false-signed, and the pro-cyclical 2.5x VIX-leverage stacks correlated tail risk into the worst day rather than hedging it; the ~8bp net edge is also inside the cost error bar once costs are budgeted per-leg (codebase DEFAULT_COSTS = 5bps/side, charged on every leg + daily hedge re-rebalance), so net is likely <=0 in low/mid VIX terciles.
bestUseInEnsemble: Only the re-specified honest version is admissible: a small, separately-capitalized, COUNTER-cyclically-sized (de-lever into dVIX spikes, deploy after realized vol mean-reverts) large-cap liquidity-provision sleeve, traded only in the stable-elevated/top-VIX-but-not-deleveraging regime with a banded (not daily) beta re-hedge. Treat it as a second thin short-vol premium sleeve diversified by horizon/surface region — NOT as a short-gamma hedge to the put-selling book. It must first pass its own pre-registered falsifier #4 (net P&L vs contemporaneous dVIX > 0) on a post-2015 holdout under full per-leg costs before any capital; the literature predicts that test fails, which is the gating kill.

spec.name: VIX-conditioned cross-sectional 5-day reversal (liquidity-provision premium harvester)
spec.family: Short-term mean reversion / cross-sectional reversal (liquidity-provision premium)
spec.mechanism: Short-horizon cross-sectional reversal is the compensation a liquidity provider earns for absorbing uninformed order-flow imbalances; it is a risk premium that scales with inventory risk. When dealers/HFTs withdraw (high realized vol / high VIX), the price concession demanded by forced sellers (and paid by forced buyers) widens, so the reversal you collect by buying the 5d losers and shorting the 5d winners grows. This is Nagel (2012, "Evaporating Liquidity", NBER w17653: reversal returns rise with VIX) and Novy-Marx & Velikov (2023, "Reversals and the Returns to Liquidity Provision", NBER w30917: net-of-cost reversal survives in large caps with turnover reduction). It is an economic premium, not a curve-fit pattern: the conditioning variable (VIX) and the sign of the conditioning (more dealer withdrawal -> larger reversal) are predicted ex ante by the inventory-risk model, and the edge must be gated to PURE liquidity shocks by removing names whose 5d move is information-driven (earnings, attention/short-vol spikes), because information moves do NOT revert.
spec.hypothesisH0: Within the ~80-name liquid large-cap PutStrike universe, the dollar- and beta-neutral bottom-decile-minus-top-decile 5-day-residual-return portfolio earns a mean net-of-cost return <= 0 per 4-day holding period, AND its net return does not increase with the VIX-scaling regime (i.e. the high-VIX bucket's net mean is not greater than the low-VIX bucket's). Equivalently: after the earnings/attention information gate and the turnover-reduction buffer, realized round-trip cost >= gross reversal edge, so there is no harvestable liquidity-provision premium at daily EOD cadence.
spec.hypothesisH1: The dollar/beta-neutral 5d-reversal decile spread, gated to pure liquidity shocks (drop names within +/-3 trading days of earnings and names with a same-window Wikipedia-pageview or FINRA-short-volume spike) and traded only through a buffer band that nets against the existing book, earns a positive net-of-cost return per 4-day hold, and that net return is monotonically increasing across VIX buckets (low < mid < high), with the majority of cumulative P&L concentrated in the top VIX tercile. The spread's net P&L is positively correlated with contemporaneous realized-vol / VIX spikes (it makes money when short-vol books bleed), giving it near-zero net index/vega exposure and positive convexity to the incumbent's short-gamma.
spec.signalDefinition:
Daily at EOD, for each name i in the ~80-name liquid universe, compute the 5-trading-day residual (idiosyncratic) return:
  r5_resid[i,t] = sum_{k=0..4} ( ret[i,t-k] - beta[i] * ret[SPY,t-k] - beta_sector[i] * ret[sectorSPDR(i),t-k] )
where ret is the daily close-to-close log return; beta[i] is the PutStrike quote beta (yahoo-finance2 beta field); beta_sector[i] is the OLS beta of name i to its sector SPDR over a trailing 60d window (sectorSPDR via XLY/XLP/XLK/... mapping). If the relative-strength engineered feature already supplies a residual-vs-SPY trailing return, use that as r5_resid directly (it is the intended primary; the explicit formula above is the fallback so the spec is self-contained).
Standardize cross-sectionally each day: z[i,t] = ( r5_resid[i,t] - median_j r5_resid[j,t] ) / MAD_j( r5_resid[j,t] )  (median/MAD for outlier robustness).
RAW signal = -z (reversal: buy losers = most-negative z, short winners = most-positive z).
INFORMATION GATE (set name to ineligible, signal forced to 0) if ANY of:
  (a) calendar/earnings feature flags an earnings date within +/-3 trading days of t;
  (b) Wikipedia-pageview feature z-score (trailing 20d) > 2.0 over the same 5d window (retail attention/info event);
  (c) FINRA short-volume-ratio feature z-score (trailing 20d) > 2.0 over the same 5d window (informed short pressure, not pure liquidity);
  (d) name is on the hard-to-borrow screen (proxy below) on the short side.
