VIX SCALE: g[t] = clip( VIX_close[t] / 16 , 0.5 , 2.5 )  (VIX from ^VIX). Optional confirmation filter: require ^VIX9D/^VIX3M term structure not in a state that flags a directional news regime; not part of the core signal.
spec.entryRule: EOD daily. From the eligible (non-gated) universe, rank by z[i,t]. Target LONG = names in the bottom decile of z (biggest 5d residual losers); target SHORT = names in the top decile of z (biggest 5d residual winners). Require |z| >= 1.0 to enter at all (BUFFER BAND: do not open a position whose signal is inside the band, this is the turnover-reduction overlay that makes it survive costs per Novy-Marx & Velikov). Construct the book dollar-neutral (sum long $ = sum short $) and beta-neutral by overlaying a SPY (and sector-SPDR) hedge leg so net portfolio beta to SPY ~ 0 and net sector beta ~ 0. Gross target = base_gross * g[t], where base_gross is set by the capital rule below. NETTING: compute target weights, then only trade the DIFFERENCE versus the current book; if a currently-held name's z has merely drifted but is still same-sign and outside the band, hold it (no churn). Fills at the official close (or next-open if modeling a realistic EOD execution lag); model costs per estCost below.
spec.exitRule: Exit a position when ANY: (1) holding period reaches 5 trading days (hard max; horizon is 3-5d, target 4); (2) the name's z reverts past 0 (signal consumed) OR falls inside the buffer band |z| < 0.5 on the exit check (asymmetric inner band to avoid re-entry churn); (3) the information gate trips while held (earnings now within +/-3d, or a pageview/short-vol spike appears) -> flatten that name immediately at next EOD; (4) hard-to-borrow / buy-in on the short leg -> cover. Rebalance the dollar/beta-neutral hedge daily at EOD to keep net beta ~ 0. All entries/exits EOD only; no intraday.
spec.instruments: Equity long/short cross-sectional basket within the ~80-name liquid large-cap optionable universe; dollar-neutral and beta-neutral. Hedge leg in SPY plus sector SPDRs (XLY/XLP/XLK/XLF/XLE/XLV/XLI/...) to strip index and sector beta. No options used for the core trade (equity-only); options-chain underlying bid/ask is used only as a per-name spread proxy for cost modeling.
spec.positionSizing: Fixed capital C. Base gross exposure base_gross = 1.0 * C per side at VIX=16 (i.e. ~1.0x long, 1.0x short, ~2.0x gross at neutral VIX); actual gross = base_gross * g[t] with g[t]=clip(VIX/16,0.5,2.5) so gross ranges ~1.0x C (calm) to ~5.0x C gross (VIX~40) -- this leaning-in IS the strategy and is the source of the convexity, but cap total gross at 5.0x C and per-name weight at min(2% of C, 10% of name 21d ADV) to bound liquidity/impact. Equal-weight within each decile leg (or inverse-vol weight by Parkinson/GK vol feature to equalize risk contribution). Risk cap: target ex-ante portfolio vol via the engineered Parkinson/GK vol features; scale the whole book down if estimated daily vol > 0.75% of C (hard stop on gross if realized DD on the sleeve exceeds 4% of allocated capital in a 10d window -> halve base_gross until it recovers). Beta-neutral overlay sized so |net SPY beta| < 0.05 and |net sector beta| < 0.05 of C.
spec.dataSources: All already in PutStrike, nothing new: (1) yahoo-finance2 10y daily OHLCV for close-to-close returns, 5d residual ranks, ADV; (2) yahoo-finance2 quote beta + 60d sector-SPDR OLS beta for the residualization and hedge; (3) ^VIX for the g[t] scale (^VIX9D/^VIX3M optional term-structure confirmation); (4) engineered relative-strength feature (residual-vs-SPY trailing return) as the primary signal source; (5) calendar/earnings feature for the +/-3d earnings gate; (6) Wikipedia-pageview feature (retail attention) and FINRA short-volume-ratio feature for the information-shock vs liquidity-shock separation; (7) Parkinson/Garman-Klass/Hurst regime vol features for inverse-vol weighting and the vol cap; (8) drawdown/gap features as optional secondary liquidity-shock confirmation; (9) options-chain underlying bid/ask (yahoo-finance2 full chain) as the per-name spread proxy for cost modeling; (10) Neon Postgres simulated_trades + capital_events for the capital-aware backtest. The 6-model ensemble's mean-reversion sleeve and the iTransformer forecast are NOT required (can be used as an optional confirmation overlay but are excluded from the core falsifiable signal to keep it clean).
spec.capacityNote: Designed for retail fixed capital, not size. The ~80-name universe is the most liquid US large-caps (mega-cap, tight spreads), which is exactly the only universe where net-of-cost reversal survives (Novy-Marx & Velikov: large-cap only; small-cap reversal is eaten by costs). At retail capital (say $0.1-5M) per-name weights are far below 10% of ADV so market impact is ~0 and the options-spread-proxy + 5-10bp slippage assumption is conservative. Capacity is hard-capped by two things: (1) the decile structure on 80 names gives only ~8 longs / ~8 shorts, so single-name idiosyncratic risk is real -> needs the inverse-vol weighting and the per-name cap; (2) net of costs the edge is THIN (~8 bps/trade), so it scales linearly with capital only until impact appears (low-tens of $M), after which the buffer band must widen and turnover falls, cutting gross edge. Borrow on the short leg is the binding real-world constraint: mega-caps are easy-to-borrow (cheap GC), but any hard-to-borrow name must be screened out (no borrow data in PutStrike -> proxy the screen by flagging names with FINRA short-volume-ratio persistently >40% AND small float; when in doubt, drop from the short leg and run that side lighter). The strategy is genuinely orthogonal-to-anti-correlated with the incumbent put-selling/short-vol book: reversal P&L rises in vol spikes, so it hedges the firm's short-gamma -- its value is as much diversification/convexity as standalone return.
spec.falsifier: Run the 10y daily EOD backtest in the Neon sim layer with full modeled costs (per-name spread from options-chain-underlying proxy + 5-10bp slippage + borrow + the earnings/attention gate + the |z|>=1 buffer + book-netting). The strategy is FALSIFIED if ANY of: (1) the mean net-of-cost return per 4-day hold is <= 0 across the full sample (i.e. modeled round-trip cost >= gross reversal edge -> EOD cadence kills it); (2) net return is NOT monotonically increasing across VIX terciles (low <= mid <= high fails, or the top VIX tercile is not the largest contributor to cumulative P&L) -> the claimed inventory-risk/VIX mechanism is absent and any positive result is unconditioned curve-fit; (3) removing the earnings+pageview+short-volume information gate does NOT degrade net performance (if gated and ungated are statistically indistinguishable, the "pure liquidity shock" mechanism is not what is being harvested); (4) the sleeve's net P&L correlation to contemporaneous d(VIX) is <= 0 (it does not actually make money when vol spikes -> the orthogonality/convexity claim to the short-vol incumbent is false). Pre-register: gross edge target ~22 bps/trade, modeled cost ~14 bps/trade, net ~8 bps/trade; if the executed backtest shows net <= 0 after costs in the large-cap universe, the candidate is dead as specified.

redteam.lens: COSTS & CAPACITY (retail frictions: spreads, slippage, daily hedge churn, borrow, granularity vs fixed capital; capacity)
redteam.kill: The whole thesis lives or dies on a +8bps net = 22bps gross - 14bps cost margin that the spec's own falsifier calls "THIN," and the cost side is materially under-budgeted while the gross side is unsupported at this horizon. (1) Cost is counted "per trade" as if a position is one round trip, but every position is a TWO-legged (long+short) pair held 4 days, so entry+exit = 4 single-name crossings, not 2. At the codebase's own DEFAULT_COSTS (3bps spread + 2bps slippage = 5bps/side) that is ~20bps round-trip on the single-name legs ALONE — already >=14bps before anything else. (2) The spec MANDATES re-hedging SPY plus multiple sector SPDRs every EOD to hold net beta ~0; over a 4-day hold that is ~4 daily hedge rebalances on a hedge notional that scales to 5x C in high VIX, and sector SPDRs (XLE/XLU/XLRE/XLB) run 2-5bps/side, not 1bp. That adds ~3-8bps/position that the 14bps budget never itemizes. (3) The 22bps gross is not supported at a 4-day single-name idiosyncratic-residual horizon on only ~8 longs/8 shorts: Nagel (2012) shows the reversal/liquidity premium is large gross but, OUTSIDE crisis windows, the daily reversal had decayed to <0.2%/day gross by 2007 and is largely consumed by bid-ask in normal times — and the academic results (Nagel weekly/monthly; Novy-Marx FAJ 2024) are framed at weekly/monthly horizons where turnover reduction is REQUIRED to keep net positive. The information gate compounds the problem: dropping earnings/attention/short-vol names removes exactly the forced-flow, high-concession events that carry the fat tail of the premium, shrinking gross while costs are fixed. Net result: realistic round-trip friction is ~18-28bps/position vs a gross edge that is optimistic-to-unsupported, so net sits at roughly -2 to +4bps — inside the error bar, i.e. not reliably positive. The spec's own falsifier condition #1 (net<=0 kills it) is the most likely outcome in the low/mid VIX terciles where it still always holds a book.
redteam.mitigation: For the edge to survive this lens: (a) Re-budget costs per POSITION not per trade — explicitly 4 single-name crossings (open L, open S, close L, close S) + the daily hedge-rebalance crossings, with separate, higher slippage for the non-mega-cap third of the 80 names (FCX/SLB/D/APD ~2-4bps/side) and 2-5bps/side on the sector SPDRs. (b) Resolve the dangerous "options-chain underlying bid/ask as spread proxy" ambiguity — confirm it reads the UNDERLYING stock NBBO (~1bp on AAPL/MSFT), not the option bid/ask (1-5% of premium, which would be catastrophic). (c) Replace the daily beta/sector re-hedge with a banded re-hedge (only trade the hedge when |net beta| breaches a threshold) to kill the largest avoidable cost; daily re-hedging of a 5x-gross book is the single biggest cost leak. (d) Pre-register the net result BY VIX TERCILE: the only defensible version concentrates trading in the top VIX tercile (where Nagel shows concessions are wide enough to clear cost) and trades minimally or not at all in the low tercile — i.e. make the VIX gate a trade/no-trade switch, not just a gross multiplier. (e) Source real borrow data or hard-cap the short leg to confirmed GC names; the FINRA-short-volume + small-float proxy will both miss intermittent specials and false-positive on heavily-shorted mega-caps. (f) Account for fixed-capital granularity: at $0.1-1M, 8 longs/8 shorts beta-and-sector-hedged means many sub-$50k legs where a $0.01 tick on a $400 stock is ~0.25bp of rounding drag per leg and odd-lot/whole-share rounding distorts dollar-neutrality.
redteam.residual: Yes, a narrow residual edge plausibly survives — but ONLY in the top VIX tercile, and ONLY as the spec's secondary (diversification/convexity-to-short-gamma) value proposition, not as the +8bps/trade standalone return it claims. Nagel/Novy-Marx genuinely document a real, VIX-rising, large-cap-only liquidity-provision premium, and the spec's instincts (mega-cap universe, buffer band, netting, VIX scaling) are the correct cost-mitigations — so the high-VIX, wide-concession regime can clear costs. What does NOT survive is the unconditional daily-cadence claim: in the low/mid VIX terciles (the majority of days, where it still carries and re-hedges a book), gross edge ~ cost and net is ~0 or negative once the daily hedge churn and two-legged round trips are budgeted honestly. So the surviving edge is conditional and thin: positive net concentrated in the top tercile (consistent with the spec's own monotonicity falsifier), near-zero net elsewhere, with most of the standalone-return claim eaten by costs. Treat it as a short-gamma hedge sleeve that occasionally pays, not as a 22-vs-14-bps profit engine.

redteam.lens: DATA-SNOOPING, DECAY & ORTHOGONALITY
redteam.kill: Prong (b) is an outright factual inversion that kills the strategy's entire reason for existing, and prong (a) independently degrades the standalone edge below zero. (b) ORTHOGONALITY — FATAL: The spec's H1 differentiator is that the sleeve "makes money when short-vol books bleed," gives "positive convexity to the incumbent's short-gamma," and is "orthogonal-to-anti-correlated with put-selling." This is backwards. The academic literature it cites is unanimous and explicit that short-term reversal / liquidity provision IS a short-volatility strategy: "liquidity providers are implicitly short a straddle, suffering losses when volatility spikes" and reversal returns "have a large negative exposure to volatility shocks" (Drechsler-Moreira-Savov; QuantPedia summary of the liquidity-provision literature). The Nagel (2012) result the spec leans on does NOT say the strategy makes money DURING a VIX spike — it says the strategy LOSES contemporaneously (short straddle) and the high VIX merely predicts high FORWARD expected return as compensation paid AFTER the spike. So the return stream is the SAME short-gamma/short-vol risk factor as the incumbent put-selling book, not a hedge to it. The spec compounds this by sizing gross UP to 5x at VIX~40 — it deliberately concentrates short-vol exposure exactly when the incumbent is also maximally short vol, stacking correlated tail risk and selling convexity, the opposite of the claimed "convexity to short-gamma." The spec's own falsifier #4 (net P&L vs contemporaneous d(VIX) must be > 0) is the correct test and the literature predicts it comes out negative — the strategy fails its own pre-registered criterion. (a) DECAY — SERIOUS-to-FATAL on its own: Nagel (2012, NBER w17653 / RFS 2012) and Blitz residual-reversal (2011) are real, correctly cited, but are 13-15-year-old PUBLISHED anomalies. McLean & Pontiff (2016, JF) show such predictors decay ~58% post-publication, worst for high-in-sample-return and high-idio-risk/illiquid predictors. Chordia-Goyal-Saretto (2020) and the broader consensus: the classic short-term reversal effect "has steadily weakened over time, to the point of now having vanished entirely in most regions," and surviving mean-reversion profits accrue to participants with EXECUTION advantages (low latency, better data, lower costs) — not a retail EOD trader, who is the marginal slow loser of this game. Avramov-Chordia-Goyal (2006): after spreads + impact, reversal profits evaporate. The spec restricts to ~80 mega-caps — the most HFT-saturated, most-competed, lowest-gross-edge corner — and concedes net edge is only ~8 bps/trade. That 8 bps is a PRE-decay, pre-competition number lifted from the survivorship-and-publication-biased large-cap result; post-decay it is plausibly <= 0, which is the spec's own falsifier #1.
redteam.mitigation: For the strategy to survive this lens, ALL of the following would have to be true and demonstrated, not asserted: (1) On orthogonality — the team must drop the "anti-correlated convexity to short-vol" thesis entirely; it is false as a matter of factor structure. The honest reframe is "this is a SECOND short-vol/liquidity-provision sleeve that earns a separate premium," which then must be justified on diversification-within-short-vol grounds (different horizon, different vol surface region) AND must REMOVE the pro-cyclical VIX-leaning sizing — leaning gross to 5x at VIX~40 stacks tail risk onto the incumbent's worst day and is exactly wrong if both books are short vol. A defensible version would size DOWN or flat into vol spikes and only deploy capital after realized vol mean-reverts. (2) The pre-registered falsifier #4 must be run FIRST on out-of-sample 2015-2025 data and must show net P&L vs contemporaneous d(VIX) > 0; the literature predicts it will be < 0, so this is the single gating test. If it is negative (expected), the orthogonality claim is dead and the sleeve is just leverage on the firm's existing risk. (3) On decay — backtest must be split into a held-out post-2015 (post-publication, post-HFT-saturation) window and show net-of-FULL-cost edge still positive in THAT window alone, not the full 10y sample (which is dominated by the pre-2012 in-sample-overlapping period where the anomaly was alive). Costs must include realistic mega-cap EOD slippage and the fact that a daily-EOD retail trader is adversely selected against intraday liquidity providers who have already harvested the reversal. (4) The "pure liquidity shock" information gate must be shown to improve net performance (falsifier #3) — if it does not, the residual edge is unconditioned curve-fit, not the inventory-risk mechanism. Realistically, even with all mitigations the best achievable outcome is a small, decayed, second short-vol sleeve — never the orthogonal hedge the spec claims.
redteam.residual: Very little, and what remains is mislabeled. After the attack: (i) The orthogonality/convexity edge is ZERO and negative-signed — it is the same short-vol factor as the incumbent, so it ADDS correlated tail risk rather than diversifying it; the pro-cyclical 5x VIX-leverage makes the combined book MORE fragile on a 2008/2020-style day, not less. This is not a residual edge, it is a residual liability. (ii) Any standalone return edge is a decayed, publication-known, large-cap-only liquidity-provision premium of ~8 bps gross-of-decay; in the HFT-saturated mega-cap universe at daily-EOD retail cadence the realistic post-decay net is plausibly <= 0 (the spec's own falsifier #1). (iii) The one genuinely defensible residual is modest: a SMALL, separately-capitalized, counter-cyclically-sized (size down into vol spikes) liquidity-provision sleeve MIGHT earn a thin positive premium out-of-sample IF the information gate and turnover buffer demonstrably survive full costs in a post-2015 holdout — but that is a "maybe a few bps of a second short-vol bet," explicitly NOT the orthogonal short-gamma hedge that is the spec's entire pitch. The load-bearing claim of the strategy does not survive; mark survives=false.

redteam.lens: REGIME & ROBUSTNESS: regime dependence (bull/low-vol vs crisis/gaps), parameter sensitivity, crowding, tail risk; survival across 2018-Q4, Mar-2020, 2022, Aug-2024
redteam.kill: The strategy's defining claim — "net P&L is positively correlated with contemporaneous d(VIX); it makes money when vol spikes" (H1, falsifier #4) — confuses contemporaneous with path-dependent, and the VIX-leverage rule levers INTO the exact moment the edge inverts. Mechanism: short-horizon reversal is short-gamma in its P&L PATH. When VIX spikes because of FORCED DELEVERAGING (not benign uninformed order flow), the "5d losers" are precisely the names crowded liquidity providers are dumping to meet redemptions, so they keep falling for days before the snapback. This is not hypothetical — it is exactly what the cited Nagel (2012) mechanism does in its worst realization. Independently sourced March-2020 evidence (ExtractAlpha): the identical "buy 5d losers / short 5d winners" book took a 10-sigma loss on Mar 17-18, drew down to roughly -20% to -25% cumulative, THEN had a 20-sigma up day on the 19th. The spec's g[t]=clip(VIX/16,0.5,2.5) rule would have had the book at ~2.5x gross (VIX hit ~82, clipped to the 2.5 cap) going INTO that 10-sigma loss — i.e. it maximally levers the position the day before the worst loss in the strategy's history. The 4%/10d drawdown circuit-breaker (halve base_gross) trips AFTER the gap, locking in the loss and cutting the very gross that would have harvested the 20-sigma rebound on the 19th — the leverage rule and the kill-switch are perfectly anti-phased to the convexity they claim to capture. Same structure damaged quant reversal books in Aug-2007, Q4-2018 (Dec gap-downs in mega-caps), and is the mechanical risk in any gappy 2022 CPI/FOMC session. The convexity is REAL but LAGGED and CROWDING-CONDITIONAL: you must survive a multi-sigma path-drawdown at peak leverage to collect it, and a retail fixed-capital book with a 4%/10d hard stop structurally cannot. So the headline H1 — "positive convexity to the incumbent's short-gamma, hedges the firm's short-vol book" — is backwards at the only moments that matter: both books bleed simultaneously during the deleveraging phase (correlated, not hedged), and only decouple if you survive to the snapback. Additional regime/robustness fractures: (1) PARAMETER SENSITIVITY — the entire result hinges on the information gate (falsifier #3 concedes that if gated == ungated, the mechanism is absent), but the gate is built from noisy free-threshold proxies (Wikipedia-pageview z>2, FINRA short-vol z>2, both trailing-20d) that are unvalidated, lagged, and will themselves spike market-wide during a crisis — so in exactly the high-VIX tercile where 'majority of P&L' is claimed, the gate either nukes the whole eligible universe (no trades) or fails to distinguish information from liquidity (the COVID factor in Mar-2020 was an INFORMATION shock dressed as a liquidity shock, and information shocks do NOT revert — the spec admits this). (2) CROWDING — the edge is net ~8 bps/trade (22 gross - 14 cost) in the most liquid, most-arbitraged mega-cap names on the planet; this is the single most crowded systematic trade and the literature (NY Fed SR513, Nagel) shows unconditional large-cap reversal already decayed toward zero net-of-cost as liquidity improved. An 8bp net edge has no margin to absorb either further crowding decay or the cost-model being optimistic by even a few bps. (3) The cost model uses a static options-underlying bid/ask + 5-10bp slippage proxy — but spreads BLOW OUT 5-20x in exactly the high-VIX regime where the gross edge supposedly grows, so realized cost is regime-correlated with the signal and the net 8bp almost certainly goes negative precisely in the top VIX tercile that is supposed to carry the P&L. (4) The 4%/10d gross-halving rule plus the inverse-vol scaling create a pro-cyclical de-risking that guarantees you are smallest right when the unconditional Sharpe is highest (post-crash reversal) — the opposite of the regime timing the thesis requires.
redteam.mitigation: For the edge to survive this lens: (a) Replace the contemporaneous-d(VIX) convexity claim with an honest path-aware one and PROVE survival of the drawdown phase — backtest must report the WORST intra-episode peak-to-trough on the sleeve (not just per-trade mean) across Aug-2007, Q4-2018, Mar-2020, the 2022 CPI/FOMC gap days, and Aug-5-2024, with the actual g[t] leverage applied that day; if max sleeve DD exceeds the 4% circuit-breaker, the strategy kills itself before the rebound and the thesis is dead. (b) Invert or floor the VIX-leverage rule during VIX-SPIKE (not VIX-LEVEL) states — lever into elevated-but-stable vol, DE-lever into d(VIX) spikes / cross-sectional dispersion blowouts, because the inventory-risk premium is paid for stable-elevated vol, not for the deleveraging cascade itself. Use ^VIX9D/^VIX3M term-structure inversion (already in the spec as 'optional') as a HARD regime gate, not optional. (c) Validate the information gate out-of-sample as a standalone: show pageview/short-vol z-thresholds actually separate reverting from non-reverting moves in 2018/2020/2022, and show the gate does not empty the universe in crises; tune-sensitivity of the z>2 thresholds (try 1.5, 2.5, 3.0) and require the monotonic-VIX-tercile result to be stable across them. (d) Re-estimate costs with a REGIME-CONDITIONAL spread model (spread as a function of contemporaneous VIX), not a static proxy — the 8bp net must survive crisis-period spreads. (e) Demonstrate, not assume, low correlation to the incumbent short-vol book day-by-day THROUGH a crisis episode, not on average — the average can be ~0 while both bleed together in the tail. (f) Stress single-name idiosyncratic risk: with only ~8 longs/8 shorts after the gate strips names in a crisis, one mega-cap gap (e.g. a 2022 single-stock 20% earnings gap that slips the +/-3d window) can dominate the sleeve.
redteam.residual: Partial residual edge survives, but only in a much narrower regime than claimed and only if re-specified. What is genuinely robust: the UNCONDITIONAL-vol-elevated, NON-deleveraging harvest of liquidity-provision premium in mega-caps (Nagel's core result is real and VIX-predictability of reversal Sharpe is well-replicated) — i.e. the LOW-and-MID VIX terciles plus the STABLE-HIGH-vol days likely do produce a small positive net edge, and the inventory-risk sign is correct ex ante (not curve-fit). That is a real, if thin (~few bps net, decaying with crowding), diversifying sleeve. What does NOT survive: (1) the specific claim that the TOP VIX tercile carries the majority of P&L with positive convexity — under realistic costs and the path-drawdown at peak leverage, the top tercile is as likely to be the largest LOSS contributor (Mar-2020 mechanism) as the largest gain; the falsifier's own monotonic-tercile test (#2) and contemporaneous-d(VIX) test (#4) will likely FAIL in a cost-and-path-honest backtest. (2) The hedge/diversification pitch to the short-vol incumbent during crises — false in the deleveraging phase, true only post-snapback. (3) The 8bp net edge as a standalone return engine — too thin vs crowding and regime-correlated costs to be a return source; its only defensible value is marginal diversification, and even that is contingent on surviving the drawdown phase that the leverage+kill-switch rules make structurally hard for retail fixed capital. Net: mark survives=false as specified; the underlying premium is real but the spec's regime-timing (lever into VIX level, kill on drawdown) is anti-phased to its own convexity, so the candidate as written would have been hurt, not helped, by Q4-2018 and Mar-2020, and is fragile in any gappy 2022/Aug-2024 session.

name: Beta-Neutral Betting-Against-Beta (BAB) — large-cap-80 long-short
family: Factor investing via ETF proxies + cross-sectional factors (value/quality/low-vol/size/momentum tilts; long-short or long-tilt; orthogonal to the incumbent short-vol put-selling and to a single-stock momentum pick)
rationale: Real structural mechanism but applied to its weakest slice (compressed mega-cap betas); three Serious red-team kills (thin/coin-flip net edge, crash-correlation with the incumbent short-vol book in the tails, regime-net-short in V-recoveries), plus a verified look-ahead defect — betaQuote is 50% of the signal yet StrategyContext exposes no historical beta (types.ts:35-48), so the financed short leg does not earn its keep; revise down to the already-implemented long-only lowvol.ts tilt.
bestUseInEnsemble: Only the long-only low-beta core is usable in the book — it is already coded as lowvol.ts (longOnly, no borrow, no neutralization), captures most of the survivable leverage-constraint premium at half the cost, and serves as a mild defensive equity sleeve; the beta-neutral financed short leg should NOT be run as a stand-alone orthogonal diversifier because its calm-month low correlation to the put-selling curve (mechanically true: no vega) masks regime-level co-movement in funding shocks (2018-Q4, Mar-2020, Aug-2024), so it correlates with the incumbent exactly when diversification would matter, while paying full borrow plus granularity-degraded neutrality.

spec.name: Beta-Neutral Betting-Against-Beta (BAB) — large-cap-80 long-short
spec.family: Factor investing / cross-sectional risk premium (low-beta long-short; orthogonal to short-vol put-selling and to a single-stock momentum pick)
spec.mechanism: Leverage-constraint risk premium (Frazzini-Pedersen 2014). Leverage- and margin-constrained investors who want more market exposure than 1x cannot freely borrow, so they "reach for beta" — overpaying for high-beta names and shunning low-beta names. This bids the security market line flatter than CAPM predicts: high-beta is over-priced (low future alpha) and low-beta is under-priced (positive alpha) per unit of risk. Going LONG low-beta and SHORT high-beta, with each leg scaled so the dollar-beta of the long leg equals the dollar-beta of the short leg (portfolio ex-ante beta = 0), isolates and harvests that CAPM-residual alpha while neutralizing market direction. The mechanism is structural (funding/margin constraints persist post-publication), not a calendar artifact, which is why a modified multi-factor alpha (~75 bps/mo in the ORIGINAL broad/small-cap cross-section) survived publication. HONEST SCOPE CAVEAT baked into this spec: on an 80-name mega-cap optionable universe, cross-sectional beta dispersion is compressed (most names sit in ~0.7-1.6), so the realized large-cap BAB premium is a fraction of the headline — this is the weakest slice of the market for the anomaly, and the spec is graded and sized accordingly.
spec.hypothesisH0: On the PutStrike ~80-name large-cap universe, a beta-neutral long-low-beta / short-high-beta portfolio rebalanced monthly earns a net (post-cost, post-borrow) CAPM-alpha statistically indistinguishable from zero: the Newey-West t-stat on the intercept of (daily strategy return) regressed on (daily SPY excess return) is |t| < 2.0 over the 10y backtest, AND its net Sharpe ≤ that of an equal-weight long-only low-beta basket (i.e. the short leg and beta-neutralization add no value after costs).
spec.hypothesisH1: The beta-neutral portfolio earns a positive net CAPM-alpha (Newey-West t ≥ 2.0 on the intercept) with realized full-sample portfolio beta to SPY in [-0.15, +0.15], and a net annualized Sharpe ≥ 0.4, while its daily-return correlation to the incumbent short-vol put-selling equity curve (measured by curveCorrelation in src/lib/backtest/metrics.ts) is ≤ 0.3 in absolute value outside crash months.
spec.signalDefinition:
For each symbol s in the universe on rebalance date t, compute a blended beta estimate betaHat(s):
(1) betaQuote(s) = quote.beta for s from yahoo-finance.ts getStockData() (the Yahoo-supplied beta field; falls back to 1.0 when null — see yahoo-finance.ts:119).
(2) betaOLS(s) = beta(logReturns(closes_s)[-252:], logReturns(closes_SPY)[-252:]) using the existing OLS estimator beta() in src/lib/strategies/indicators.ts:162 (cov(stock,SPY)/var(SPY)) over the trailing 252 trading days of adjusted closes from the 10y series. NOTE: this is a 252d window, intentionally LONGER than the engine's existing rolling_beta_spy_60d iTransformer feature (itransformer-features.ts row[88], 60d), to reduce estimation noise; the 60d feature is too short for a portfolio-formation beta.
Blend: betaHat(s) = 0.5*betaQuote(s) + 0.5*betaOLS(s). If betaQuote is missing (==1.0 default sentinel AND quote.beta was null) use betaHat(s) = betaOLS(s).
Rank all N≈80 names by betaHat ascending. LONG set L = bottom 15 (lowest beta); SHORT set H = top 15 (highest beta). Raw leg weights: long name i gets wL_i ∝ 1/betaHat_i (cap betaHat floor at 0.3 to avoid blow-up); short name j gets wH_j ∝ betaHat_j. Normalize each leg to sum to 1 (gross 100% per side before neutralization). Beta-neutralize: let bL = Σ wL_i*betaHat_i (long-leg weighted beta), bH = Σ wH_j*betaHat_j (short-leg weighted beta). Scale legs so dollar-betas match: final long weight = +k * wL_i, final short weight = -(k*bL/bH) * wH_j, where k is the gross-leverage scalar (see positionSizing). Emit as signed TargetWeight[] (the Strategy.generate contract in strategies/types.ts) so portfolio ex-ante beta ≈ 0.
spec.entryRule: Daily EOD cadence but ACT only every rebalanceDays=21 (monthly); warmupBars=252 (need a full year for betaOLS). On a rebalance day: (a) recompute betaHat for all universe names with enough history; (b) form L/H sets and beta-neutral target weights as above; (c) trade the difference vs current holdings toward target. CONDITIONING OVERLAY (gross-exposure damper): read FRED financialStress = STLFSI4 and hySpread = BAMLH0A0HYM2 from src/lib/fred.ts. Compute 21-day change of each. If BOTH STLFSI4 is rising (Δ21 > 0 AND level > its trailing median) AND HY OAS is widening (Δ21 > 0), set the gross-leverage scalar k = 0.5 (halve gross) for that month; else k = 1.0. Rationale: BAB underperforms sharply when funding tightens and betas compress toward 1 (a documented conditioning variable); the overlay cuts exposure into exactly those regimes. (HARD DATA LIMITATION, stated for the falsifier: fred.ts currently fetches only ~14 months of FRED history (fred.ts:112), so the overlay is only backtestable on the most recent ~1y unless the FRED start date is widened — the core BAB signal needs no FRED and is fully 10y-backtestable; the overlay must be validated on its own shorter window or with a widened fetch.)
spec.exitRule: No discretionary exit; the strategy is continuously held and re-formed each 21-day rebalance — positions drift between rebalances (the engine does not re-trade daily). A name leaves a leg automatically when it falls out of the bottom-15 / top-15 betaHat rank at the next rebalance, or when it drops out of the universe (delisting / data failure handled per-symbol like the screener). Risk stop: if realized portfolio beta drifts outside [-0.25, +0.25] at any daily mark (recomputed from the last 21d), force an off-cycle re-neutralization (re-scale the short leg) the next EOD. Hard de-risk: if STLFSI4 spikes into the top decile of its available history, flatten to k=0 (cash) until it exits the top decile — protects the known BAB crash failure mode.
spec.instruments: Cash equity long-short on single names from the ~80 high-liquidity optionable large-cap universe: ~15 long (lowest-beta), ~15 short (highest-beta), beta-neutralized, monthly rebalance. No options → no assignment/vega/theta exposure (structurally orthogonal to the short-vol incumbent). Requires short-borrow on the high-beta leg (large-caps are easy-to-borrow; modeled at 50 bps/yr via CostModel.shortBorrowAnnualBps). Instrument class = "equity" in the Strategy contract. Recorded in the generalized simulated_trades / capital_events Neon schema (signed positions).
spec.positionSizing: Fixed starting capital C (e.g. $100k sim). Gross target = k * G with base gross G = 100% per side (long 100% of C, short 100% of C, i.e. ~2x gross, ~0 net) — k is the overlay scalar (1.0 normal, 0.5 in funding stress, 0 in crisis decile). Per-name cap: |weight| ≤ 8% of C so no single name dominates (≈ at most 2x the equal 1/15 ≈ 6.7% leg weight). Beta-neutralization scales the short leg by bL/bH so dollar-betas cancel. Risk cap: target annualized portfolio vol ≤ 8%; if the trailing-60d realized vol of the formed portfolio exceeds 8%, scale k down pro-rata (vol-targeting) — this keeps the long-short, which can have fat tails in dislocations, inside a fixed risk budget on fixed capital. Capital for the short leg is treated as fully reserved (Reg-T style 100% sim collateral) so the sim never assumes unfunded leverage.
spec.dataSources: All in-repo, free, daily: (1) yahoo-finance.ts getStockData → quote.beta (Yahoo beta field) + 10y daily adjusted-close OHLCV per name; (2) SPY 10y daily closes (same provider) as the market proxy for betaOLS; (3) src/lib/strategies/indicators.ts beta() + logReturns() (OLS beta estimator, already implemented and unit-tested); (4) src/lib/fred.ts financialStress (STLFSI4) + hySpread (BAMLH0A0HYM2) for the conditioning overlay; (5) src/lib/backtest/costs.ts CostModel (spread/slippage/borrow) + dailyBorrowCost(); (6) src/lib/backtest/metrics.ts computeMetrics() + curveCorrelation() for performance + the orthogonality test; (7) Strategy/StrategyContext contract in src/lib/strategies/types.ts (no-look-ahead, signed weights) for both backtest and the live daily runner; (8) Neon simulated_trades + capital_events for P&L tracking. No new infra, no paid data. The ONLY parameter not in any feed is the precise short-borrow rate — handled by the existing CostModel.shortBorrowAnnualBps (default 50 bps/yr, swept 30-100 bps in the cost-robustness check).
spec.capacityNote: Capacity is NOT the binding constraint — it is the edge. At retail/sim size ($100k-$1M) on 30 mega-cap names, market impact is negligible and the modeled 4-5 bps/side slippage is conservative; the strategy could scale to tens of millions before impact bites on these names. The binding constraint is EDGE THINNESS on this universe: BAB's premium lives in broad/small-cap beta dispersion, and the 80-name optionable mega-cap slice has compressed betas (most in 0.7-1.6), so the harvestable gross alpha here is a small fraction of the ~75 bps/mo headline — plausibly 25-45 bps/mo gross, i.e. ~12-22 bps/mo NET after the ~22 bps/trade all-in cost (10 bps round-trip equity friction on the ~50-60% monthly turnover + ~8 bps/mo borrow drag on the short leg + occasional hard-to-borrow spikes on the highest-beta meme names like TSLA). Net edge is real but THIN and regime-dependent; honestly this scores moderate, not high, on Edge — its value to the book is Orthogonality (a market-neutral, no-vega return stream that diversifies the short-vol theta engine), not standalone return. If forced to choose, an equal-weight long-only low-vol tilt captures most of the survivable premium with half the cost and no borrow — the short leg only earns its keep if the H0 t-test on the neutralized portfolio's alpha clears 2.0.
spec.falsifier: Run the walk-forward backtest (no look-ahead StrategyContext, 252d warmup, 21d rebalance, DEFAULT_COSTS incl. 50 bps/yr borrow) over the full 10y on the ~80-name universe and regress daily net strategy return on daily SPY excess return. THE STRATEGY IS FALSIFIED (drop it / demote to long-only low-vol tilt) if ANY of: (1) the Newey-West (≈21-lag) t-stat on the regression INTERCEPT (net alpha) is < 2.0 — i.e. no statistically reliable post-cost CAPM-alpha on this universe; (2) realized full-sample portfolio beta to SPY is outside [-0.20, +0.20] — the neutralization fails and it is a closet directional bet; (3) net annualized Sharpe < 0.4 after DEFAULT_COSTS, OR net Sharpe does not exceed an equal-weight long-only bottom-15-beta basket (the short leg + neutralization add no value after borrow); (4) under a borrow-cost sweep, raising shortBorrowAnnualBps from 50 to 100 bps flips cumulative net alpha negative (edge is inside the borrow assumption); (5) |curveCorrelation| between this strategy's equity curve and the incumbent short-vol put-selling curve EXCEEDS 0.5 in non-crash months (it is not orthogonal — just short-vol in disguise via the short-high-beta/short-growth tilt). Passing requires clearing ALL five; the most likely kill, stated honestly, is (1) — that on the compressed-beta mega-cap-80 universe the net alpha t-stat lands in 0.5-1.5, real but not significant.

redteam.lens: COSTS & CAPACITY (retail frictions: spread/borrow/granularity vs fixed capital; capacity)
redteam.kill: The net edge survives only at the optimistic corner of two assumptions, and the dominant cost (borrow) is mis-specified for this exact leg. Reconciling the spec to the repo's actual DEFAULT_COSTS (5 bps/side spread+slippage, 50 bps/yr borrow) on the real 2x-gross book (long 100%C + short 100%C, ~75-name universe, 15+15 traded): per-MONTH round-trip cost = friction + borrow = 11-20 bps (at 55-100% one-way turnover/side) + 4.2 bps borrow = 15-24 bps/mo. Gross edge claimed 25-45 bps/mo. So NET spans ~+0.8 bps/mo (gross 25, cost 24) to ~+22 (gross 35, cost 12) — i.e. it clears costs only if gross lands top-of-range AND turnover/borrow bottom-of-range. Two specifics make the pessimistic corner the realistic one: (1) BORROW IS THE BINDING COST AND IS WRONG FOR THE LEG. Web-confirmed mega-cap spreads are ~1-4 bps round-trip, so the spec's 3 bps/side is genuinely conservative — spread is NOT the attack. But the flat 50 bps/yr borrow is an easy-to-borrow rate applied to the HIGHEST-beta slice (TSLA and meme-adjacent names), which is precisely where episodic hard-to-borrow spikes (1-10%+/yr) occur. The spec's own falsifier (4) concedes raising borrow 50->100 bps could flip net alpha negative; at a plausible blended 150 bps/yr the gross-25 case is already negative (-0.5 bps/mo). (2) GRANULARITY BREAKS THE BETA-NEUTRALITY THE SHORT LEG IS SOLD ON. At fixed $100k, each name targets ~$6,667; whole-share shorts of high-priced names (AVGO ~$1700 = 25% of target; NVDA ~$1150) force ±2-12% weight wobble, and the engine's 0.2% dust filter ($200) adds a ~3% no-trade band per name. The exact dollar-beta cancellation that justifies the H1 [-0.15,+0.15] tolerance cannot be realized at $100k, so the short leg adds residual directional risk while still paying full borrow+friction. Independent literature corroboration: a value-weighted LARGE-CAP BAB earns ~56 bps/mo gross but has NO significant net alpha vs the FF5 model and transaction costs cut BAB profitability ~60% — and this mega-cap-80 universe (betas compressed to 0.7-1.6) is a strictly worse slice than that. Net: gross edge is NOT reliably larger than round-trip cost; it is a coin-flip that the spec's own falsifier predicts most-likely lands at t-stat 0.5-1.5 (indistinguishable from zero).
redteam.mitigation: Edge would survive this lens if: (a) borrow on the short leg were measured from actual locate quotes per name and the highest-beta hard-to-borrow names (TSLA-type) were excluded or capped, keeping blended borrow truly near 50 bps/yr; (b) the strategy ran at >=$1-5M so whole-share granularity error becomes <0.5% per name and beta-neutrality is actually achievable (capacity is not the constraint, so scaling up is free and HELPS here); (c) the gross premium were demonstrated, not assumed — i.e. the walk-forward backtest returns gross alpha at the top of the 25-45 bps/mo range with realized one-way turnover <=55%/side; (d) the spec fixed its unit/borrow inconsistency (it states '8 bps/mo borrow' = 96 bps/yr while DEFAULT_COSTS is 50 bps/yr = 4.2 bps/mo) so the cost build-up is internally coherent. Demoting to the long-only low-beta basket (the spec's own fallback, captured by lowvol.ts) removes the borrow cost and the granularity-driven neutrality failure entirely and keeps most of the survivable premium at half the cost.
redteam.residual: Thin and conditional, not robust. After this attack the most defensible net figure is ~5-15 bps/mo (0.6-1.8%/yr gross-of-nothing-else) ONLY under benign borrow and top-of-range gross; under realistic borrow on the high-beta short leg plus granularity-degraded neutrality at $100k it compresses toward 0 to +5 bps/mo and can go negative in any month a short name goes hard-to-borrow. The residual that IS real and survives is not standalone return but the long-only low-beta tilt (no borrow, no neutrality requirement, half the friction) — the short leg + beta-neutralization do not pay for their incremental borrow+granularity cost unless the H0 alpha t-test clears 2.0, which the spec itself flags as the most likely failure. Capacity is genuinely a non-issue (scales to tens of $M on these names), so it is not a residual concern. Net: keep as an orthogonality/diversification sleeve at size >=$1M if and only if the backtest clears the falsifier; do not bank on it as a cost-surviving return source at $100k.

redteam.lens: Data-snooping, decay & orthogonality: (a) is BAB a real mechanism or a curve-fit/arbitraged-away anomaly on this universe, and (b) is the stream orthogonal to short-vol put-selling or short-crash-risk in disguise?
redteam.kill: Both prongs fail. (a) DECAY + CONSTRUCTION-DEPENDENCE: BAB was published in 2010 (NBER w16601) / 2014 (JFE). Web-checked sources confirm post-publication decay — capital flowed into low-beta/defensive products ("low-beta stocks inevitably overbought"), beta dispersion compressed, and the leveraged construction is hurt by financing costs. Decisively, Novy-Marx & Velikov (2022 JFE, "Betting Against Betting Against Beta") show the headline ~0.69-0.75%/mo is largely an artifact of FP's non-standard rank-weighting that non-transparently equal-weights and over-commits to microcaps (~$1.05/$1 to the bottom 1% of market cap), plus incidental profitability/investment tilts. On an 80-name MEGA-CAP, name-weighted universe BOTH drivers are mechanically removed — there are no microcaps and no rank-weighting trick — so the spec harvests precisely the residue the literature finds small-to-insignificant. The spec concedes this ("weakest slice of the market," net 12-22 bps/mo) and its own most-likely-kill is falsifier (1): t-stat lands 0.5-1.5. (b) ORTHOGONALITY FAILS IN THE TAILS: the short leg is short-high-beta (TSLA/NVDA/semis/high-beta growth). Web-checked evidence shows short-vol put-selling and short-high-beta/long-low-beta both collect a skew/crash-risk premium and both blow up in the SAME funding-stress regimes (2008-09, Feb-2018 Volmageddon, Mar-2020). Frazzini-Pedersen themselves: BAB realizes negative returns exactly when funding tightens — the identical failure mode to short-vol. So calm-month |corr|<0.3 (mechanically true: no vega) hides regime-level co-movement; it is short-crash-risk in disguise where it matters. The spec's own crash-month carve-out and falsifier (5) tacitly admit this. Repo-verified: beta() at indicators.ts:162, curveCorrelation() at metrics.ts:98, shortBorrowAnnualBps:50, yahoo beta fallback at yahoo-finance.ts:119, and the FRED ~14-month fetch limit (fred.ts:111-113) means the stress overlay is only ~1y-backtestable — untested in exactly the crash regimes it claims to protect against.
redteam.mitigation: It would survive if: (1) the walk-forward Newey-West t on net CAPM-alpha clears 2.0 on THIS mega-cap-80 universe (spec itself rates this as the likely kill, expecting 0.5-1.5) AND survives the 50→100bps borrow sweep without flipping negative; (2) the short leg adds Sharpe over a long-only bottom-15-beta basket after borrow (spec admits long-only "captures most of the survivable premium with half the cost and no borrow"); AND (3) orthogonality is measured at the REGIME level, not just benign-month daily corr — e.g. demonstrating the strategy does not co-draw with the short-vol curve in 2018/2020-style funding shocks, with the FRED stress-damper widened to a full 10y backtest (currently impossible at fred.ts:111). Absent the tail-correlation test, the orthogonality claim is unfalsified, not validated.
redteam.residual: Thin and conditional. What survives the lens is a long-only low-volatility tilt (the slow-decaying, structurally-grounded slice of the leverage-constraint premium that needs no borrow and is not the Novy-Marx artifact). That residual is real but small (~single-digit bps/mo net) and is NOT orthogonal to the short-vol book in crashes. The beta-neutral long-short overlay — the actual thing being graded — adds little net edge after borrow and reintroduces short-crash-risk that correlates with the incumbent in the tails, so as a diversifier its claimed value (a no-vega orthogonal stream) is largely illusory when diversification would matter most. Net: demote to a small long-only low-vol tilt; do not run the financed short leg as an orthogonal diversifier on this universe.

redteam.lens: REGIME & ROBUSTNESS (regime dependence, parameter sensitivity, crowding, tail risk; survival across 2018-Q4, Mar-2020, 2022, Aug-2024)
redteam.kill: The edge is regime-conditional in exactly the direction that kills it during the named crises, and its only crisis defense is unbacktestable. (1) Short leg = wrong side of every V-shaped recovery. The short-high-beta basket on this mega-cap-80 universe is dominated by NVDA/TSLA/AMD/META/AVGO (universe.ts) — the precise names that led the Mar-2020 and post-2020 rallies. Web-corroborated: MSCI Min-Vol returned 2.6% in 2020 vs 15.9% market, "provided weaker protection during the March 2020 downturn than in previous crashes, then lagged through the subsequent bull run"; low-beta "never had a chance to materialize before it was overwhelmed by the speculatively charged rally." Beta-neutrality is ex-ante only — in a sharp rebound the long-low-beta/short-high-beta book runs net SHORT realized beta and bleeds. Aug-2024 (yen-carry unwind then 3-day snapback) and the 2018-Q4 -> Jan-2019 reversal are the same trap. (2) Documented "beta crashes": funding stress compresses cross-sectional betas toward 1, mechanically raising the BAB factor's OWN beta precisely as funding tightens, so losses cluster in stress (Frazzini-Pedersen; ScienceDirect "Market neutrality and beta crashes"). This is fat-left-tail/negative-skew behavior the 8% vol target and [-0.25,0.25] beta stop react to with a lag, not ahead of a gap. (3) The crisis overlay is not backtestable: fred.ts:111-114 fetches only ~14 months of FRED history, and the spec itself concedes this. So the gross-damper (k=0.5), the hard de-risk (flatten when STLFSI4 in top decile), and the STLFSI4-decile definition CANNOT be validated over 2018-Q4, Mar-2020, or 2022 — the only four regimes that matter for this lens. STLFSI4 is weekly and lagging; it would have flattened AFTER the Mar-2020 gap, not before. (4) Signal contamination compounds regime risk: the StrategyContext no-look-ahead contract (types.ts:35-48) exposes only bars/closes/regime — there is NO historical quote.beta accessor, yet betaQuote is 50% of the blend. In the 10y walk-forward, betaQuote is either unavailable or injects today's Yahoo beta into every past rebalance (look-ahead), biasing the ranking toward names that turned out low-beta. (5) Crowding: low-vol/BAB is one of the most-arbitraged anomalies; the spec's own honest caveat says the mega-cap-80 slice (betas compressed to 0.7-1.6) is "the weakest slice of the market for the anomaly," leaving 12-22 bps/mo NET — thin enough that one crisis-month beta-crash erases years of carry. The spec's own most-likely-kill falsifier (1) predicts t-stat lands at 0.5-1.5. Under this lens that thin, crowded, structurally-net-short-in-rebounds edge with an unbacktestable crisis guard does not survive.
redteam.mitigation: To survive this lens the spec would need: (a) widen the FRED fetch to the full 10y and DEMONSTRATE the overlay/de-risk actually cut losses in Mar-2020, 2018-Q4, and 2022 out-of-sample, not just assert it — and prove the weekly-lagged STLFSI4 fires before the gap, not after; (b) drop quote.beta from the signal entirely (use only the 252d betaOLS, which IS reproducible inside the no-look-ahead context) so the ranking is not contaminated; (c) report realized-beta and equity-curve behavior specifically inside each of the four crisis windows (and the immediate rebound months) to show the short leg does not detonate in snapbacks — ideally adding an explicit rebound/short-squeeze guard or capping single-name short weight on the highest-beta meme names; (d) characterize tail/skew (worst-month, left-tail CVaR, drawdown during 2020 V-recovery) and show the 8% vol target plus [-0.25,0.25] beta stop bound it ex-ante, not after a 1-day gap; (e) pass the borrow sweep AND a crowding/decay check (rolling 3y alpha) since the premium is heavily published and arbitraged. Even then, the structural net-short-beta-in-rebounds problem caps how much of the named-crisis risk can be removed.
redteam.residual: Thin and conditional, not zero. What plausibly survives is a long-only low-vol tilt (the spec's own fallback, and what lowvol.ts already implements) which captures most of the durable premium with no borrow and no short-leg rebound blow-up — but that is a directional defensive sleeve, not the market-neutral orthogonal return stream the H1/orthogonality thesis is selling. The beta-neutral long-short specifically adds (i) short-leg theta-of-momentum risk that crashes in V-recoveries (Mar-2020, Aug-2024, Jan-2019) and (ii) a crisis guard that is unvalidated over 9 of 10 years. After this attack the residual standalone edge of the SHORT leg + neutralization is at best 12-22 bps/mo NET in calm/bull regimes only, with episodic large negative-skew losses in exactly the crisis/rebound regimes the lens asks about. Net: the directional low-vol core has a defensible (if crowded) residual edge; the beta-neutral overlay's incremental edge does not survive the regime/tail lens until the crisis-window backtests are produced.

name: Credit-Stress Defensive Sector Tilt (HYG/TLT + STLFSI4 + T10Y3M gated rotation: long XLP/XLU/XLV vs XLY/XLK/XLI)
family: Macro-regime sector/style rotation (FRED credit/curve/stress + breadth risk-on/off)
rationale: Fatal regime finding (hedge is structurally absent in the fast vol-led gaps — Mar-2020, Aug-2024 — that define a put-seller's worst months, where defensives correlate to 1 and the weekly cadence + FRED publish lags whipsaw) caps the score; verified in code that the load-bearing FRED gates and falsifiers are unrunnable (fred.ts fetches ~14 months with no lag-shift, no FRED plumbing in scripts/backtest, curveCorrelation is unconditional full-sample only and no put-selling P&L curve exists), so the "all existing infra, nothing new" claim is false and the standalone edge sits on its own falsification line (~1.3-1.6x cost after borrow + whipsaw).
bestUseInEnsemble: Only as a cheap, long-only (gross 1.0x, no borrow) slow-credit-bear defensive tilt — XLP/XLU/XLV over XLY/XLK/XLI gated on HYG/TLT + breadth — sized as accepted-negative-carry insurance, NOT as an orthogonal alpha; it could add value to a daily ensemble in 2018Q4/2022-style credit grinds but must NOT be relied on to hedge gap crashes, and the FRED-timing overlay should be dropped until a real ALFRED-vintage, lag-shifted loader and a conditional worst-decile tail-correlation metric are built.

spec.name: Credit-Stress Defensive Sector Tilt (HYG/TLT + STLFSI4 + T10Y3M gated rotation: long XLP/XLU/XLV vs XLY/XLK/XLI)
spec.family: Macro-regime sector/style rotation (credit + curve + financial-stress + breadth risk-on/off tilt)
spec.mechanism: Recession/default-risk premium harvested via a credit-to-equity repricing LAG, not cross-sectional momentum and not short-vol. High-yield credit spreads and the St. Louis Fed Financial Stress Index are forward-looking prices of default/liquidity risk that empirically lead equity drawdowns by weeks-to-months (Gilchrist-Zakrajsek EBP; Faust-Gilchrist-Wright-Zakrajsek). Credit desks reprice default risk faster than benchmark-anchored, flow-sticky sector-ETF allocations move. So when the CHANGE in HY spread turns up through a threshold WHILE financial stress rises AND the 10y-3m curve is flat/inverted, equity beta is about to be repriced: high-beta cyclicals (XLY/XLK/XLI) underperform low-beta defensives (XLP/XLU/XLV). The edge is the few-week lag between credit pricing risk and sector ETFs catching up. Crucially the return stream is LONG realized-vol / crash-protective: it pays in exactly the spread-blowout months when short-vol (the incumbent put-selling book) bleeds, so it is structurally a hedge, not a correlated bet. Honest caveat baked into the spec: the durable, literature-supported component is the slow defensive TILT in a credit/stress/curve regime; the high-frequency 'credit-timing alpha' is thin and the load-bearing risk is that this collapses into a low-Sharpe beta-rotation that only earns its keep in tail months.
spec.hypothesisH0: In a walk-forward backtest (2007-present) on SPDR sector ETFs with PutStrike's DEFAULT_COSTS (6 bps round-trip ETF + 50 bps/yr borrow on shorts), the dollar-neutral leg (long defensives XLP/XLU/XLV minus short cyclicals XLY/XLK/XLI), conditioned ONLY on RiskOff>=3 days, has a net mean forward 21-trading-day return <= 0 (one-sided), AND its daily-return correlation to a 30-45 DTE SPY/index put-selling P&L curve is >= 0 (i.e. it provides no diversification). Equivalently: the defensive-minus-cyclical spread net edge per rebalance is statistically indistinguishable from zero after costs.
spec.hypothesisH1: When RiskOff>=3 (credit-spread change up + financial-stress rising + curve flat/inverted + breadth confirming), the forward 21d net return of long-defensives-minus-short-cyclicals is > 0 with a positive, economically meaningful gross edge that survives the 6 bps/leg + borrow cost, AND the strategy's daily P&L correlation to a short-vol/put-selling curve (via curveCorrelation in src/lib/backtest/metrics.ts) is <= 0 in the full sample and strongly negative in the worst-decile equity months for the put book — confirming it is an orthogonal/hedging diversifier to the incumbent.
spec.signalDefinition:
All inputs are existing PutStrike data; compute at EOD with strict date<=t (engine enforces no-lookahead). Define a 0-4 RiskOff score from four gates, each using named features/series:
GATE A (credit, Yahoo real-time proxy — NO FRED lag risk): let R = HYG/TLT ratio. creditSpreadChange20 = (R_t - R_{t-20})/R_{t-20} (this is feature row[113] credit_spread_change_20d). hygMomZ = zscore(simpleReturns(HYG_closes)[-... ], 252) of the 20d HYG/TLT log-return over a 1y window. Gate A = 1 if creditSpreadChange20 < 0 (HY underperforming Treasuries = spreads widening) AND hygMomZ < -1.0 (HYG/TLT 20d return below -1 SD of its 1y distribution).
GATE B (financial stress, FRED, LAG-SHIFTED): stressMom = fred_financial_stress(STLFSI4) 4-week change using values shifted by +5 trading days to respect the Thursday weekly publication lag (see falsifier). Gate B = 1 if lagged STLFSI4_t - STLFSI4_{t-20d-lag} > 0.
GATE C (curve, FRED T10Y3M, lag-shifted +1 trading day for T+1 publish): Gate C = 1 if fred_t10y3m_spread_lagged < 0.25 (percentage points).
GATE D (equity breadth confirmation, Yahoo, zero-lag): riskAppetite20 = log(XLY_t/XLY_{t-20}) - log(XLP_t/XLP_{t-20}) (feature row[153] risk_appetite_ratio_20d / 'XLY vs XLP 20d spread'); rsSlope = sign of the 10-day change in the 50d XLY/XLP relative-strength ratio; iwmSpy20 = totalReturn(IWM,20) - totalReturn(SPY,20). Gate D = 1 if riskAppetite20 < 0 AND rsSlope < 0 AND iwmSpy20 < 0.
RiskOff = A+B+C+D (0..4). Also compute sectorBreadthBullish = row[161] for a veto only (if breadth>=0.8, i.e. broad uptrend, cap defensive tilt — avoids fighting a strong tape).
spec.entryRule:
Rebalance weekly on the first trading day after a full EOD close (Strategy.rebalanceDays=5, instrument='etf', warmupBars=300 to cover 252d z-score + lag). Emit signed target weights (TargetWeight[]) on fixed capital E:
- RiskOff>=3 (DEFENSIVE): long sleeve = +1/3 E each in XLP, XLU, XLV; short/underweight sleeve = -1/3 E each in XLY, XLK, XLI -> dollar-neutral overlay, gross 2.0x, net ~0. Long-only variant for accounts without shorting: 100% E split across XLP/XLU/XLV, 0% cyclicals.
- RiskOff==2 (NEUTRAL): flat (all weights 0) OR hold equal-weight SPY-proxy; no active tilt.
- RiskOff<=1 (RISK-ON): reverse sign — long +1/3 E each XLY/XLK/XLI, short -1/3 E each XLP/XLU/XLV (long-only variant: 100% across XLY/XLK/XLI).
Veto: if sectorBreadthBullish(row[161]) >= 0.8, force at most NEUTRAL (no full defensive) — do not fully fight a broad uptrend. All entries are ETF market-on-close fills costed at DEFAULT_COSTS.
spec.exitRule: Pure state-machine: positions are fully re-derived from RiskOff each weekly rebalance; no separate stop/target. A position is exited/flipped when the score crosses a band boundary (>=3, ==2, <=1) at the weekly check. Hard de-risk overrides: (1) if ctx.regime.regime=='CRISIS' (VIX>~35) AND RiskOff has already fallen back to <=1 for 2 consecutive weeks, flatten (the lead is spent; mean-reversion risk). (2) Time-based: if a DEFENSIVE state persists >12 weeks with no new spread-widening impulse (creditSpreadChange20 back above 0 for 3 straight weeks), decay tilt to NEUTRAL to avoid paying borrow indefinitely on a stale signal. No intraday exits (daily EOD cadence constraint).
spec.instruments: SPDR Select Sector ETFs only (penny-wide spreads, no assignment risk): long XLP, XLU, XLV; short/underweight XLY, XLK, XLI. Held as a dollar-neutral-ish overlay (gross 2x, net ~0) or a long-only defensive sleeve. XLU is fetched via the sectorEtfs map already wired in macro inputs (XLK/XLF/XLV/XLE/XLI/XLY/XLP/XLC/XLB/XLU/XLRE per itransformer-features.ts line 196); XLP/XLY are first-class macro inputs. Optional lower-turnover expression: SPY-vs-RSP or a low-vol ETF (SPLV) if added to the universe. ETFs chosen explicitly to maximize cost robustness — this strategy must NOT touch single options.
spec.positionSizing: Fixed-capital, vol-targeted, risk-capped. Base gross exposure 2.0x E in the dollar-neutral state (six legs at 1/3 E each, three long three short); long-only variant gross 1.0x. Scale the whole book by targetVol/realizedVol where targetVol=8% annualized and realizedVol = 20d annualized vol of the long-minus-short spread (annualizedVol() helper), capped at 1.0x leverage multiplier (never lever up, only down). Hard risk cap: per-rebalance max loss budget = 1.5% of E; if the 20d spread vol implies a 1-day 2-sigma move > 1.5% of E, shrink gross until it fits. Short borrow (50 bps/yr, dailyBorrowCost) is charged on the short notional — a real drag the sizing must clear. No position exceeds 1/3 E gross per ETF. Capital-aware via the existing fixed-E weight engine (weights are fractions of equity).
spec.dataSources: ALL existing PutStrike infra, nothing new: yahoo-finance2 daily closes for HYG, TLT, XLP, XLU, XLV, XLY, XLK, XLI, SPY, IWM, QQQ, ^VIX/^VIX3M/^VIX9D (regime snapshot). FRED via src/lib/fred.ts: STLFSI4 (financialStress), T10Y3M (t10y3mSpread), BAMLH0A0HYM2 (hySpread, used only for offline validation of the HYG/TLT proxy). Existing engineered features reused verbatim: row[113] credit_spread_change_20d, row[111] hyg_return_20d, row[114] hyg_spy_divergence, row[153] risk_appetite_ratio_20d (XLY-XLP), row[161] sector_breadth_bullish, row[144] fred_financial_stress, row[145] fred_t10y3m_spread, row[165] financial_stress_momentum. Indicator helpers: zscore, totalReturn, simpleReturns, annualizedVol, beta, correlation (src/lib/strategies/indicators.ts). Cost model: DEFAULT_COSTS (src/lib/backtest/costs.ts). Orthogonality measured with curveCorrelation (src/lib/backtest/metrics.ts) against a put-selling equity curve. CRITICAL DATA HAZARD: src/lib/fred.ts forward-fills FRED series to the daily calendar but does NOT lag-shift for publication delay; STLFSI4 is weekly (Thu release) and BAMLH0A0HYM2/T10Y3M publish ~T+1. The spec REQUIRES applying an explicit +5td (weekly) / +1td (daily) lag to all FRED gates, and treats the Yahoo HYG/TLT ratio as the real-time tradable substitute for the monthly-lagged FRED HY series.
spec.capacityNote: Effectively unlimited for any retail/small-fund book. The six instruments (XLP/XLU/XLV/XLY/XLK/XLI) are among the most liquid ETFs on earth ($1B+ ADV each, sub-cent-to-1c spreads); a retail-to-low-eight-figure account fills at mid with negligible impact. Turnover is intrinsically low: weekly rebalance but the score only flips bands a handful of times per year (regime signals are persistent), so realized annual turnover is modest (estimate 4-10x gross/yr) and most weeks emit zero trades. The binding constraint is NOT capacity but signal frequency: the strategy is in a non-neutral tilt maybe 25-40% of the time and only earns its tail premium in the few credit-stress episodes per decade, so the sample of 'real' firings is small (the core statistical risk, not a liquidity risk). Borrow on shorts (XLY/XLK/XLI all easy-to-borrow, ~50 bps/yr) is trivial.
spec.falsifier: Run the walk-forward backtest 2007-01-01 to present with DEFAULT_COSTS and the LAG-SHIFTED FRED gates. The strategy is FALSIFIED and must be discarded if ANY of the following hold: (1) EDGE: the net (post-6bps + borrow) mean forward-21d return of the long-defensives-minus-short-cyclicals spread, conditioned on RiskOff>=3, is <= 0 OR not statistically > 0 at p<0.10 across the firing sample (Newey-West t on overlapping 21d returns); i.e. realized net edge < ~10-15 bps/rebalance against the 22 bps cost. (2) ORTHOGONALITY (the load-bearing axis): curveCorrelation between this strategy's daily equity curve and a 30-45 DTE SPY put-selling curve is >= 0 over the full sample, OR is not negative during the worst-decile drawdown months of the put book — if it does not hedge the incumbent in tails, its main reason to exist is gone. (3) LOOKAHEAD ARTIFACT: re-running with the FRED publication lag REMOVED materially improves results (net Sharpe rises by >0.3), proving the apparent edge was leaking unpublished STLFSI4/T10Y3M data rather than tradable signal. (4) DECOMPOSITION: if dropping the two FRED gates (B,C) and using only the Yahoo HYG/TLT + breadth gates (A,D) destroys the edge, the strategy depends on lag-fragile macro data and is not robustly implementable. Passing requires net edge > cost (1), negative incumbent correlation (2), AND robustness to the lag (3) and to FRED removal (4).

redteam.lens: COSTS & CAPACITY (realistic retail frictions: spread, slippage, commission, borrow, assignment, min-granularity vs fixed capital; turnover; capacity)
redteam.kill: The gross edge is not comfortably larger than round-trip cost once the 6-leg, gross-2.0x dollar-neutral structure and borrow are accounted for on the engine's own accounting (weights are fractions of equity E; tradeCost = spreadBpsPerSide+slippageBpsPerSide = 3+2 = 5 bps/side on traded notional per costs.ts/engine.ts). A flat->defensive entry trades 6 x (1/3 E) = 2.0 E of notional = 10 bps of E; the round-trip is ~20 bps of E, and a sign-REVERSAL (RiskOff>=3 defensive -> RiskOff<=1 risk-on, which the spec explicitly does) flips all six legs and trades 4.0 E = 20 bps one-way / ~40 bps round-trip. Against expectedEdgeBpsPerTrade=35 (read as 35 bps of E over the 21d window on the same 2.0x book), a 20 bps round-trip leaves ~15 bps net -- which sits exactly ON the spec's own falsification threshold ('net edge < ~10-15 bps/rebalance => FALSIFIED'). Add borrow: short notional = 3 x 1/3 E = 1.0 E at 50 bps/yr; a defensive episode held the spec's stated up-to-12 weeks (~0.23 yr) costs ~11.5 bps of E, dropping net to ~3.5 bps -- statistically indistinguishable from zero. Annualized, the spec's own '4-10x gross/yr' turnover at 5 bps/side is 40-100 bps/yr of spread+slippage drag plus ~15 bps/yr borrow (short ~1.0 E for the admitted 25-40% of the year), i.e. ~55-115 bps/yr of cost, while the gross edge is concentrated in 'a few credit-stress episodes per decade' (2-4 firings x ~35 bps). A weekly 0-4 state machine with four gates sitting near their thresholds will whipsaw across the ==2 NEUTRAL band in choppy/head-fake regimes (2011, 2015-16, 2018Q4), each marginal flip paying 10-20 bps of E for zero captured 21d edge. So the edge is only ~1.3-1.6x round-trip cost at best and goes negative under the realistic (upper-turnover + multi-week-hold + whipsaw) case. Note: the per-unit frictions are correctly modeled and the cost model is fairly conservative; the kill is the EDGE/COST RATIO, not a mispriced spread.
redteam.mitigation: Survival would require: (1) reporting expectedEdgeBps explicitly as a fraction of E on the gross-2.0x book AND demonstrating realized net (post-spread+slippage+borrow) > ~25-30 bps/firing with Newey-West p<0.10, i.e. clearing cost by a real multiple, not sitting on the 10-15 bps falsification line; (2) hard turnover/whipsaw control -- a hysteresis band (require RiskOff to cross e.g. >=3 to enter but only <=1 to exit, not symmetric 2-boundary chatter) and a minimum-holding / no-flip-on-marginal-score-move rule, with the backtest REPORTING realized annual turnover (the 4-10x range is an unverified assumption, and the whole thesis flips on which end is true); (3) measuring borrow as realized drag per episode given actual hold durations, not assuming it is 'trivial'; (4) preferring the long-only defensive sleeve (gross 1.0x, no borrow, ~half the round-trip notional) or the lower-turnover SPY-vs-RSP / SPLV expression the spec mentions, which materially improves the edge/cost ratio; (5) since the documented value is the tail-hedge property, sizing/justifying it as a hedge whose negative-carry in calm regimes is an accepted insurance premium rather than claiming a standalone positive net edge per trade.
redteam.residual: Yes, but narrow and conditional. The instrument selection genuinely defeats the classic retail-options cost attack: penny-wide spreads (XLV ~$148 => ~0.7 bps/side; XLP/XLU/XLY/XLK/XLI similar), $1B+ ADV, no assignment, no option commission, no min-contract-granularity problem (fixed-E fractional-weight engine), and capacity is truly effectively unlimited for retail-to-low-eight-figures with negligible impact -- the capacityNote is correct and the cost MODEL (5 bps/side) is appropriately conservative. The residual real edge is the TAIL-HEDGE / orthogonality property (long realized-vol payoff in spread-blowout months when the put-selling incumbent bleeds); in the 2-4 genuine credit-stress firings per decade the ~35 bps gross plausibly clears the ~20 bps round-trip with room to spare, so as a crash hedge it can be net-positive in exactly the months that matter. What does NOT survive on this lens is the claim of a standalone, statistically-significant positive net edge PER TRADE across the firing sample after realistic borrow and upper-bound turnover -- that margin is razor-thin (~1.3-1.6x cost), assumption-dependent (4x vs 10x turnover, hold duration, whipsaw), and lands on the spec's own falsification boundary, so under default skepticism it is marked not surviving.

redteam.lens: DATA-SNOOPING, DECAY & ORTHOGONALITY
redteam.kill: Two independent kills. (a) DECAY/MECHANISM: the long leg harvests the defensive/low-vol premium, a published, crowded, partly-explained-away factor — Fama-French/Novy-Marx absorb it into profitability/investment and post-2010 low-vol ETF inflows compressed the valuation discount (textbook post-publication decay). The credit-lead overlay rests on Gilchrist-Zakrajsek EBP, which is a real RECESSION/real-activity signal, NOT a documented tradable defensive-minus-cyclical sector alpha; the only intermarket evidence found argues the OPPOSITE direction (defensive equity leadership leads credit spreads), gutting the 'credit ETFs lag by weeks' core claim. The spec's own caveat concedes the high-frequency credit-timing alpha is 'thin' and the book 'collapses into a low-Sharpe beta-rotation that only earns its keep in tail months.' (b) ORTHOGONALITY-AS-DISGUISE: long-defensive-minus-short-cyclical is a long-low-vol/short-high-beta convergence trade. Its negative correlation to a put-selling book in tails is achieved BY CONSTRUCTION as conditional short-beta crash protection — the same risk-off factor every crash hedge loads. That is correlated-to-short-vol-by-sign (sign-flipped beta), not an orthogonal, independently-positive-EV stream. A book that only pays in the few spread-blowout months and bleeds borrow + low-Sharpe drift the rest of the time is short-vol's mirror image, not a diversifier with its own edge. (c) FALSIFIABILITY/SNOOPING-CONTROL FAILURE: the data-snooping guardrails the spec leans on cannot actually be run on existing infra. Verified in code: scripts/backtest/data.ts loads only Yahoo price bars and engine.ts AlignedData carries only closes/bars/vix/vix9d — there is ZERO FRED plumbing in the backtest path (grep for STLFSI4/T10Y3M/FRED across scripts/ returns nothing). src/lib/fred.ts is a live-API client that fetches only ~14 months and never lag-shifts. So GATE B (STLFSI4), GATE C (T10Y3M) and falsifiers #3 (lag-removal lookahead test) and #4 (FRED-drop decomposition) are NOT executable without new infra — flatly contradicting the spec's repeated 'ALL existing PutStrike infra, nothing new.' An anti-snooping protocol you cannot execute does not protect against snooping.
redteam.mitigation: To survive: (1) Build the missing infra — a historical FRED loader (full-history ALFRED vintages, not the 14-month live endpoint) wired into AlignedData with explicit publication-lag shifting, so falsifiers #3 and #4 can actually run; until then the lookahead control is vaporware. (2) Pre-register a single decision and run it once: decompose into (i) static long-defensive/short-cyclical beta-rotation and (ii) the marginal credit/stress TIMING overlay, and demonstrate via a spanning regression that the overlay's alpha is positive AFTER controlling for SPY beta, a low-vol factor (e.g. SPLV-SPY or BAB), and a short-vol/put-write P&L curve — i.e. prove edge is orthogonal in a regression, not merely negative-signed in tails. (3) Show the timing component survives on a post-2012 (post-EBP-publication) out-of-sample slice with net edge > cost at p<0.10 on the small firing sample with Newey-West overlap correction AND a deflated/multiplicity-aware threshold (four gates + thresholds + vetoes = a large search space; the 35 bps/22 bps numbers are unjustified ex-ante). (4) Re-frame honestly as a crash-hedge overlay sized for tail protection, dropping the 'orthogonal diversifier with standalone positive EV' claim, OR prove standalone Sharpe in non-tail regimes.
redteam.residual: Yes, a thin and conditional one. The slow DEFENSIVE TILT in a credit/stress/curve regime is the literature-supported, durable component — but as a CRASH-HEDGE OVERLAY, not as an orthogonal alpha stream. Residual value: it does plausibly pay in the handful of spread-blowout months per decade (2008, 2011, 2018Q4, 2020, 2022) when a short-vol/put-write book bleeds, so it has genuine tail-hedge utility for a put-selling incumbent. But that residual is (i) negative-beta crash insurance, i.e. correlated short-vol in disguise rather than diversifying alpha, (ii) likely negative or near-zero net-of-cost+borrow expectancy in the 60-75% of time it is non-defensive (the spec concedes 'low-Sharpe beta-rotation'), and (iii) decayed/crowded on the long-low-vol leg. After this attack the edge survives only as 'cheap-ish conditional crash protection,' not as the claimed orthogonal, standalone-positive-EV, statistically-significant credit-timing strategy. And even that residual is unverifiable in the current harness because the FRED gates carrying the 'forward-looking' content are not implementable in the stated backtest.

redteam.lens: REGIME & ROBUSTNESS (regime dependence, parameter sensitivity, crowding, tail risk; survival across 2018-Q4 / Mar-2020 / 2022 / Aug-2024)
redteam.kill: The entire value proposition — an orthogonal crash hedge for a put-selling book — rests on a credit-leads-equity lag that only exists in SLOW, credit-led drawdowns (2008, 2018-Q4, 2022) and is ABSENT or NEGATIVE in the fast, vol/leverage-led gaps (Mar-2020, Aug-2024) where a put-seller actually bleeds. Web-confirmed: in Aug-2024 the carry/vol shock had credit LAG the equity drop and round-trip in 1-3 days, so RiskOff fires (if at all) only after the snapback via a weekly rebalance + STLFSI4 +5td / T10Y3M +1td publication lags — a whipsaw LOSS in the exact tail month the spec sells it as protection. Mar-2020 is the same failure milder: the 30% drop in ~16 trading days outruns the weekly cadence and the 'few-week' lead compresses to days, and XLU/XLP correlated to 1 in the mid-March liquidity scramble so 'defensive' gave little protection in the worst week and risked flipping defensive into the late-March V-bottom (XLK led the recovery). So the hedge is structurally missing in precisely the gap-tails it's advertised to hedge. Compounding this: the orthogonality falsifier is NOT TESTABLE with existing infra (curveCorrelation in metrics.ts computes only unconditional full-sample Pearson; there is no put-selling P&L curve anywhere in scripts/, and no worst-decile conditional-correlation machinery), and in gap crashes cross-asset correlation goes to 1, so the tail correlation is plausibly POSITIVE exactly where the spec demands it be 'strongly negative.' The 'all existing infra, nothing new' claim is false on the load-bearing axis.
redteam.mitigation: To survive: (a) add a vol-led-gap override (e.g., VIX9D/VIX or 1-day VIX-spike gate) that SUPPRESSES new defensive entries when the move is a leverage shock rather than a credit grind, accepting the strategy only targets slow credit-led bears; (b) actually build the missing put-selling P&L curve plus a CONDITIONAL worst-decile tail-correlation metric and demonstrate negative correlation in the put book's worst months specifically, not just full-sample; (c) fix src/lib/fred.ts (currently forward-fills with NO publication-lag shift and fetches only 14 months of history — it cannot run the claimed 2007-present backtest, and the mandated +5td/+1td lags do not exist in code) and prove the edge survives FRED removal (falsifier 4) on out-of-sample gap AND slow-bear episodes; (d) replace the hard threshold cliffs (hygMomZ<-1.0, T10Y3M<0.25, RiskOff bands at 3/2/1) with hysteresis/smoothed bands to kill weekly sign-flip whipsaw near boundaries.
redteam.residual: Yes, but narrow and regime-contingent, not the all-weather hedge advertised. A genuine slow-defensive TILT in credit/curve/stress regimes survives — it would have worked well in 2018-Q4 and 2022 (web-confirmed: XLP -0.83% vs deep XLK losses in 2022). But the residual is a low-Sharpe, crowded (textbook long-staples/short-cyclicals macro trade, heavily front-run so the few-week lag alpha is compressed), statistically underpowered tilt: non-neutral only 25-40% of the time with a handful of real firings per decade (~5-8 independent episodes, dominated by the easy slow-bear regimes), which is too thin to clear the p<0.10 Newey-West bar after 22bps cost. It is a modest defensive beta-rotation for slow credit-led drawdowns — useful but NOT an orthogonal tail hedge against the gap events that define a put-seller's worst months, which is its stated reason to exist.

name: XS-RankLS: Concordance-Gated Cross-Sectional iTransformer+Ensemble Long/Short (dollar-neutral, beta-hedged)
family: ML-forecast-driven directional trading: the iTransformer 60d path + 6-model ensemble + existing concordance gate are used as a confidence filter / cross-sectional ranker on top of documented base anomalies (cross-sectional & time-series momentum), NOT as standalone alpha. Directional exposure is taken in beta-hedged equity (dollar-neutral) or defined-risk option structures, only when model_confidence is high and the two model families concord.
rationale: Fatal orthogonality failure verified in code: the L/S basket is short-gamma-in-disguise (momentum-crash convexity survives beta-hedging) and the falsifier's own curveCorrelation (metrics.ts L98-126) is full-sample Pearson that provably cannot see the left-tail co-movement with the short-vol book; compounded by no persisted forecast panel (hf-model.ts L177 returns false server-side, so currently unfalsifiable), in-sample model_confidence (L415-417) that decays toward 0.50 OOS, and a ~20bps headline eaten to ~5-12bps by every-cycle borrow + short-leg drag — below its own t>=2 bar.
bestUseInEnsemble: Do not add the dollar-neutral beta-hedged L/S as specified — it carries a shared short-gamma left tail that would CONCENTRATE, not diversify, crash risk on top of the incumbent put-selling book in a vol-spike/quant-unwind. The only salvageable fragment is a long-only large-cap under-reaction/momentum sleeve (literature places the alpha on the long side), which removes the every-cycle borrow, half the round-trip, and the short-leg drag — but even that is thin, crowded, post-decay, and must first prove an OOS edge on a point-in-time panel that does not yet exist and against a tail-conditioned (not full-sample Pearson) orthogonality test.

spec.name: XS-RankLS: Concordance-Gated Cross-Sectional iTransformer+Ensemble Long/Short (dollar-neutral, beta-hedged)
spec.family: ML-forecast-driven cross-sectional equity long/short (relative-rank, NOT absolute path): iTransformer 60d path + 6-model statistical ensemble + concordance gate used as a multi-feature CROSS-SECTIONAL RANKER on top of the documented cross-sectional/time-series momentum (under-reaction) premium.
