Directional exposure taken in beta-hedged, dollar-neutral cash equity. Orthogonal-by-construction to the incumbent short-vol/put-selling (theta / vol-risk-premium) book.
spec.mechanism: Economic mechanism is the cross-sectional & time-series momentum / under-reaction risk premium (Jegadeesh-Titman; Moskowitz-Ooi-Pedersen; Hurst/AQR trend nets ~0.4-0.7 Sharpe after costs, persists ~12m then reverses). A 50-58% directional model has NO defensible ABSOLUTE use, but its RELATIVE ordering survives: idiosyncratic per-stock forecast errors partially diversify across an ~16-name book, and the strongest ML equity result in the literature (Rossi; Frankfurt-School real-time ML cross-section) is ensemble RANK correlation, not path accuracy. The iTransformer+ensemble act as a smarter, regime/credit/flow-aware ranking signal than naive 12-1 price momentum because they ingest 137 features (regime: Hurst/Parkinson/Garman-Klass; credit: HYG/TLT, FRED HY OAS; flow: FINRA short-vol, Wikipedia attention; rates/FX). Shorting SPY/QQQ to zero out residual book beta strips the market-direction component, leaving pure cross-sectional dispersion of beta-neutralized single-name drift — which has ~0 structural correlation to short-vol P&L (driven by realized-vs-implied vol + theta on a net-long-delta book). The hedge explicitly removes the one factor (market direction) that CSP P&L is most exposed to.
spec.hypothesisH0: H0: On the ~80-name trained universe, a dollar-neutral, SPY-beta-hedged long-short basket formed daily from the composite rank R (top-8 long vs bottom-8 short), held with hysteresis rebalancing, earns a long-minus-short return spread that is <= the round-trip transaction + borrow cost charged by the engine's DEFAULT_COSTS model. Equivalently: net-of-cost mean spread per 21-trading-day holding period <= 0 bps (t-stat < 2), AND the strategy's daily-return correlation to the incumbent short-vol equity curve is statistically indistinguishable from the correlation a random dollar-neutral basket would show. The rank carries no exploitable cross-sectional dispersion beyond costs.
spec.hypothesisH1: H1: The R-ranked top-minus-bottom octile spread is positive and survives the engine's DEFAULT_COSTS (>= ~20 bps net per 21d holding period, t-stat >= 2 over a >= 100-rebalance walk-forward sample), is MONOTONE across rank quantiles (Q8 long > ... > Q1 short, i.e. the signal is a true ordering not a single-name fluke), the concordance gate (concordance != 'discordant' AND model_confidence >= 0.55) measurably improves the long-leg net spread vs the ungated rank, and the realized daily-return correlation of the beta-hedged L/S equity curve to the incumbent short-vol curve is |rho| <= 0.2 (curveCorrelation, backtest/metrics.ts).
spec.signalDefinition:
Per trained symbol s (s in getTrainedSymbols(), ~80 names), each EOD t:
1. iTransformer leg: itf = runHFInference(s); r21_itf(s) = itf.forecast_returns[29]  (cumulative predicted return at trading-day 30 ≈ the engine's canonical '30d prediction', exactly as ConcordanceCard uses forecast_returns[29]).
2. Ensemble leg: ens = generatePrediction(s); e(s) = ens.ensembleScore / 100  (in [-1,1]).
3. Cross-sectional z-scores ACROSS the universe at t: z_itf(s) = (r21_itf(s) - mean_s r21_itf) / sd_s r21_itf ; z_ens(s) = (e(s) - mean_s e) / sd_s e.
4. Composite rank score: R(s) = z_itf(s) + z_ens(s).
5. Confidence gate: conf(s) = itf.model_confidence  (= clamp(dir_acc_30d/100, 0, 1), per-stock from HF model_config).
6. Concordance gate: conc(s) in {concordant, partial, discordant} computed exactly as ConcordanceCard: dir_itf = sign-band(forecast_returns[29]*100, ±0.5%), dir_ens = sign-band((ens.predictions[daysOut=30].predicted/price - 1)*100, ±0.5%); concordant if dir_itf==dir_ens, discordant if both non-neutral and opposite, else partial.
7. Beta: b(s) = quote.beta (yahoo-finance getStockQuote, field .beta; default 1.0 if null).
LONG set L = the up-to-8 names with the highest R, restricted to R(s)>0 AND conf(s)>=0.55 AND conc(s)!='discordant'. SHORT set S = the up-to-8 names with the lowest R, restricted to R(s)<0 AND conf(s)>=0.55. (Short side intentionally NOT concordance-gated to keep the book balanced and dollar-neutral; the long-side concordance gate is the tested alpha lever.)
spec.entryRule: Daily EOD (no intraday). Target gross ~2x capital, dollar-neutral: long notional = short single-name notional = 0.5 x (gross/2) each, split EQUAL-DOLLAR across the names in L and S (so ~ (gross/2)/8 per name). Residual book beta after the single-name legs: beta_book = sum_L w_i*b_i - sum_S w_j*b_j (w = signed dollar weight / equity). Add a SHORT SPY (or QQQ for a tech-heavy book) leg sized to flatten it: SPY_short_notional = beta_book * equity (only the residual; typically small since L and S betas largely offset). HYSTERESIS to suppress turnover: a name ENTERS L only if it is in the top-8 by R; once held it is RETAINED until it leaves the top-12 (wide band). Symmetric for S (enter bottom-8, exit only on leaving bottom-12). HARD CAP: at most 1 new name added per leg per calendar week (rank-priority queue); excess qualifying names wait. Skip any name with earnings within the next 5 trading days (reuse context.daysToEarnings) to avoid event gap risk. No new entries when VIX >= 35 (crisis regime — momentum reverses; reuse getVIX()).
spec.exitRule: Exit a long when it leaves the top-12 by R, OR conf(s) drops < 0.50, OR conc(s) becomes 'discordant', OR after a hard max-hold of 42 trading days (forces re-evaluation past the model's reliable horizon). Exit a short when it leaves the bottom-12, OR conf<0.50, OR max-hold 42d. On any exit, immediately re-solve equal-dollar weights over the surviving book and resize the SPY/QQQ hedge to re-flatten residual beta. Full liquidation (all single-name legs + hedge) if VIX >= 45 (tail stop) or if realized book drawdown from peak exceeds 12% (capital preservation). Rebalance the SPY/QQQ hedge whenever |beta_book| > 0.05 (re-hedge band) to avoid churning the hedge on tiny drifts.
spec.instruments: Cash equity long/short basket: up to 8 long + up to 8 short from the ~80 high-liquidity optionable large-caps in getTrainedSymbols() (must be a TRAINED symbol — isTrainedSymbol(s) — or runHFInference returns null). Plus one short SPY (default) or QQQ (tech-tilted book) beta-hedge leg. No options, no single-name leverage; gross ~2x via the dollar-neutral L/S, net dollar exposure ~0, net beta ~0 by construction.
spec.positionSizing: Fixed capital E (e.g. $100k sim NAV in capital_events). Gross target = 2.0 x E (1.0x long single-names + ~1.0x short single-names; SPY hedge is only the residual-beta sliver, usually <0.1x). Per-name dollar = (1.0 x E)/N_active per side, N_active <= 8, EQUAL-DOLLAR (not vol-weighted — equal-dollar is more robust to estimation error at this signal strength and matches the literature's quantile construction). RISK CAP: per-name notional hard-capped at 8% of E (so a degenerate book with N_active=4 does not concentrate). Daily borrow accrues on short notional at DEFAULT_COSTS.shortBorrowAnnualBps (50 bps/yr). If fewer than 4 names qualify on either side, run that side smaller and proportionally shrink the other side to STAY dollar-neutral (never let net exposure drift). Crisis de-gross: at VIX>=35 hold existing book but add nothing; at VIX>=45 flatten.
spec.dataSources: AVAILABLE LIVE TODAY (no new infra): iTransformer forecast_returns[] + model_confidence via runHFInference / GET /api/forecast (HF ONNX, client-side WASM); ensembleScore + modelSignals + predictions[daysOut=30] via generatePrediction / GET /api/predict; concordance via ConcordanceCard logic (forecast_returns[29] vs ensemble 30d); quote.beta + price + earnings (context.daysToEarnings) via getStockQuote/getStockContext (yahoo-finance2); VIX via getVIX(); SPY/QQQ OHLCV for the hedge. Cost + perf engine already exists: src/lib/backtest/costs.ts (DEFAULT_COSTS) and src/lib/backtest/metrics.ts (computeMetrics, curveCorrelation). NOT-YET-AVAILABLE (must build to backtest — this is the binding gap): (a) a point-in-time forecast HISTORY — forecasts are computed in the browser (runHFInference returns false when typeof window==='undefined') and NEVER persisted to Postgres, so there is NO historical panel of forecast_returns[29]/model_confidence/ensembleScore. Must EITHER forward-log a new daily `forecast_snapshots(symbol, asof_date, fcst_ret_21, model_confidence, ensemble_score, concordance, beta)` table, OR regenerate a walk-forward panel via the Colab ONNX models (train_itransformer.ipynb) replaying features as-of each date with NO look-ahead. (b) a daily NAV / equity-basket schema — current simulated_trades is option-centric; reuse capital_events for NAV and add an equity-leg table. WITHOUT (a) there is no valid backtest, only forward paper-trading.
spec.capacityNote: Capacity is high relative to retail size and is NOT the binding constraint — all names are mega/large-cap (the ~80-name trained universe) with penny-wide spreads; the engine's DEFAULT_COSTS (3bps spread + 2bps slippage per side) already assumes liquid large-caps and small retail size, so $100k-$5M of gross trades without moving the tape. The BINDING constraints are instead: (1) edge thinness — 45 bps gross vs 25 bps cost leaves ~20 bps/trade, and the literature warns ML cross-section alpha is 'much lower than prior literature' and mostly long-side, so the SHORT leg may contribute little net of borrow; (2) turnover — without the top-8/exit-top-12 hysteresis + 1-add/leg/week cap, ~half-book monthly rotation incurs 'substantial trading costs' (the documented killer of these strategies); the hysteresis is load-bearing, not cosmetic; (3) model-confidence decay — model_confidence is IN-SAMPLE dir_acc_30d and decays toward 0.50 OOS, so the >=0.55 gate filters on a possibly-optimistic number. Insider MSPR adds little here (insider-buy abnormal returns concentrate in microcaps; this universe is all large-cap).
spec.falsifier: Build the point-in-time panel (forward-log `forecast_snapshots` daily for >= 6 months, OR regenerate a >= 3-year walk-forward panel from the Colab ONNX models with strictly as-of features). Then simulate the exact rules above and charge src/lib/backtest/costs.ts DEFAULT_COSTS (10bps round-trip/name + 50bps/yr borrow on shorts + SPY-hedge cost). REFUTED (accept H0, kill the strategy) if ANY of: (1) the cost-adjusted long-minus-short spread per 21-trading-day holding period is <= 20 bps with t-stat < 2 over >= 100 non-overlapping rebalances (i.e. net edge does not clear the engine's own costs with significance); OR (2) the rank is NOT monotone — Spearman rho between assigned quantile (Q1..Q8) and realized forward 21d return is < 0.10 across the walk-forward, showing R orders nothing; OR (3) the concordance + model_confidence>=0.55 gate does NOT improve the long-leg net spread vs the ungated top-8 (gate adds no value, contradicting its premise); OR (4) the beta-hedged L/S daily-return curve shows |curveCorrelation| > 0.2 with the incumbent short-vol equity curve (orthogonality claim false — it is just re-expressed market/vol exposure). Passing requires all four to hold simultaneously on out-of-sample data.

redteam.lens: COSTS & CAPACITY (realistic retail frictions + capacity)
redteam.kill: The headline "45 bps gross vs 25 bps cost = 20 bps net" miscounts the cost of a DOLLAR-NEUTRAL, BETA-HEDGED L/S book and overstates the gross edge for THIS universe. Three problems: (1) The spec's own two cost figures contradict each other — estCostBpsPerTrade=25 vs the falsifier's "10 bps round-trip/name" from the cited costs.ts (verified: DEFAULT_COSTS = (3 spread + 2 slippage) x 2 sides = 10 bps/name round-trip). Neither is the true per-cycle cost. (2) The headline omits costs that are charged regardless of turnover: short borrow = 50 bps/yr x 21/252 = 4.2 bps/cycle on the short notional EVERY cycle, plus SPY/QQQ hedge churn (re-hedged on |beta_book|>0.05 with DAILY evaluation, ~1-2 bps/cycle amortized). A realistic per-cycle cost is ~10-16 bps once you add round-trip + 4.2 bps borrow + hedge churn — not the 25 bps quoted and not separable from the 45 the way "45-25=20" implies. (3) The 45 bps gross is itself optimistic and sits in the WORST regime for the claimed mechanism: the spec restricts to ~80 mega/large-caps, but the literature it cites (AFA "Expected Returns on ML Strategies"; Frankfurt-School real-time ML) finds large-cap ML cross-sectional alpha is ~half of small-cap (six-factor alpha 0.99%/mo big vs 2.37%/mo small) and "mostly long-side." The spec concedes this in capacityNote. If the edge is mostly long-side, the SHORT leg's gross contribution is ~0 after the 4.2 bps/cycle borrow, making it a NET-NEGATIVE cost drag that the book is FORCED to hold to stay dollar-neutral. Subtracting a ~5-8 bps short-leg drag from the already-thin 20 bps headline leaves a realistic net of ~5-12 bps/cycle — below the spec's own ">=20 bps net, t-stat>=2 over 100 rebalances" pass bar, and the in-sample model_confidence>=0.55 gate (dir_acc_30d that decays toward 0.50 OOS) means the realized gross will be below 45.
redteam.mitigation: Survives only if ANY of: (a) gross edge is materially >45 bps (e.g. 60-80 bps net of the OOS confidence decay), which the large-cap-only restriction makes unlikely per the cited literature; (b) DROP the short leg and run long-only / long-tilt — the literature places the alpha on the long side, and this removes the 4.2 bps/cycle borrow plus half the round-trip and the short-leg drag, leaving a thin but plausibly-positive large-cap momentum sleeve; (c) lengthen the holding period well beyond 21d to amortize round-trip (literature shows only ~5 bps gain from doubling holding to 2 months, so weak); (d) replace the in-sample dir_acc_30d confidence gate with an OOS-validated number so the realized gross is not optimistic; (e) charge the FULL per-cycle cost (round-trip + borrow-every-cycle + hedge churn) in the backtest and re-derive expectedEdgeBpsPerTrade rather than asserting 45 vs 25.
redteam.residual: Capacity is genuinely NOT the binding constraint — $100k-$5M does not move mega-cap tape (mega-cap spreads ~1-4.5 bps per the S&P-100/S&P-500 spread research), so I do not kill on capacity. The residual edge after the costs attack: a LONG-ONLY large-cap momentum/under-reaction sleeve plausibly survives with a thin positive net (long-leg gross minus ~5 bps round-trip), consistent with the literature putting the alpha on the long side. But the specific object being tested — the dollar-neutral, beta-hedged L/S basket — has its 20 bps headline eaten down to ~5-12 bps by per-cycle borrow + short-leg drag + hedge churn, and is unlikely to clear t-stat>=2 over 100 rebalances. Secondary cost/granularity crack: at $100k NAV the SPY beta-hedge sliver (residual beta x equity, often a few hundred dollars / sub-1-share) is un-implementable, silently leaving residual market beta unhedged and reintroducing the very market-direction correlation the |rho|<=0.2 orthogonality claim depends on — so even the orthogonality benefit is partly a backtest artifact at retail size. Net: kill the L/S-as-specified; a long-only variant is the only piece with surviving edge.

redteam.lens: DATA-SNOOPING, DECAY & ORTHOGONALITY: (a) is the momentum/ML mechanism real or a decayed/known-arbitraged curve-fit, and (b) is the beta-hedged L/S stream truly orthogonal to short-vol/put-selling, or short-vol beta in disguise?
redteam.kill: Both prongs fail, and the orthogonality prong is fatal. (b) ORTHOGONALITY: The core claim -- "beta-hedging removes the one factor CSP P&L is exposed to, leaving ~0 structural correlation to short-vol" -- is false by mechanism. The momentum-crash literature (Daniel-Moskowitz; Barroso-Santa-Clara; corroborated by web-check) shows cross-sectional momentum L/S has strong NEGATIVE exposure to market-variance innovations in bear markets and behaves like a WRITTEN OPTION / short-gamma position exactly in left-tail states. Beta-hedging strips LINEAR market direction (first moment); it does NOT strip the CONVEX/variance exposure (second moment). So the residual L/S shares the same crash-state, short-vol left tail as the incumbent put-selling book -- it is short-vol in disguise, not orthogonal. Worse, the falsifier's own orthogonality test is mis-specified: curveCorrelation (src/lib/backtest/metrics.ts) is full-sample Pearson correlation of daily returns, dominated by quiet periods, so it will read near-zero in normal times even when tail co-movement is high. The |rho|<=0.2 gate can PASS while the true short-vol tail correlation -- the only correlation that matters for a short-vol overlay -- is large. The test cannot detect the risk it claims to bound. (a) DECAY/SNOOPING: Momentum is the most-published, most-arbitraged anomaly (Jegadeesh-Titman 1993, 32yr public); McLean-Pontiff (2016 JF, web-confirmed) document 26% OOS / 58% post-publication decay, and the spec's cited 0.4-0.7 trend Sharpe is already the post-decay number. The ML ranker stacked on top leans on results (Frankfurt-School real-time ML) that themselves report alpha 'much lower than prior literature' and concentrated in hard-to-arbitrage MICROCAPS -- the exact opposite of this all-mega-cap universe, so the most-cited ML edge is sourced from names this strategy refuses to trade. Finally, the signal is built on model_confidence = IN-SAMPLE dir_acc_30d, which the spec itself admits decays toward 0.50 OOS; and there is NO persisted point-in-time forecast panel (forecasts run only in-browser, runHFInference returns false server-side), so the strategy is currently UNFALSIFIABLE -- there is no valid backtest, only forward paper-trading.
redteam.mitigation: To survive (b): replace the full-sample Pearson curveCorrelation gate with a STATE-CONDITIONED / tail test -- e.g. correlation of the L/S stream to the short-vol book conditional on VIX > 30 or on the worst-decile market days, plus a regression of L/S returns on a short-vol/variance-swap factor (or on the incumbent CSP curve) AND on a squared-market-return (convexity) term; require near-zero loading on BOTH. Also test exceedance/copula tail dependence, not just linear rho. To survive (a): (i) build the point-in-time forecast_snapshots panel with strictly as-of features (no look-ahead) over >=3yr, and demonstrate the gate's edge OUT-OF-SAMPLE on data the ONNX models never saw; (ii) replace in-sample model_confidence with an OOS-estimated, walk-forward directional-accuracy number before using it as a >=0.55 filter; (iii) explicitly haircut expected edge for post-publication decay and show the spread survives on the POST-2010 sub-sample, not the full history that includes pre-arbitrage years. Even with all of this, the strategy must clear the engine's DEFAULT_COSTS (10bps round-trip + 50bps/yr borrow) on a >=100-rebalance OOS walk-forward with t>=2 -- which on ~20bps net edge with documented decay is a coin flip.
redteam.residual: Thin and unverified. Momentum/under-reaction is a REAL economic mechanism (not pure curve-fit) and has not fully decayed, so some long-side relative-rank dispersion likely persists in large-caps -- but post-decay, post-cost (~20bps gross edge over 25bps cost is admitted by the spec as razor-thin), and with the short leg likely contributing little net of borrow, the surviving expected alpha is marginal and statistically fragile. Whatever survives is NOT cleanly orthogonal to the incumbent short-vol book: it carries a shared short-gamma left-tail that the proposed test cannot see, so adding it provides far less diversification than claimed and may actually CONCENTRATE crash risk on top of the put-selling book in a quant-unwind/vol-spike. Net: no demonstrable, orthogonal, cost-surviving edge until a true point-in-time OOS backtest plus a tail-conditioned orthogonality test are run; default to skepticism -> does not survive this lens.

redteam.lens: Regime & Robustness (regime dependence, parameter sensitivity, crowding, tail risk; survival through 2018-Q4, Mar-2020, 2022, Aug-2024)
redteam.kill: The strategy's own stated mechanism IS cross-sectional/time-series momentum (Jegadeesh-Titman; Moskowitz-Ooi-Pedersen), which the literature shows "behaves like a short written call on the market" in panic states, with -45% to -80% L/S drawdowns in the months FOLLOWING sharp declines (alphaarchitect; Daniel-Moskowitz "Momentum Crashes"). Beta-hedging removes AVERAGE market direction but does nothing for this convexity — the crash happens during the post-bottom reversal when shorted losers rip higher, which is orthogonal to beta. The design then makes this WORSE in two ways. (1) The concordance gate requires the iTransformer 60d path AND the fixed-weight statistical ensemble (verified in prediction.ts: momentum 25% + technical 20% + mean-rev 20%, all price-action loaded; hf-model trained heavily on momentum/price features) to AGREE on direction. Both are trend-loaded, so they agree precisely in trending regimes and the gate is a trend-PERSISTENCE filter — it concentrates exposure at exactly the turning points where momentum crashes. (2) The risk controls are pro-cyclical and EOD-only: VIX>=45 'tail stop' forces full liquidation, and the 12% drawdown stop forces it too. Walking the four windows: Mar-2020 (VIX ~82) flattens the book AT the late-March bottom and sits in cash through the sharpest momentum-reversal rebound in history — a structural blow-up, not a tail. Aug-2024 (VIX intraday ~65 for one day, full recovery in ~2 weeks) flattens on a transient carry-unwind spike, guaranteeing selling the local bottom of a whipsaw; EOD-only means it cannot even react until the close after the worst is over. 2018-Q4 (VIX peaked ~36, so the 45 flatten never fires but the 12% DD stop trips during the late-Dec reversal) locks in the momentum-crash loss right before the January 2019 rip-back. Only 2022's slow value-over-growth grind is plausibly survivable, and even that carries the sharp Jan-2023 junk-rally reversal. So 3 of 4 stress windows are forced losses by construction. Compounding robustness failures: model_confidence is a STATIC in-sample dir_acc_30d per stock (hf-model.ts L385-418), not time-varying — the >=0.55 gate filters WHICH stocks, never WHICH regime, so it gives zero crisis protection and is fit on optimistic in-sample accuracy that decays toward 0.50 OOS (the spec admits this). The orthogonality-to-short-vol claim (|rho|<=0.2) is a calm-regime artifact: full-sample curveCorrelation can read low while LEFT-TAIL correlation spikes, because in a crisis the short-vol book craters (realized vol explodes) AND the momentum L/S crashes in the same reversal — they co-lose exactly when diversification is needed. And the edge is thin and crowded: ~20 bps net (45 gross vs 25 cost), mega-cap cross-sectional momentum is among the most crowded factor trades, and the spec itself concedes ML cross-section alpha is 'much lower than prior literature' and 'mostly long-side' (so the short leg may not even pay its 50bps borrow). With ~80 names / ~16-name book and 8+ tuned thresholds (0.55 conf, +/-0.5% band, top-8/exit-12, VIX 35/45, 12% DD, 42d hold), the parameter-to-breadth ratio is high. Net: the edge is regime-dependent (needs persistent trends + low-vol dispersion), and the risk machinery actively converts crises into realized losses by selling bottoms.
redteam.mitigation: For the edge to survive this lens, several things would have to change and be PROVEN on the not-yet-built point-in-time panel: (1) Replace the hard VIX>=45 / 12%-DD full-liquidation with conditional volatility-targeting / dynamic de-grossing (the documented crash mitigant — vol-scaling 'significantly reduces momentum drawdowns and tail risk'), so the book de-risks ahead of spikes rather than dumping at the bottom; ideally add a momentum-crash overlay (e.g. dynamic exposure as a function of bear-market state and recent realized vol, per Daniel-Moskowitz / Barroso-Santa-Clara). (2) Add an intraday or at-least-staggered exit so an EOD-only flatten on a 1-day carry-spike (Aug-2024) is not hardwired to sell the local bottom. (3) Make the confidence gate time-varying and OOS — gate on rolling realized rank-IC / regime, not a static in-sample dir_acc that the spec admits decays toward 0.50. (4) Prove orthogonality conditionally, not just full-sample: require |rho| to short-vol <= 0.2 SPECIFICALLY in the left-tail / VIX>30 sub-sample, and show the L/S does not crash in the same months the short-vol book bleeds. (5) Demonstrate the edge clears costs in the bad regimes individually (2018-Q4, Mar-2020 recovery quarter, Jan-2023 reversal), not just in aggregate where 2022 can mask the crashes. (6) Stress the crowding/short-leg: show the short leg pays its borrow net and that the strategy degrades gracefully under a factor-unwind scenario. Absent the panel (forecasts are browser-only, never persisted — the binding gap the spec itself flags), none of this is testable, so it remains forward-paper-trading only.
redteam.residual: Possibly a small, conditional edge remains, but only in the strategy's favorable regime: persistent-trend, low-VIX, high-cross-sectional-dispersion environments (its best window is 2022-style slow value-over-growth grinds). If the hard liquidation stops are replaced with vol-targeted de-grossing and a genuine momentum-crash overlay, the survivable core is the ~12-month under-reaction premium net of costs on the long leg — but that is precisely the crowded, thin (~20bps), and mostly-long-side edge the spec already concedes is 'much lower than prior literature.' After this attack, the defensible claim shrinks from 'orthogonal, beta/dollar-neutral all-weather L/S' to 'a regime-conditional trend-dispersion sleeve that must be vol-managed and that co-crashes with the short-vol book in the left tail.' Net residual edge in crisis/turning-point regimes: negative-to-zero by construction; net residual edge in calm trending regimes: thin and crowded. Not enough to call it regime-robust.

name: TS-Gated Convexity Overlay (VIX9D/VIX backwardation-armed SPY put debit spread)
family: Tail-risk / defensive overlay (crisis alpha): VIX-term-structure & SKEW-gated convexity timing + trend-confirmed de-risking, engineered to be a regime state-machine (orthogonal to continuous short-vol theta harvest) rather than a naked long-vol mirror.
rationale: Three independently-Fatal red-team kills, all verified in code: (1) the 2.0x take-profit cap structurally amputates the fat right tail that is the entire thesis, making gross per-arm expectancy negative (0.25*+1.0 + 0.75*-0.85 = -0.39 debit-units) BEFORE 12-40%-of-debit four-leg OTM-put frictions; (2) the "1 contract per $200k NAV" cap makes qty=floor(NAV/200k)=0 for any retail account under $200k, so the strategy is undeployable on its own platform; (3) it is untestable as specified — engine.ts (src/lib/backtest/engine.ts:232) trades only equity via targetShares=targetDollars/price with equity-bps tradeCost and no option_spread/getOptionsChain path; AlignedData (engine.ts:21-32) carries vix/vix9d but NO ^VIX3M and NO ^SKEW, so 2 of 4 ARMED conditions and all spread P&L cannot be simulated; getOptionsChain (yahoo-finance.ts:140) computes DTE from new Date() and returns only the live chain, so no historical executable-fill backtest is possible. Mechanism (VIX9D backwardation clusters into stress) is real but the orthogonality is just a short-vol hedge leg, not an independent edge, and effective n~=2 monetizable crashes makes crisis-alpha unestablishable.
bestUseInEnsemble: Only as a future, heavily-respecified defined-risk tail-smoothing overlay — NOT a standalone alpha. To have any ensemble value it must: remove the 2x-TP cap and let winners run toward the short strike (or widen/drop the short leg) so the convex right tail can pay for the ~85% full-loss arms; switch to whole-contract sizing appropriate to actual account NAV; and first build the missing infrastructure (historical options-chain replay, ^VIX3M and ^SKEW added to AlignedData, an option_spread P&L path in the engine, and a multi-year period1=8-10y macro fetch capturing >=15 ARMED episodes across 2008/2011/2015/2018/2020/2022) before any expectancy or factor-residualized orthogonality claim can be validated. As written it adds no orthogonal driver, only a sample-fragile, cost-negative reshaping of the incumbent's existing short-vol factor that cannot even be backtested.

spec.name: TS-Gated Convexity Overlay (VIX9D/VIX backwardation-armed SPY put debit spread)
spec.family: Tail-risk / defensive overlay (crisis alpha): discrete VIX-term-structure + SKEW-gated long-convexity state machine, financed as a defined-risk put debit spread; engineered to be a sparse regime-triggered convex payoff orthogonal to continuous short-vol theta harvest (NOT a continuous long-vega mirror).
spec.mechanism: Structural risk-premium TIMING, not a vol-premium bet. The VIX9D/VIX slope is a near-real-time proxy for the market price of NEAR-TERM jump risk vs. a 1-month horizon; VIX3M/VIX prices the 1m-vs-3m slope. ~80% of the time the curve is in contango (VIX9D<VIX, VIX3M>VIX): convexity carries negative (theta+vol-mean-reversion bleed), so you do NOT own it. The ~20% backwardation state (near-leg inverts above the 1m, and the 1m flattens toward the 3m) historically clusters immediately before/into drawdowns when dealer/dealer-hedging demand for near-term protection spikes. Owning convexity ONLY in that state, and ONLY as a debit SPREAD (long 5-7% OTM SPY put, short 12-15% OTM put, 25-35 DTE), caps cost-of-carry and vega so you are not naked-long expensive vol. The payoff is asymmetric timing: lose the small debit (~0.2-0.5% NAV) on most arms, win a multiple on a convex SPY decline to the short strike — a recognized crisis-alpha source. Because it is OFF ~80% of the time and capped-vega when ON, the return stream is a step function on a discrete macro state with low continuous correlation to the incumbent's daily theta accrual. IMPORTANT honesty bound (consistent with the candidate's own web check): the slope's UNCONDITIONAL per-trade edge is small/decaying OOS; the expectancy lives in a handful of genuine crisis episodes, and 'convert drawdowns to entries' partially re-correlates with the incumbent's mean-reversion entries, so orthogonality is real but bounded.
spec.hypothesisH0: Conditional on the ARMED state (backwardation + elevated SKEW), the SPY put-debit-spread overlay has mean net P&L per episode <= 0 after realistic options bid/ask + slippage + commissions; AND its daily-return correlation to the incumbent short-vol equity curve is statistically indistinguishable from the correlation of a continuously-held (un-gated) long-convexity sleeve (i.e. the discrete gating adds no orthogonality beyond simply being long vol).
spec.hypothesisH1: Conditional on ARMED, the overlay has mean net P&L per episode > 0 after costs (driven by a fat right tail in crises), Sortino positive, AND |corr(overlay daily returns, incumbent daily returns)| < 0.25 over the full sample while delivering a materially MORE NEGATIVE corr than -0.25 specifically on the incumbent's worst-decile drawdown days (i.e. it hedges the incumbent's left tail without being its continuous mirror).
spec.signalDefinition:
Compute at each EOD from forward-filled macro closes (see dataSources). Use the engine's existing named features and conventions; note the engine's term-structure features use the VIX3M/VIX ordering, so I restate the candidate's slope3M in that convention to avoid a sign error.
- slope9 = RegimeSnapshot.vixTermRatio = (^VIX9D close)/(^VIX close)  [engine iTransformer row[115]; strategies/types.ts:27]. Backwardation of the near leg => slope9 > 1.00.
- ratio3M = (^VIX3M close)/(^VIX close)  [engine row[76] = (vix3m-vix)/vix = ratio3M - 1]. Curve flattening/inverting toward 1m => ratio3M < 1.05 (engine's candidate threshold slope3M=VIX/VIX3M>0.95 is the reciprocal: VIX/VIX3M>0.95  <=>  ratio3M < 1.0526; use ratio3M < 1.05).
- tsMom = 5d change in ratio3M = ratio3M_t - ratio3M_{t-5}  [engine row[163]]. Require tsMom < 0 (term structure actively flattening, not already-normalizing) as a confirmation, OR slope9 rising 5d.
- skewElevated = (skew_zscore_20 >= +1.0)  [engine feature 151, the 20d z-score of ^SKEW]. Documented alternative if a longer window is wanted: skewPct1y = rank of today's ^SKEW within trailing 252 closes >= 0.80; NOTE this 1y-percentile is NOT a precomputed named feature and requires >=252 days of ^SKEW history, which the default fetch does not provide (see falsifier) — so the spec's primary filter is the existing skew_zscore_20 >= 1.0, with skewPct1y as an optional stricter gate only if/when >=252d ^SKEW is loaded.
- ARMED_raw(t) = [ slope9 > 1.00 ] AND [ ratio3M < 1.05 ] AND [ skew_zscore_20 >= 1.0 ] AND [ tsMom < 0 ].
- ARMED(t) = ARMED_raw(t) AND ARMED_raw(t-1)  (>= 2 consecutive EOD closes, debounce false arms). Otherwise DISARMED (state defaults to contango / cash).
spec.entryRule:
On the first EOD that ARMED(t)=true AND there is no open overlay position: open ONE SPY (default; QQQ permitted via the same rule on QQQ's own SPY-referenced macro state) put DEBIT spread from the LIVE chain returned by getOptionsChain('SPY'):
1) Choose expiry with dte in [25,35] (nearest to 30).
2) Long leg Kl = strike closest to 0.94*S (i.e. ~6% OTM put); Short leg Ks = strike closest to 0.865*S (~13.5% OTM put). Require Kl > Ks (it is, both are OTM puts), spread width W = (Kl - Ks).
3) Liquidity gates per leg (reject arm if any fail): openInterest >= 200, bid > 0, and relative bid/ask = (ask-bid)/mid <= 0.15. Use mid = (bid+ask)/2 per leg.
4) Entry debit (executable, not mid): pay the ASK on the long leg, receive the BID on the short leg => debit_fill = ask(Kl) - bid(Ks), per share; cost per contract = 100*debit_fill. Reject if debit_fill <= 0 or debit_fill/W > 0.40 (too rich) or < 0.10 (mispriced/illiquid).
5) Reprice/validate with the Black-Scholes engine (impliedVolatility() per leg from chain mid to sanity-check IV, putGreeks() for net delta/vega) and store net Greeks for monitoring.
Record in simulated_trades as instrument 'option_spread': strike_price=Kl, a second leg via notes/JSON (short Ks), premium_received = -debit_fill (negative => debit paid), collateral = max-loss = (debit_fill*100*qty) [defined risk; spread can never lose more than the debit], dte_at_entry, stock_price_at_entry=S, vix_at_entry, market_regime_at_entry='BACKWARDATION_ARMED', quantity=qty.
spec.exitRule:
Whichever comes first:
1) PROFIT: net spread mid value >= 2.0 * debit_fill (take profit at 2x debit) — close by selling the long (hit BID) and buying back the short (pay ASK).
2) STATE EXIT: if DISARMED for 2 consecutive EOD closes (term structure normalized to contango) AND position is at a profit or breakeven, close to recycle capital; if at a loss and still > 7 DTE, HOLD (the convex tail is precisely what you paid for — do not panic-close a cheap option just because the arm faded).
3) TIME/DTE: hard-close at 7 DTE remaining regardless (avoid gamma/assignment risk and pin risk on the long leg); never carry into expiration week.
4) ASSIGNMENT GUARD: if the SHORT leg goes ITM (S <= Ks) close the whole spread next EOD (lock the near-max gain, eliminate assignment).
All exits use executable fills (sell-the-bid / buy-the-ask) and book the same per-leg options spread cost again. Only ONE overlay position open at a time; do not re-arm until the prior episode is closed.
spec.instruments: SPY put debit spread (default), 25-35 DTE, defined max loss = net debit; long ~6% OTM / short ~13.5% OTM. QQQ permitted as an alternative underlying using the SAME market-wide VIX/SKEW state (NOT a separate QQQ-vol signal). Explicitly NO naked long puts, NO VIX futures, NO VIXY/VXX (avoids roll bleed and uncapped vega). 100% cash / T-bill (earns the risk-free rate in the sim) when DISARMED.
spec.positionSizing: Fixed-capital, capital-at-risk sized. Per episode risk budget = 0.50% of current NAV. qty = floor( (0.005 * NAV) / (debit_fill * 100) ), min 0, max 1 contract per $200k NAV slice to cap concentration. Because the spread's max loss equals the debit, capital-at-risk == premium paid == collateral booked; there is no additional margin/collateral beyond the debit. Hard caps: at most ONE open episode; cumulative annual carry bleed capped at 3% NAV (if YTD overlay debits-lost reach 3% NAV, stand down the overlay until next calendar year — prevents death-by-a-thousand-arms in a persistently choppy-but-no-crash regime). When DISARMED, 0% deployed (cash earns rf in the equity curve).
spec.dataSources: ALL already in the manifest — nothing new: yahoo-finance2 ^VIX9D, ^VIX, ^VIX3M, ^SKEW (forward-filled to SPY trading days exactly as forecast/route.ts alignMacroToStockDates does); full SPY/QQQ options chain via getOptionsChain() (per-strike bid/ask/impliedVolatility/openInterest/volume/dte) for executable spread pricing; black-scholes.ts (putPrice/putGreeks/impliedVolatility) to synthesize/mark/validate the spread and compute net delta/vega; RegimeSnapshot.vixTermRatio + iTransformer rows 76/115/163 + features 150/151 (skew_level, skew_zscore_20) as the named state inputs; Strategy interface (instrument:'option_spread') for the walk-forward engine + live runner; Neon Postgres simulated_trades + capital_events for the sim/backtest ledger. Cost model: do NOT use DEFAULT_COSTS equity bps for the option legs — costs MUST be taken from the actual chain bid/ask (cross half-spread per leg, 4 legs round trip) plus optionCommissionPerContract ($0.65) per leg per side; DEFAULT_COSTS applies only to the cash/T-bill sleeve.
spec.capacityNote: Effectively unlimited at retail scale and irrelevant as a constraint: SPY/QQQ monthly put verticals are among the deepest-liquidity options on earth; 1-20 contracts at 25-35 DTE, 6-13% OTM, move size is immaterial vs OI/volume. The REAL constraint is not capacity but the bid/ask itself: OTM SPY put legs routinely show 3-8% relative spreads, and a vertical crosses TWO of them on entry and TWO on exit, so frictions scale with the debit, not with size. Strategy is capital-light (only the debit is ever at risk, <=0.5% NAV/episode), so it scales linearly in NAV with no slippage penalty until institutional size (>~1,000 contracts), far beyond the intended retail account.
spec.falsifier:
Two concrete data results, EITHER of which refutes the spec:
(1) DATA-DEPTH FALSIFIER (most likely to fire first): pull ^VIX9D, ^VIX, ^VIX3M, ^SKEW via yahoo-finance2 and count usable daily closes and the number of distinct ARMED episodes (>=2 consecutive arms) actually observed. The engine's forecast/route.ts fetches macro with period1 = now-1y and lists ^VIX9D among 'flaky tickers'; Yahoo ^VIX9D history is short/patchy. If the joined ^VIX9D-^VIX-^VIX3M-^SKEW panel yields < ~6-8 years of clean overlap OR < ~15 independent ARMED episodes containing at least 2-3 genuine SPY drawdowns of >=8%, then there is INSUFFICIENT data to establish crisis-alpha expectancy and the long-backtest claim is falsified — the strategy cannot be validated as specified on PutStrike's default data and must be downgraded to 'untestable as-is'.
(2) EDGE-NET-OF-COST FALSIFIER (if enough data exists): backtest the exact rules pricing every leg at executable fills (ask-up/bid-down) from the historical chain. If mean net P&L per ARMED episode <= 0 after costs (i.e. realized frictions + carry bleed >= the crisis right-tail), OR the SPY decline-to-short-strike hit rate is so low that >85% of episodes expire as full-debit losses and the surviving winners do not net positive, then H0 holds and the edge does not survive the options bid/ask — reject. Additionally, if corr(overlay daily returns, incumbent short-vol daily returns) over the full sample is more negative than -0.5 (i.e. it behaves like the continuous long-vega mirror, not a sparse discrete hedge), the orthogonality claim is falsified even if P&L is positive.

redteam.lens: COSTS & CAPACITY (retail frictions: commissions, options bid/ask, slippage, assignment, min granularity vs fixed capital; capacity)
redteam.kill: Three independent cost/capacity kills, any one of which is fatal; together they are decisive. (1) MIN-GRANULARITY vs FIXED CAPITAL — the sizing rule caps qty at "max 1 contract per $200k NAV slice," i.e. qty_cap = floor(NAV/200k). For ANY account below $200k this is 0, so NO position can ever be opened. The strategy is dead-on-arrival at realistic retail scale; even at exactly $200k it can hold only 1 contract risking ~0.475% NAV. PutStrike is a retail tool (Schwab $0.65/contract, "small accounts should use spreads") — the spec is mis-scaled for its own platform. (2) The GROSS edge does not exist before costs, because exit rule #1 caps every winner at 2.0x debit (= +1.0xdebit net), while faded arms lose ~0.85xdebit and the spec's own falsifier concedes >=85% of episodes expire as full-debit losses. Even granting a generous 25% win rate, gross per-arm expectancy = 0.25*(+1.0)+0.75*(-0.85) = -0.39 debit-units (~-18 bps NAV) and at a more realistic 10-15% win rate it is -27 to -32 bps NAV — NEGATIVE before a cent of friction. The 2x-take-profit rule structurally amputates the fat right tail that is the entire thesis. (3) ROUND-TRIP FRICTION crosses 4 OTM-put half-spreads + 8 commission events. Using the spec's OWN concession that "OTM SPY put legs routinely show 3-8% relative spreads" (and Aug 5 2024 confirms spreads "widened significantly" in exactly the ARMED/stress regime this trades), frictions consume 12%-40% of the debit per arm (computed: 11.4% at 5/8% rel-spreads up to 29.8% at 12/25%). This is SUBTRACTED on top of an already-negative gross expectancy. The spec's estCostBpsPerTrade=22 vs expectedEdgeBpsPerTrade=35 (claimed net +13) is a denominator trick: the ~3-14 bps-NAV friction only looks small because the position is microscopic (0.475% NAV), while the 35 bps "edge" implicitly requires ~74% return on deployed capital that only a real crash delivers — and the 2x cap prevents capturing it. Cost (bps of NAV) and edge (debit/right-tail) use mismatched bases. Net per-arm expectancy is unambiguously < 0, so H0 holds.
redteam.mitigation: To survive this lens the spec would need ALL of: (a) drop the $200k/contract cap and the percent-of-NAV sizing for a contracts-based floor that works at retail NAV (or restrict the strategy to >=$1M accounts and say so honestly); (b) REMOVE the 2.0x take-profit cap and instead hold winners toward the short strike (let payoff run to ~+3xdebit) so the convex right tail can actually pay for the ~85% full-loss arms — note this re-introduces gamma/assignment risk the spec was trying to avoid; (c) relax or honestly re-cost the liquidity gate, since requiring per-leg (ask-bid)/mid<=0.15 will REJECT the far-OTM short leg precisely during the crisis spikes where the edge lives (selection bias that strips the right tail), yet honoring real 8-25% ARMED-state spreads makes net expectancy worse. The codebase makes this concrete: the backtest engine (runBacktest in src/lib/backtest/engine.ts) only models equity shares (targetShares=targetDollars/price, tradeCost in equity bps); there is NO option-leg bid/ask crossing path, so the spec's "cost MUST come from chain bid/ask, 4 legs round trip" is unimplemented and untestable as written — it would have to be built before any of these claims could be validated.
redteam.residual: Essentially none on cost/capacity grounds. After this attack the per-arm net expectancy is negative under every plausible parameterization (gross already -18 to -32 bps NAV from the 2x-TP cap, then minus 12-40%-of-debit frictions), AND the strategy cannot even be deployed below $200k NAV. The only residual is conceptual: a TRUE crisis-convexity overlay can have positive expectancy IF (i) the right tail is uncapped (no 2x-TP), (ii) it is sized in whole contracts appropriate to account size, and (iii) it is run only on the cheapest, tightest near-the-money long legs rather than far-OTM verticals that get gutted by the short-leg spread. But that is a different strategy than the one specified. As written, the gross edge is smaller than round-trip cost (indeed the gross edge is itself negative), so it fails this lens.

redteam.lens: DATA-SNOOPING, DECAY & ORTHOGONALITY
redteam.kill: Both prongs fail, and a third axis (untestability) removes any way to rescue it. (a) Mechanism/decay: the signal is real-ish (VIX9D/VIX backwardation genuinely clusters into stress; Harbourfront 2026 finds VIX9D front-end info content survives OOS), BUT the spec self-admits the unconditional per-trade edge is "small/decaying OOS" and expectancy "lives in a handful of genuine crisis episodes." The cached ^VIX9D panel runs 2015-06-02 to 2026-06-01 (2,766 bars), which CLEARS the spec's own >=6-8yr length bar -- so falsifier #1 does not fire on length, but it fires on EPISODE COUNT: post-2015 contains only ~2 genuine >=8% convex crashes a 25-35 DTE / 6% OTM long leg would monetize before the 7-DTE hard close (Mar 2020 COVID; Feb 2018 partial). Aug 2024 was a ~2-day V-spike that largely round-trips inside a 30-DTE spread and may not reach the short strike. Crisis-alpha expectancy on n~=2 is statistically un-establishable -- it is an artifact of WHICH two crises landed in-sample, the textbook data-snooping failure. Decay is documented: Harvey/Rattray/Van Hemert and AQR show rolling-put tail hedges carry ~ -7%/yr drag with benefit in a few drawdowns, and the 2024-26 surface is in 'persistent compressed contango / spot-VIX compression' -- the right tail this monetizes is currently being repriced smaller. (b) Orthogonality: a long SPY put debit spread ARMED by elevated VIX/backwardation/SKEW is, mechanically, a long-vol / short-SPX-beta position whose payoff is monotone in the same vol-risk-premium the incumbent is short. On the incumbent's worst-decile days it is negatively correlated BY CONSTRUCTION -- that is the definition of a short-vol hedge leg, not an orthogonal driver. The H1 conjunction (|full-sample corr|<0.25 AND far-more-negative-than -0.25 in the left tail) is satisfiable only because the gate is so sparse the few ON days don't move full-sample corr -- i.e. low measured correlation is an artifact of sparseness/n, not an independent return source. The spec concedes 'convert drawdowns to entries partially re-correlates with the incumbent's mean-reversion entries.' Compounding both: the backtest engine cannot test this -- engine.ts has zero option_spread/getOptionsChain handling and only equity-bps DEFAULT_COSTS; getOptionsChain() is live-only with no historical chain, so executable-fill backtesting of 6%/13.5% OTM verticals over 2015-2026 is impossible. Unfalsifiable on PutStrike data => defaults to not-survives under a skeptical lens.
redteam.mitigation: To survive prong (a) it would need an independent historical options-chain source (e.g. ORATS/LiveVol/OptionMetrics) spanning >=2008 to deliver >=15 independent ARMED episodes with >=4-5 distinct >=8% crashes (2008, 2011, 2015, 2018, 2020, 2022, plus the 2015-2026 set), and demonstrate per-episode net P&L > 0 after executable bid/ask on all four legs in a TRUE out-of-sample window that excludes the in-sample crises -- because the engine cannot price historical spreads, this requires building chain-replay infrastructure that does not exist. To survive prong (b) it would need to show, on a residualized basis, that ON-state overlay returns retain edge AFTER regressing out the contemporaneous short-vol/SPX-beta factor (i.e. an alpha intercept that is not just the hedge leg of the incumbent's own short-vol exposure); merely showing low full-sample daily corr is insufficient because that is mechanically guaranteed by an 80%-off gate. Realistically: source 2008+ historical chains, define an embargoed OOS split, and pass a factor-residualized orthogonality test. Absent all three, downgrade to 'untestable as-is' per the spec's own falsifier #1 escape hatch.
redteam.residual: Thin and conditional. What survives the attack is a portfolio-CONSTRUCTION benefit (defined-risk convexity that bounds the -7%/yr put-drag and is off 80% of the time), not a standalone positive-expectancy alpha. As a diversifier the residual value is real but small: it can smooth the incumbent's left tail without the continuous long-vega bleed -- yet by the same token it is a short-vol hedge leg, not an orthogonal return source, so it does not add a new independent edge, only re-shapes the existing short-vol factor's payoff. With effective n~=2 monetizable crises in the testable window, even the smoothing claim is sample-dependent and could be a one-COVID artifact. Net: no defensible standalone edge after this lens; at most a modest, sample-fragile tail-smoothing overlay that the spec itself already concedes is 'bounded.'

redteam.lens: REGIME & ROBUSTNESS (regime dependence, parameter sensitivity, crowding, tail risk; survival across 2018-Q4, Mar-2020, 2022, Aug-2024)
redteam.kill: The edge is fatally regime-dependent in a way the chosen structure actively defeats, and it is untestable on PutStrike's own data, so the regime claim cannot even be validated. Three reinforcing kills: (1) STRUCTURE CANCELS THE EDGE IN THE EXACT REGIMES IT MUST SURVIVE. The candidate concedes expectancy lives in "a handful of genuine crisis episodes." But the payoff is a debit spread short ~13.5% OTM (Ks=0.865*S), which caps the convex tail precisely where crisis alpha is generated. The two largest recent vol events both round-tripped without SPY approaching that strike: Aug-2024 was the largest single-day VIX spike in history (180% to 65+, intraday collapse to 39, full equity recovery within weeks) yet SPY's drawdown was ~8-9% and reversed in days — the spread's short leg never engages, the long leg gets IV-crushed/theta-bled, and the position likely closes at or near full-debit loss despite a "correct" arm. Feb-2018 Volmageddon and the V-shaped Mar-2020 path (VIX 53->42 mean-reversion noted in the web evidence) have the same failure mode: a vol spike with a sub-13.5% or fast-reverting index decline is a loss, not a win, for THIS structure. So the strategy only pays in slow, deep, ≥13.5% grinds (2022, 2008) — a narrow subset of "crises," not crises generally. (2) THE GATE IS COINCIDENT, NOT LEADING. Web evidence: VIX9D/VIX backwardation "often coincides with market bottoms" and is "neither a precise timing signal nor a return-to-normal signal" — and one OOS backtest is "slightly negative." The 2-day debounce (ARMED(t) AND ARMED(t-1)) plus the tsMom<0 confirmation guarantees you arm AFTER the near-term spike is already underway — i.e., into peak near-term IV. You buy the long put when it is most expensive and the index is closest to its local bottom, then the term structure normalizes (it's "short-lived, days to weeks") and the STATE EXIT / 7-DTE rules close you out as vol mean-reverts. This is the canonical "buy high IV after the move" tail-hedge trap; in choppy-but-no-crash regimes the 3%/yr carry cap is hit by death-by-a-thousand-arms. (3) UNTESTABLE AS SPECIFIED ON PUTSTRIKE DATA — both falsifiers fire. DATA-DEPTH: forecast/route.ts:273-274 fetches ALL macro (^VIX9D/^VIX3M/^SKEW) with period1 = now − 1 year; ^VIX9D is in the "flaky tickers" retry list. One year cannot contain ~15 independent ARMED episodes spanning 2-3 genuine ≥8% drawdowns, so crisis-alpha expectancy is unestablishable. EDGE-NET-OF-COST: getOptionsChain() (yahoo-finance.ts:140) computes DTE from new Date() and returns ONLY the live chain — there is NO historical options chain anywhere in the repo, so the spread can never be priced at executable historical fills. Compounding this, the backtest engine (engine.ts) trades signed EQUITY weights with no 'option_spread' execution path, and AlignedData carries only vix/vix9d — no ^VIX3M and no ^SKEW exist in the backtest data model, so two of the four ARMED conditions (ratio3M<1.05, skew_zscore_20≥1.0) and the entire spread P&L cannot be simulated. The 35 bps expectedEdge vs 22 bps cost is therefore an unbacktested assertion, and OTM SPY put verticals cross 4 wide (3-8%) half-spreads per round trip — the spec itself admits "frictions scale with the debit," which on a 6-13% OTM vertical is a large fraction of the premium.
redteam.mitigation: To survive this lens the spec would need: (a) a historical options-chain data source (e.g., ORATS/CBOE/OptionMetrics or a synthesized BS surface from historical IV) wired into the backtest engine with an actual 'option_spread' P&L path, plus ^VIX3M and ^SKEW added to AlignedData — none of which exist today; (b) a multi-year macro fetch (period1 = 8-10y, not 1y) to capture ≥15 ARMED episodes including 2018-Q4, Mar-2020, 2022, Aug-2024; (c) widening or removing the short strike so the convex right tail is not capped exactly where crisis alpha lives (accepting more vega/cost), OR re-spec'ing the payoff to win on vol spikes (the common case) rather than only on ≥13.5% index declines (the rare case); (d) a leading rather than coincident trigger, or an explicit acceptance that the gate enters at peak IP and a backtest proving the right-tail still nets positive after IV-crush on the long leg; (e) parameter-sensitivity sweeps on the four thresholds (slope9>1.00, ratio3M<1.05, skew z≥1.0, 2-day debounce) showing the episode count and expectancy are not artifacts of a single calibration. Even with all of these, the candidate's own honesty bound (small/decaying unconditional edge, bounded orthogonality, partial re-correlation with the incumbent's mean-reversion entries) caps the upside.
redteam.residual: Thin and unproven. The only genuinely defensible residual is the defined-risk DESIGN: max loss = debit, ~0.2-0.5% NAV/episode, 3%/yr carry cap, off ~80% of the time — so the strategy cannot blow up the book, and in a slow deep grind (2022-style, or 2008) where SPY falls past 13.5% OTM, the spread does pay a multiple and would have provided real left-tail hedging on the incumbent's worst-decile days. That is a real but narrow convex-insurance profile, NOT validated crisis alpha. After this attack the edge that remains is: a capped-cost, capped-payoff insurance overlay that (i) pays only in the minority of crises that are slow/deep rather than fast/sharp, (ii) is structurally guaranteed to MISS the two highest-vol events of the last decade (Feb-2018, Aug-2024) because they round-tripped above the short strike, and (iii) cannot be backtested on PutStrike's data to size that payoff. Verdict: downgrade to "untestable as-is"; do not deploy on the strength of the regime/crisis-alpha claim.

name: Defensive Quality-minus-Junk (QMJ) Proxy — Earnings-Yield + Low-Vol Composite, Beta-Neutral L/S
family: Factor investing via ETF proxies + cross-sectional factors (value/quality/low-vol/size/momentum tilts; long-short or long-tilt; orthogonal to the incumbent short-vol put-selling and to a single-stock momentum pick)
rationale: Three Fatal red-team findings confirmed against source: data amputation (StockQuote exposes no ROE/GP/leverage, so it is a decayed defensive-value/BAB clone not QMJ), self-falsifying arithmetic (spec's own 12bps edge < 14bps cost = net-negative before scrutiny, worse on the wide-spread/hot-borrow short junk book), and an uncomputable falsifier (no historical PE/EPS panel exists — chart() returns OHLCV only — so the 120-month net-of-cost test on the value leg cannot be run); orthogonality is also false in the left tail where the short-junk leg shares the same funding-liquidity de-grossing risk as the incumbent short-vol book.
bestUseInEnsemble: Negligible as specified. The only salvageable fragment is the pure low-beta (BAB) tilt in slow grinding-bear regimes, but the spec's own H0 says the E/P tilt adds nothing beyond low-beta, and BAB itself is crowded/decayed post-2015 and shares the incumbent's left-tail funding-liquidity risk — so it would double crisis exposure rather than diversify. Do not include; if a defensive sleeve is ever wanted, build true QMJ on a real fundamentals feed (Sharadar/Compustat/FMP) with a point-in-time panel, not this proxy.

spec.name: Defensive Quality-minus-Junk (QMJ) Proxy — Earnings-Yield + Low-Vol Composite, Beta-Neutral L/S
spec.family: Factor investing via cross-sectional tilts (value/quality/low-vol composite; dollar- and beta-neutral single-name long-short; orthogonal to incumbent short-vol put-selling). NOTE: this is a PROXY for Asness-Frazzini-Pedersen QMJ, not the published factor — the profitability/growth/safety legs are unavailable (see dataSources).
spec.mechanism: Asness-Frazzini-Pedersen "Quality Minus Junk": investors systematically underpay for safe, profitable, stable cash-flow streams ("boring cash machines") and overpay for speculative high-risk "junk." A quality-minus-junk portfolio earns a premium because (a) leverage/lottery-seeking investors crowd into high-beta junk, depressing its risk-adjusted return (the Frazzini-Pedersen leverage-constraint channel), and (b) limits-to-arbitrage and flight-to-quality keep boring names persistently cheap relative to their cash-flow reliability. The economic claim is a behavioral + institutional mispricing of cash-flow quality, carrying NO vega/theta exposure — orthogonal in mechanism to the incumbent short-vol VRP harvest. HONEST CAVEAT: with only valuation (E/P) + low-risk (vol/beta/drawdown) inputs and NO fundamental quality fields, this captures the "defensive value" slice of QMJ, which overlaps heavily with Betting-Against-Beta and which the post-publication record shows has decayed (USMV 10%/yr vs SPY 15.5%/yr last decade; low-vol L/S largely eaten by turnover/fees per AQR & AlphaArchitect). The mechanism is real but the implementable proxy realizes only a fraction of it.
spec.hypothesisH0: A monthly-rebalanced, dollar- and beta-neutral long-short on the quality-proxy composite (long top-quintile, short bottom-quintile) over the ~80-name PutStrike universe produces a NET (after commissions, bid/ask, slippage, and short borrow) average return per rebalance that is statistically indistinguishable from zero (one-sided t-test, alpha=0.05, over a ≥120-month backtest), AND its information ratio is not significantly greater than that of a pure Betting-Against-Beta proxy (long low-`rolling_beta_spy_60d` / short high-beta) on the same universe — i.e., the earnings-yield tilt adds no incremental net alpha beyond low-beta.
spec.hypothesisH1: The quality-proxy L/S earns positive NET average return per rebalance (one-sided t-test rejects zero at alpha=0.05) AND delivers a net information ratio strictly greater than the BAB proxy's by a margin that survives the realistic cost stack — establishing incremental orthogonal content from the cheap-quality (E/P) tilt over pure low-beta. Spanning regression of the QMJ-proxy monthly return on {SPY excess, BAB-proxy return, incumbent short-vol P&L} yields a significantly positive intercept (alpha > 0, p < 0.05).
spec.signalDefinition:
Per stock i, monthly (last trading day), build a quality-proxy z-composite from VERIFIED PutStrike fields only. Inputs (all cross-sectionally z-scored across the eligible ~80 names that month; winsorize z at +/-3):
  EarningsYield_i = 1 / StockQuote.trailingPE_i   (E/P; set to NaN and EXCLUDE the name if trailingPE <= 0 or missing — i.e., no negative-earnings or non-priced names get a value tilt)
  Defensiveness_i = mean of [ z(-volatility_60d_i), z(-garman_klass_vol_20d_i), z(-rolling_beta_spy_60d_i) ]   (low realized vol + low beta = defensive)
  TailSafety_i    = mean of [ z(-max_drawdown_60d_i), z(-gap_frequency_20d_i), z(return_consistency_20d_i) ]    (shallow drawdowns, few gaps, steady returns = low gap/crash risk)
  ValueTilt_i     = z(EarningsYield_i)
Composite quality score:
  Q_i = 0.40*ValueTilt_i + 0.35*Defensiveness_i + 0.25*TailSafety_i
(Weights fixed a priori — value 0.40 carries the incremental-over-BAB content; defensiveness 0.35; tail-safety 0.25. These are declared, NOT tuned; any in-sample weight optimization invalidates the test.)
Rank Q_i across eligible names. LONG = top quintile (≈ top 15 of ~75-80 eligible after PE filter); SHORT = bottom quintile (≈ bottom 15, the "junk" = expensive + high-vol + high-beta + gappy). All inputs are the same fields PutStrike already computes (FEATURE_NAMES in itransformer-features.ts: volatility_60d, garman_klass_vol_20d, rolling_beta_spy_60d, max_drawdown_60d, gap_frequency_20d, return_consistency_20d) plus StockQuote.trailingPE.
spec.entryRule: Cadence: once per month, on the last trading day (EOD), to match the 21-trading-day horizon and minimize turnover cost (monthly, not daily, rebalance is deliberate — daily would multiply costs and kill the edge). Eligibility filter each month: (1) name in the ~80-stock universe; (2) trailingPE > 0 and present; (3) liquid optionable large-cap with a locatable short borrow (drop any name flagged hard-to-borrow). Construct LONG basket = top-quintile Q, SHORT basket = bottom-quintile Q. DOLLAR-NEUTRAL: long dollars = short dollars = G (gross/2). BETA-NEUTRAL: scale each leg's notional so portfolio net beta ≈ 0 using rolling_beta_spy_60d — i.e., short-leg dollars = long-leg dollars × (mean long-leg beta / mean short-leg beta); if the ratio pushes gross past the cap, scale both legs down. Within each leg, EQUAL-WEIGHT names (or beta-weight to neutralize within-leg dispersion). Submit as marketable-limit orders at the EOD mid +/- half-spread; skip any name whose quoted bid/ask spread > 25 bps (cost guard).
spec.exitRule: Hold to the next monthly rebalance (≈21 trading days), then fully reconstitute: recompute Q for all eligible names, form new top/bottom quintiles, and trade only the DIFFERENCE (names leaving a quintile are closed; names entering are opened; names persisting are merely re-weighted) to minimize turnover cost. No intra-month profit target or stop (this is a cross-sectional factor, not a single-name bet — individual-name stops would re-introduce idiosyncratic risk the neutralization is designed to remove). Two protective overrides only: (a) RISK-OFF GOVERNOR — if VIX (vix_level) > 35 at a rebalance, halve gross exposure (defensive factors can crash in violent de-grossing / quant deleveraging events, e.g. Aug 2007, Mar 2020); (b) FORCED EXIT of any single name that becomes hard-to-borrow on the short leg or loses optionability/liquidity. Full unwind of the book is a manual kill-switch only (e.g., if the falsifier below triggers in live sim).
spec.instruments: Cash equity long-short, single names, ~15 long / ~15 short, dollar- and beta-neutral, monthly rebalance. Long leg = cash equity (no options, no assignment). Short leg = borrowed shares (requires locatable short borrow; large-cap general-collateral borrow assumed ~25-50 bps/yr, but hard-to-borrow names are filtered out). NO options used — deliberately orthogonal to the incumbent put-selling book so the two return streams can be combined without doubling vol exposure. Universe: the ~80 high-liquidity optionable large-caps already in PutStrike (the optionability requirement guarantees borrow availability and tight spreads). Simulatable in the existing Neon simulated_trades schema by generalizing it to equity long/short rows (side, shares, entry/exit price, borrow accrual).
spec.positionSizing: Fixed starting capital C (e.g., $100,000). Target gross exposure G_gross = 1.0 x C (i.e., $50k long + $50k short; dollar-neutral, net ≈ $0). This is UNLEVERED at the account level (gross = capital), conservative for retail and keeps short-borrow and margin costs bounded. Per-leg dollars ≈ G_gross/2 / N_names ≈ $50,000 / 15 ≈ $3,333 per name (equal-weight, then beta-adjusted). Risk cap: scale G_gross down so that ex-ante annualized portfolio volatility (estimated from the long-short return covariance, using volatility_60d and pairwise correlations) does not exceed 8% — a market-neutral factor sleeve should be low-vol; if estimated vol > 8%, shrink gross pro-rata. Single-name cap: no name exceeds 10% of a leg. Risk-off governor (VIX>35) halves G_gross. Reserve ≥ 25% of C as cash buffer for short-leg margin variation and adverse mark-to-market. Capital-aware: at $100k with $3.3k/name and large-cap prices, most names get 10-30 shares — sufficient granularity; below ~$50k capital this strategy is impractical (lot-size discretization swamps the neutralization), which is a documented capacity floor on the SMALL side.
spec.dataSources: PARTIAL — explicitly flagged. AVAILABLE & USED (verified in source): StockQuote.trailingPE -> E/P (src/lib/yahoo-finance.ts L120); StockQuote.beta and per-stock rolling_beta_spy_60d; volatility_60d, garman_klass_vol_20d, parkinson_vol_20d; max_drawdown_60d / max_drawdown_20d, avg_gap_20d, gap_frequency_20d, return_consistency_20d, tail_ratio_20d, skewness_60d, kurtosis_60d (all in FEATURE_NAMES, itransformer-features.ts L55-135); VIX via vix_level for the risk-off governor; 10y daily OHLCV for the backtest. NOT AVAILABLE — the true QMJ Profitability/Growth/Safety legs need ROE, gross-profitability (GP/assets), earnings/cash-flow stability, leverage, accruals, payout — and the Yahoo quote object exposes ONLY {marketCap, dividendYield, beta, trailingPE, 52w hi/lo} (StockQuote interface, yahoo-finance.ts L37-51): NO returnOnEquity, profitMargin, grossProfit, totalDebt, or multi-year earnings series. Therefore the "quality" leg here is a valuation+low-risk PROXY, NOT fundamental QMJ. This is the single biggest edge-eroder and is disclosed up front. Optional cheap add (justified-but-not-required): QUAL/USMV/VLUE ETF proxies for a coarse external quality cross-check, but they cannot recover per-name fundamental quality. No new infra required.
spec.capacityNote: Capacity is NOT the binding constraint at retail — it is the OPPOSITE problem. Per-name notional (~$3.3k) is a vanishingly small fraction of large-cap ADV, so market impact is ~0 and the strategy scales to low-millions before impact matters. The binding constraints are instead: (1) TURNOVER — quintile membership on a low-vol/value composite flips ~40-60% of names per month; with ~12-18 round-trip legs/month at ~6-15 bps each, trading cost alone is ~10-14 bps/trade, which is the same order as the realistically tiny gross edge of a PROXY factor on only ~80 names; (2) SHORT BORROW — recurs every month on the short leg (~2-4 bps/month equiv on GC names, more if any name tightens); (3) REDUNDANCY/CROWDING — heavily overlaps BAB (graded down for this in the slate), and the broader low-vol/quality-value L/S is a crowded, post-2015-decayed trade (USMV < SPY last decade; AQR/AlphaArchitect: anomaly "largely eaten by turnover/fees"); (4) SMALL-side floor — below ~$50k capital, share-lot discretization breaks the dollar/beta neutralization. Net: this is the honestly WEAKEST of the three candidates — break-even-to-marginally-negative net of costs on the available proxy data — and should be scored accordingly on Edge and CostRobustness.
spec.falsifier: Run the backtest on PutStrike's own 10y daily data (trailingPE snapshots are the limiting series; where historical PE is unavailable, reconstruct E/P from trailing-EPS × shares / price). Build the monthly QMJ-proxy L/S exactly as specified, then DEDUCT the realistic cost stack each rebalance: per traded leg subtract (half-spread + 5 bps slippage) round-trip, plus monthly short-borrow accrual on the short notional. The strategy is FALSIFIED — and must be discarded, not patched — if EITHER: (A) the NET (post-cost) mean monthly long-short return is <= 0 OR its one-sided t-stat over the ≥120-month sample is < 1.65 (i.e., cannot reject H0 of zero net edge); OR (B) in a spanning regression of the net QMJ-proxy monthly return on {SPY-excess, the BAB-proxy net return, the incumbent short-vol simulated P&L}, the intercept (net alpha) is not significantly positive (p >= 0.05) OR the QMJ-proxy's net information ratio does not exceed the BAB-proxy's net IR by at least 0.15 — proving the earnings-yield tilt adds no incremental orthogonal net edge beyond low-beta. Either outcome confirms the candidate's own pre-registered concern (proxy too weak / redundant with BAB / eaten by turnover) and kills it.

redteam.lens: COSTS & CAPACITY (retail frictions: commissions, equity bid/ask spread, slippage, short borrow, min lot granularity vs fixed capital; capacity)
redteam.kill: The gross edge does not clear round-trip cost — confirmed quantitatively three ways. (1) The spec's own pre-registered numbers already kill it: expectedEdgeBpsPerTrade=12 < estCostBpsPerTrade=14, i.e. net = -2 bps before any independent scrutiny, and the capacityNote explicitly concedes "break-even-to-marginally-negative net of costs." (2) Rebuilding the cost stack from first principles using the SPEC'S OWN favorable inputs (50% monthly quintile turnover -> ~34 traded legs/month; typical 4 bps half-spread; 5 bps slippage; GC borrow ~25-50 bps/yr -> ~1.6 bps/mo of gross; SEC/TAF reg fees on sells ~0.2 bps) totals ~12.1 bps of gross per rebalance vs a 12 bps edge = net ~0. (3) The fatal blow is an asymmetry the spec waves away: the SHORT quintile is BY CONSTRUCTION the "expensive + high-vol + high-beta + gappy" junk book — exactly the names with the WIDEST equity spreads, the MOST slippage when traded in size, and borrow rates ABOVE general-collateral (web-confirmed GC ~25-50 bps/yr for the easy names like AAPL at 0.25%, but the volatile short book runs hotter). Modeling the short leg at ~9 bps half-spread + 8 bps slippage and ~80 bps/yr borrow, with the spec's high-end 60% turnover, gives ~20.5 bps round-trip cost vs 12 bps edge = net ~ -8.5 bps. Separately, the min-position-granularity attack lands: at fixed C=$100k, gross 1.0x, ~$3,333/name, mega-cap prices of $150-$1,700 yield only 3-33 whole shares/name (3-4 shares on $800-$1,700 names). Whole-share rounding averages ~290 bps of per-name notional (worst ~800 bps), which corrupts the within-leg equal-weighting AND the beta-neutralization that the ENTIRE dollar/beta-neutral market-neutral thesis depends on — re-injecting residual idiosyncratic and net-beta noise whose tracking error dwarfs a 12 bps signal. Note web checks VALIDATED the spec's input assumptions (large-cap spreads single-digit/low-double-digit bps; GC borrow ~25-50 bps/yr), so the failure is not bad inputs — it is that even honest inputs leave no daylight between edge and cost.
redteam.mitigation: For the edge to survive this lens, several things would all have to be true at once: (a) the realized gross edge of the implementable proxy would need to be materially HIGHER than 12 bps — but the spec itself documents the opposite (defensive-value/low-vol L/S has post-2015 decayed, USMV 10%/yr vs SPY 15.5%/yr, "largely eaten by turnover/fees" per AQR/AlphaArchitect), and the proxy uses only E/P + low-risk inputs with NO fundamental quality, capturing only a fraction of QMJ; (b) turnover would need to be cut hard (e.g., quarterly rebalance, wider quintile-hysteresis bands so names don't churn at the boundary, or a no-trade buffer) to drop traded-leg cost from ~10-17 bps toward ~3-5 bps; (c) the short leg would need to avoid the wide-spread/high-borrow junk names (but those ARE the alpha source on the short side, so trimming them guts the thesis); (d) capital would need to be 5-20x larger (~$0.5-2M) so $3,333->$50k+/name makes whole-share rounding negligible and restores neutralization fidelity — directly contradicting the retail $100k framing and the spec's own ~$50k small-side floor. Even with all of these, the spanning-regression bar (net IR > BAB net IR by >=0.15) is a second, independent hurdle the spec's own falment expects to fail. Realistically, none of these mitigations are available simultaneously at retail scale.
redteam.residual: Effectively none at the specified retail configuration. Best case (spec's own optimistic inputs) is net ~0 bps/rebalance; realistic case is net ~ -8.5 bps/rebalance, before even accounting for the lot-granularity degradation of neutralization. The ONE thing the spec gets right under this lens is true market-impact capacity: at ~$3.3k/name vs large-cap ADV, impact is ~0 and the sleeve scales to low-millions — so the failure is NOT a capacity/impact constraint, it is a turnover-cost-vs-tiny-edge constraint plus a small-capital lot-granularity constraint. Any residual edge is statistically indistinguishable from zero and would be swamped by the cost stack; the strategy meets its own pre-registered falsifier (A: net mean <=0 / t<1.65). Recommend discard on Edge and CostRobustness, consistent with the spec's self-assessment as "the honestly WEAKEST of the three candidates."

redteam.lens: DATA-SNOOPING, DECAY & ORTHOGONALITY — (a) is the mechanism real or a decayed/known-arbitraged anomaly, and (b) is the stream truly orthogonal to short-vol put-selling or correlated short-vol/beta in disguise?
redteam.kill: The strategy fails BOTH halves of this lens. (a) DECAY: The parent QMJ mechanism (Asness-Frazzini-Pedersen, 2013/2019) is genuinely robust — significant in 23/24 countries, behaviorally + leverage-constraint grounded — so it is NOT a pure curve-fit. But this spec does NOT implement QMJ; dataSources confirms StockQuote (yahoo-finance.ts L36-51, verified) exposes only {marketCap, dividendYield, beta, trailingPE} with NO returnOnEquity/grossProfit/profitMargin/totalDebt. So the profitability/growth/safety legs — the parts of QMJ that are MOST robust and LEAST ETF-replicated — are amputated. What remains is exactly the 'defensive-value + low-vol' slice that the post-publication record shows has decayed the most: McLean-Pontiff (2016) document ~58% average post-publication anomaly decay (74% value-weighted in follow-ups), and QMJ/low-vol were published and ETF-ized (USMV/QUAL) well before any 2016-2026 backtest window. The low-vol anomaly specifically shows post-2015 crowding acceleration correlated with ETF inflows (arXiv 2512.11913) and USMV trailing SPY over the last decade — the spec concedes this itself. (b) ORTHOGONALITY: The claim 'no vega/theta, orthogonal in mechanism to short-vol VRP' is true contractually but FALSE in realized covariance, which is what the spanning regression actually tests. The SHORT leg is defined as 'expensive + high-vol + high-beta + gappy junk' — i.e., the high-beta/lottery/high-convexity basket. Shorting it IS a short-volatility/short-convexity position: it bleeds in calm melt-ups and crashes in violent de-grossing (Aug 2007 quant meltdown, Mar 2020 — the spec's own VIX>35 governor is an admission of this). A short-vol put-selling book bleeds in those SAME left-tail de-grossing events. So in the tail — which dominates both a short-vol P&L's variance and the spanning-regression covariance — the QMJ-proxy and the incumbent are POSITIVELY correlated, not orthogonal. The diversification benefit the whole thesis rests on evaporates precisely when it is needed. Finally, the spec self-falsifies on arithmetic before the orthogonality test is even reached: expectedEdgeBpsPerTrade=12 < estCostBpsPerTrade=14, i.e. NET NEGATIVE, which trips falsifier branch (A) (net mean <= 0, t-stat < 1.65) by the author's own honest admission ('honestly WEAKEST of the three... break-even-to-marginally-negative net of costs').
redteam.mitigation: For survival it would need ALL of: (1) Recover the actual QMJ fundamental legs (ROE, gross-profitability/assets, accruals, leverage, earnings stability) via a real fundamentals feed (Compustat/Sharadar/FMP) — without these it is a defensive-value/BAB proxy, not QMJ, and is the decayed slice. (2) Demonstrate, with a tail-conditional analysis (not just full-sample beta), that the short-junk leg's return is NOT positively correlated with the incumbent short-vol P&L during the worst short-vol months/de-grossing events — i.e., prove the streams diverge in the left tail, not just on average. A full-sample low correlation is insufficient because short-vol risk lives in the tail. (3) Show net-of-cost edge that is positive at all (current 12bps edge < 14bps cost is fatal on its own) and survives the spanning intercept (alpha>0, p<0.05) AND beats BAB IR by >=0.15. (4) Widen the universe beyond ~80 names — quintiles of 15 on a decayed proxy give too few independent bets for the t-stat to clear 1.65 net of turnover. Absent (1)+(2), the lens-specific failure stands regardless of cost engineering.
redteam.residual: Essentially none on the implementable proxy. After this attack the only residual is the theoretical/academic QMJ premium that the spec admittedly cannot access (no fundamental fields), plus possibly a thin BAB/low-beta tilt — but BAB is itself crowded/decayed post-2015 and the spec's own H0 says the E/P tilt adds no incremental alpha beyond low-beta. The spec's pre-registered falsifier ('proxy too weak / redundant with BAB / eaten by turnover') is exactly what this lens confirms. The honest self-assessment is correct: this is the weakest candidate, net-negative on available data, and its diversification claim against the short-vol book is overstated because both books share left-tail de-grossing risk. Discard, do not patch — patching requires a fundamentals data source the project does not have, which is new infra the spec explicitly says it avoids.

redteam.lens: REGIME & ROBUSTNESS (regime dependence, parameter sensitivity, crowding, tail risk; survival through 2018-Q4, Mar-2020, 2022, Aug-2024)
redteam.kill:
The strategy's long-edge mechanism IS the crash mechanism. The spec leans on the Frazzini-Pedersen leverage-constraint channel: low-beta/defensive names are persistently cheap because constrained, leverage-seeking investors crowd into high-beta junk. But that same channel reverses violently exactly when funding liquidity tightens — AlphaArchitect/F-P confirm BAB "realizes negative returns during times of tightening funding liquidity constraints," i.e. precisely the de-grossing tail events (Aug-2007 quant quake, Mar-2020, Aug-2024) the strategy needs to avoid. So the book carries hidden short-tail/short-correlation exposure: it earns a small premium in calm/low-vol regimes and gives it back in a few violent quant-deleveraging events. That is the opposite of orthogonality to the incumbent short-vol VRP book — both are short the same realized-stress factor and will draw down TOGETHER, so combining them does NOT diversify; it doubles the crisis exposure the spec claims to avoid.

Regime-by-regime survival, the four named windows: (1) 2018-Q4 — a low-vol/rate-shock selloff; defensive long-leg likely cushions but the E/P value tilt (40% of the composite, the only thing claimed to beat BAB) had no consistent value premium then and the net edge is ~break-even, so it neither helps nor clears costs. (2) Mar-2020 — low-vol gave only "mediocre protection" in the crash and then BADLY LAGGED the V-shaped growth-led recovery (Man Group, BNP, Morningstar); worse, the long E/P value leg was crushed in the 2020 growth rally while the short "expensive junk" leg (high-multiple growth) ripped higher — the L/S loses on BOTH legs in the rebound. This is a serious-to-fatal drawdown window. (3) 2022 — the single regime that should favor value/low-vol (rising rates, growth derating); even here Novy-Marx/Velikov (JFE Jan-2022) show BAB's apparent premium is an artifact of small-cap/equal-weighting and transaction costs, and on an ~80-name large-cap universe with 40-60% monthly turnover the realizable edge is thin. (4) Aug-2024 — FATAL governor failure: the VIX>35 risk-off governor is checked only at the MONTHLY rebalance, but the carry-trade unwind spiked VIX above 60 INTRADAY on Aug-5 and largely reverted within days (BIS Bulletin 90).
