A month-end snapshot would have seen a near-normal VIX and never halved gross; the book ate the indiscriminate, deleveraging-driven correlation spike (BIS: implied correlations jumped from systematic deleveraging, not fundamentals — exactly when single-name market-neutral neutralization fails) at full size.

Crowding/redundancy: the spec's own falsifier requires the E/P tilt to beat BAB by IR>=0.15, but academic work (arXiv 1510.01679) shows low-vol P&L is already strongly correlated with the E/P factor — they are not orthogonal, so H1's "incremental content beyond low-beta" is the thing most likely to fail its own pre-registered test. The trade is also a documented, post-2015-decayed, crowded factor (USMV<SPY last decade) that the spec admits is "break-even-to-marginally-negative net of costs."

Parameter/tail fragility: the a-priori 0.40/0.35/0.25 weights are asserted not tuned, but the entire incremental-alpha claim rests on the 0.40 value weight that is the least robust leg; the 8% vol cap and beta-neutralization are estimated from trailing 60d vol/beta that themselves spike and decorrelate in crises (neutralization is computed on stale pre-crisis betas), so beta-neutrality silently breaks at the worst moment. gap_frequency_20d in the composite is backward-looking and won't flag a name about to gap.
redteam.mitigation: For the edge to survive this lens it would need: (1) an INTRADAY or daily VIX/credit-spread/funding-stress trigger for the risk-off governor (not a month-end snapshot), plus a hard de-gross rule on realized-correlation spikes — otherwise it is structurally exposed to fast deleveraging events like Aug-2024; (2) genuine REAL fundamental quality fields (ROE, GP/assets, leverage, earnings stability) to make it true QMJ rather than a defensive-value/BAB clone — the published full QMJ is materially more robust in crises than the low-vol-only proxy, but those fields are absent from the StockQuote interface (verified: yahoo-finance.ts L36-51 has no returnOnEquity/grossProfit/totalDebt/forwardPE); (3) explicit proof via spanning regression that crisis-window returns are NOT just short-correlation/short-funding-liquidity beta in disguise; (4) a point-in-time 10-year EPS panel to even RUN the falsifying backtest — which the codebase does not have (no historical PE series; trailingPE is a single live snapshot), so the pre-registered ≥120-month net-of-cost t-test is currently uncomputable as written. None of these mitigations are available within PutStrike's current data, so the spec cannot be repaired without new infrastructure it explicitly disclaims needing.
redteam.residual: Essentially none that is robust. After this lens, what remains is a small, regime-conditional premium that is (a) positive only in calm, trending, low-vol bull tape, (b) negative or badly lagging in exactly the four named stress windows (Mar-2020 and Aug-2024 are the most damaging; 2018-Q4 and 2022 are break-even at best after costs/turnover), and (c) short the same funding-liquidity/correlation tail as the incumbent short-vol book, so it adds tail risk rather than diversification. The only conceivably durable residual is the pure low-beta (BAB) component in slow grinding-bear regimes like 2022 — but the spec's whole thesis is that the E/P tilt adds value BEYOND BAB, and that incremental piece is the least regime-robust and most crowded part. Net: the proxy fails its own falsifier under realistic costs, and even if it marginally passed in-sample it would not survive a crisis-conditioned out-of-sample test. Discard, do not patch — consistent with the spec's own pre-registered kill conditions.

name: Event-Vol Term-Structure RV (vega-balanced calendar around scheduled earnings)
rationale: Self-conceded net -290 bps (130 gross edge vs 420 cost), with two independent retail frictions (4-leg single-name straddle spread crossed twice ~16 crossings + $0.65x16 commission) each individually exceeding the gross edge; the only cost-surviving universe (~10 mega-caps) is mutually exclusive with the signal-validity universe (80-name cross-sectional deciles), and the repo verifiably lacks the earnings/options history to ever run the prescribed falsifier (CLAUDE.md explicitly rejected Yahoo earnings dates for insufficient history; costs.ts is equity-calibrated with no option bid/ask model; black-scholes.ts is put-only).
bestUseInEnsemble: The genuinely orthogonal idea here is vega-neutral term-structure SLOPE as a low-correlation diversifier to the incumbent short-vol/put book; if anything survives, harvest only the curveCorrelation()-validated slope signal as a tiny (<=2-3% capital) calm-regime sleeve on the ~10 tightest mega-caps, executed as single net-priced spreads with a long-wing gap cap and a VIX>35/inverted-term-structure kill-switch — but treat it as a forward-only research probe, not a deployable contributor, since its edge is unprovable on existing data and net-negative at retail frictions.

spec.name: Event-Vol Term-Structure RV (vega-balanced calendar around scheduled earnings)
spec.family: Earnings & event-driven (event-vol term-structure relative value; deliberately vega-neutral, off the short-vol axis)
spec.mechanism: Behavioral/structural mispricing of the SHAPE (slope) of the single-name IV term structure around a scheduled earnings catalyst, harvested market- and vega-neutral. By variance additivity, the ATM straddle in the first expiry that SPANS earnings (E1) prices ambient variance over its life PLUS one discrete event-variance chunk; the nearest expiry that does NOT span earnings (E0) prices ambient variance only. Backing out m_event = sqrt(max(0, T1*sigma1^2 - T0*sigma0^2)) isolates how richly the market prices THIS event. Retail/dealer flow systematically over- or under-bids near-dated event protection relative to ambient/longer-dated vol; the edge is mean-reversion of that event-premium RICHNESS, captured as a calendar (short the rich expiry, long the cheap expiry) tuned to ~zero net vega so P&L is driven by term-structure slope re-pricing, not by the level of vol falling. The vega-balance is the explicit, load-bearing lever that keeps the return stream off the incumbent's 30-45 DTE short-vol/VRP axis. NOTE: a naive long-straddle-into-the-IV-ramp is NOT edge because in variance space total variance falls as ambient days are consumed; only the event-premium term-structure mispricing is.
spec.hypothesisH0: Cross-sectionally, event-premium richness R carries no information about subsequent vega-neutral calendar-spread P&L: after realistic single-name multi-leg options bid/ask + commissions, the mean net P&L of the top-decile-R and bottom-decile-R calendar baskets is statistically indistinguishable from zero and from each other (the slope re-prices no more than fair, and the structure's edge is fully eaten by the four-leg options spread).
spec.hypothesisH1: Names in the top decile of R (event rich) systematically exhibit a post-event front-leg IV collapse larger than the ambient/back-leg move, so a vega-balanced calendar that is net-short the event-spanning front leg earns a positive gross slope P&L (and bottom decile the reverse), with a top-minus-bottom decile spread large enough to clear costs on at least the most liquid sub-universe.
spec.signalDefinition: Inputs from CURRENT chains only (zero earnings history), per name with a known next-earnings date D_E from getStockContext().earningsDate (daysToEarnings = d_E). Using OptionContract.impliedVolatility at the ATM strike (|strike/underlyingPrice - 1| minimized, require both bid>0 and ask>0 and openInterest>=250 on the ATM put AND call of each leg): let E1 = first expiry with expiration > D_E (spans earnings, T1 yrs = dte/365), E0 = nearest expiry with expiration < D_E that still has >=2 trading days left (does NOT span, T0 yrs), E2 = a later ambient expiry with 28-45 DTE (sigma_ambient = its ATM IV). sigma1 = mean(ATM put IV, ATM call IV) at E1; sigma0 likewise at E0; sigma_amb at E2. Event-implied move m_event = sqrt(max(0, T1*sigma1^2 - T0*sigma0^2)). Fair single-event move proxy m_fair = k * sigma_amb * sqrt(1/252), with k = universe-median realized-to-implied event-move ratio calibrated cross-sectionally each day (default k=1.0 until calibrated; k is a single shared constant, NOT per-name fit). Event-richness R = m_event / m_fair. Term-structure slope diagnostic slope = sigma1 - sigma_amb (must be > 0, i.e. genuine front kink, else skip). Cross-sectional gate: compute R for all ~80 names with valid D_E within 3<=d_E<=8 trading days and rank into deciles daily.
spec.entryRule: Once per day at EOD. Eligible name requires ALL of: (1) valid next-earnings date with 3 <= daysToEarnings <= 8 trading days AND an expiry strictly straddling it; (2) earnings-date validation passes the dual-confirmation layer (see dataSources) — yahoo earningsTimestamp is a known-fragile placeholder, so the date is accepted ONLY if it agrees with a Wikipedia-pageview attention z-score spike window and the front-kink condition slope>0 is consistent with an event sitting between E0 and E1; ambiguous/placeholder dates are DROPPED, not traded; (3) all four traded contracts (ATM put+call at E1 and E2) have bid>0, ask>0, openInterest>=250, and per-leg quoted bid/ask <= 12% of mid (hard liquidity gate — this is what makes or breaks cost survival); (4) R in top decile (event RICH) -> SELL the E1 ATM straddle, BUY a vega-matched fraction n_B of the E2 ATM straddle (net SHORT the event leg); OR R in bottom decile (event CHEAP) -> the reverse (long E1, short E2). n_B = vega(E1 straddle)/vega(E2 straddle) using black-scholes.ts putGreeks vega (ATM straddle vega ~= 2x ATM put vega), so net portfolio vega ~ 0 by construction. Cap to the 25 most liquid names by ATM dollar-OI to keep spreads tight. Skip if slope <= 0.
spec.exitRule: Close the entire structure on the first trading day AFTER the confirmed earnings release (target T+1 EOD), capturing the front-leg event-IV crush and the slope re-pricing; hard exit no later than 1 day before E0 expiry to avoid the short ambient leg going into expiration. Early-close triggers: (a) if realized slope re-prices to within 0.5 vol-pt of ambient before earnings (mean-reversion already paid) -> close for profit; (b) hard stop if the structure's mark-to-market loss exceeds 1.5x the net debit/credit collected at entry (defined-risk backstop); (c) if the validated earnings date moves or is retracted intraday-of-confirmation, close immediately (the 'which expiry spans the event' assumption is voided). Never hold the short front leg through a second, unmodeled catalyst.
spec.instruments: Defined-risk single-name option double-calendar / diagonal on ~80 high-liquidity optionable large-caps (the existing PutStrike universe). Concretely: short (or long) the earnings-week ATM straddle in E1 vs a vega-matched ATM straddle in E2 (28-45 DTE), net vega ~0 so it is a term-structure SLOPE bet, max loss bounded by the structure + the 1.5x-net stop. Instrument class = option_spread in the Strategy interface (types.ts); recorded as paired legs in simulated_trades (Neon). Restricted to the 25 tightest-spread names per day; NEVER implemented as a naked short front straddle (that collapses the return stream back onto the incumbent short-vol axis and forfeits the entire orthogonality claim).
spec.positionSizing: Fixed-capital, defined-risk, tiny per name. Per-trade max loss = 1.5x net debit collected, capped at 1.0% of total equity; size the number of double-calendars so that (1.5 x net_debit_per_unit x units) <= 0.01 x equity. Hard caps: <= 6 concurrent event-RV positions, <= 6% of equity gross max-loss-at-risk across the book at any time, <= 1 position per underlying. Allocate the daily slot budget to the most extreme |R - median| names first within the 25-name liquid set. Reserve cash for the net debit; treat short-leg assignment risk as zero-tolerance by closing at T+1 (never carry a short option through the weekend post-earnings). This sits ALONGSIDE the incumbent put book, capped so the event-RV sleeve is <= 10% of total deployed capital until it proves out of sample.
spec.dataSources: All free/daily already in repo: yahoo-finance2 full multi-expiry options chains (OptionContract: impliedVolatility, bid, ask, openInterest, volume per strike/expiry) for sigma0/sigma1/sigma_amb and the liquidity gates; black-scholes.ts (impliedVolatility solver as IV cross-check + putGreeks.vega for the vega-balance ratio n_B; note: engine is currently put-only, so call/straddle vega is computed as ~2x ATM put vega or via a thin call-parity wrapper to be added); getStockContext() earningsDate/daysToEarnings (yahoo quoteSummary calendarEvents) for D_E. FRAGILE INPUT = earnings-date TIMING: the code takes calendarEvents.earnings.earningsDate[0] with NO AMC/BMO disambiguation and yahoo earningsTimestamp can be a placeholder/estimate — a wrong date silently corrupts which expiry 'spans' the event and inverts the trade. Required validation layer (cheap, already-available data): accept D_E only if (a) it falls inside yahoo's earningsDate min-max range, (b) a Wikipedia-pageview attention z-score (wikipedia.ts feature already engineered) spikes in the [D_E-2, D_E+1] window, and (c) the front-kink slope = sigma1 - sigma_amb > 0 is present (the options market itself is pricing an event between E0 and E1). Drop the name if any of the three disagree. Costs from backtest/costs.ts DEFAULT_COSTS (optionCommissionPerContract=$0.65) PLUS an explicit single-name straddle bid/ask model (the equity 6 bps assumption is irrelevant here; options spread is the binding cost).
spec.capacityNote: Capacity at retail size is NOT the binding constraint — per-trade size is tiny ($1-2k gross premium) and only ~25 liquid names qualify on any earnings-dense day, so a retail book never moves these markets. The binding constraint is the OPPOSITE: per-trade COST, not capacity. The structure trades FOUR option legs (ATM put+call in two expiries) round-trip = ~8-12 contract crossings, and single-name ATM straddle bid/ask runs ~1.5-4% of premium PER side even on the most liquid names (vs the 6 bps equity assumption in DEFAULT_COSTS, which is inapplicable). Honest math (S=150, 8-DTE front spanning earnings vs 35-DTE ambient, sigma_amb=0.32, 5% implied event move): generous gross slope edge ~130 bps of gross premium deployed; realistic round-trip cost ~330 bps (1.5% half-spread, best case) to ~830 bps (4% half-spread, illiquid) -> NET roughly -140 to -700 bps per trade. The orthogonality claim is the strongest in the family and is real (vega-neutral slope P&L is genuinely low-correlation to a delta-hedged short-straddle book), but it is bought at a multi-leg options-spread cost that the gross edge does not clear at retail frictions. Viable ONLY if restricted to the ~10 tightest-spread mega-caps AND executed as a single net-priced spread order (one bid/ask, not four), which materially cuts the crossing cost; otherwise it is cost-negative.
spec.falsifier: Run the no-look-ahead backtest/live-sim on the ~80-name universe over all available earnings events: for every eligible name, log m_event, R, the entry net debit/credit, and the realized close-at-T+1 P&L NET of DEFAULT_COSTS commissions AND a single-name straddle bid/ask charged at the per-leg quoted (ask-bid)/mid actually observed in the chain (no idealized spread). REFUTED if ANY of: (1) the top-decile-R minus bottom-decile-R mean NET calendar P&L per trade is <= 0 OR not statistically > 0 at p<0.05 (the slope mispricing does not exist or does not revert in the window); (2) median per-trade NET P&L across all eligible trades is negative AND the 25-tightest-spread restriction does not flip it positive (cost dominates — the realistic-cost ~420 bps exceeds the ~130 bps gross edge, the expected outcome); (3) the realized return stream's correlation to the incumbent short-vol/put-selling equity curve (curveCorrelation in metrics.ts) exceeds 0.4 (orthogonality claim fails — it is short-vol in disguise, which happens if the vega-balance is not actually held); or (4) >20% of traded events had the earnings date subsequently proven wrong (AMC/BMO or placeholder), i.e. the dual-confirmation validation layer fails to keep the 'which expiry spans the event' assumption correct. Any one of these kills the spec.

redteam.lens: COSTS & CAPACITY (retail frictions: option bid/ask, commissions, slippage, assignment, granularity vs fixed capital)
redteam.kill: The gross edge does not clear round-trip cost, and the spec admits it: expectedEdgeBpsPerTrade=130 vs estCostBpsPerTrade=420, i.e. net ~ -290 bps before even stressing it. The structure is 4 option legs (ATM put+call at E1 and E2), so round-trip is ~16 contract crossings. Web-checked spreads confirm the spec's own numbers are not pessimistic: even SPY, the single most liquid underlier, shows ~2.4% full-chain option spread (~1.2% half); single-name earnings-week ATM straddles run 1.5-4% half-spread PER leg PER side. On the spec's own scenario (S=150, ~$13 gross premium per vega-matched unit), spread cost alone is ~330 bps (best case, tightest mega-caps) to ~830 bps (illiquid) round-trip -> net -200 to -700 bps. Worse, the spec UNDER-counts the fixed per-contract commission: backtest/costs.ts DEFAULT_COSTS optionCommissionPerContract=$0.65, and 8 contracts x 2 sides = $10.40 of commission per 1-lot unit. Against the spec's own 'tiny $1-2k gross premium' sizing that is another ~500-775 bps of pure fixed drag that the 420 bps estimate appears to omit. Two independent costs (multi-leg single-name spread crossed twice + fixed commission on 16 crossings) each individually exceed the 130 bps gross edge. The T+1 forced exit the morning after earnings is also the widest-spread, lowest-liquidity moment of the option's life, so the realized exit spread is worse than the entry 12% liquidity gate (which is measured only at entry).
redteam.mitigation: For survival you would need ALL of: (1) execute each calendar as a SINGLE net-priced 4-leg spread order (one bid/ask), not four separate crossings - the spec names this but it is aspirational on single-name earnings-week options where MMs widen net-spread quotes precisely into the catalyst; (2) restrict to the ~10 tightest mega-caps where half-spreads approach the 1.2% SPY floor - but this collapses the universe and breaks the signal (see residual edge); (3) trade enough size that the fixed $0.65 x 16 = $10.40 commission is a small fraction of premium, i.e. NOT the 'tiny $1-2k' the positionSizing mandates - there is a direct conflict between the 1% max-loss/tiny-size rule and commission amortization; (4) a measured gross edge materially above 130 bps - the falsifier's own criterion (2) flags the realistic ~420 bps cost as exceeding it. The honest path is to treat this as REFUTED-by-design (the spec's capacityNote and falsifier #2 already predict the negative outcome) unless a backtest on the ~10-name liquid subset, costed at the actually-observed per-leg (ask-bid)/mid AND full $0.65/contract commission on all 16 crossings, empirically flips median net P&L positive. Default skepticism says it will not.
redteam.residual: Minimal-to-none at retail frictions. The only place edge could survive is the ~10 tightest-spread mega-caps executed as single net-priced spreads - but that escape hatch is self-defeating for THIS signal: the entry logic is a CROSS-SECTIONAL decile rank of event-richness R across ~80 names (top decile SELL event leg, bottom decile reverse). You cannot form a statistically robust top-vs-bottom decile spread from ~10 names - the decile gate degenerates to ~1 name per side, destroying the diversification and the p<0.05 top-minus-bottom test that falsifier #1 requires. So the cost-survival universe and the signal-validity universe are mutually exclusive. Capacity per se is genuinely NOT the binding constraint (retail size never moves these markets, and the spec correctly identifies this) - the binding constraint is per-trade cost, and on that axis the structure is cost-negative by its own arithmetic. The orthogonality/vega-neutrality claim is real and the cleverest part of the family, but it is purchased with a 4-leg single-name options spread that the 130 bps gross slope edge cannot pay for. Net: no durable retail edge survives the costs lens.

redteam.lens: DATA-SNOOPING, DECAY & ORTHOGONALITY
redteam.kill: (a) The mechanism is real but is a 15-year-old published, widely-arbitraged anomaly, and (b) the orthogonality claim does not survive scrutiny — it is short-vol in disguise. On decay: the core mechanism (slope of the single-name IV term structure predicting option returns) IS Vasquez (2017, JFQA), sample 1996-2007, ~27% gross / ~12% net monthly — i.e. a heavily-cited factor whose sample ends ~19 years before today, squarely in the post-publication-decay danger zone. The earnings-specific 'front-month event vol is rich and gets crushed' version is textbook practitioner knowledge (ORATS, tastytrade, SteadyOptions) — the single most-traded, most-crowded options-around-earnings idea in retail. The spec's OWN honest math concedes gross ~130 bps vs realistic round-trip cost ~330-830 bps -> NET -140 to -700 bps; it admits the edge is cost-negative except in ~10 mega-caps executed as a single net-priced spread, which is a knife-edge, not a strategy. On orthogonality (the load-bearing claim): the source factor itself lives in the variance-risk-premium family — the closely-related Cao/Vasquez/Xiao literature ties term-structure slope directly to the equity VRP and delta-hedged option returns, so 'off the short-vol axis' is false at the factor level. Worse, the structure is net-SHORT the event-spanning front leg, whose dominant exposure is the discrete event-variance crush — collecting the event VRP IS short-vol, merely concentrated into one day. The vega-neutrality is only a STATIC black-scholes ATM-vega match at entry between a short-dated event leg and a 28-45 DTE ambient leg sitting at very different points on the vol-of-vol surface; it neutralizes vega LEVEL but not the event-crush convexity, so realized P&L correlation to a short-straddle/put-selling book will leak well above the spec's own 0.4 orthogonality threshold. Finally, the falsifier is effectively unrunnable for data-snooping defense: per the repo's own CLAUDE.md, Yahoo earnings dates return only the last 4 quarters and were REJECTED as a data source for insufficient history, and the repo holds no historical options-chain archive — so the prescribed no-look-ahead historical backtest cannot be executed, leaving only a multi-year forward live-sim that cannot reach the decile-spread/p<0.05 bar in any reasonable time. An edge that is (i) published and old, (ii) net-negative at retail frictions by the author's own admission, (iii) not actually orthogonal to short-vol, and (iv) cannot be out-of-sample validated, fails this lens on all four counts.
redteam.mitigation: For decay: provide a genuine post-2015 out-of-sample test on real historical single-name chains (e.g., OptionMetrics/ORATS history, which the repo does NOT have) showing the top-minus-bottom-decile R spread is still positive AND statistically significant in the most recent 3-5 years specifically, not just the full 1996-2007-style window. For orthogonality: replace the static entry-time BS ATM-vega match with a live, marked vega- AND event-variance-neutral hedge, and empirically demonstrate realized daily-return correlation to the incumbent put/short-straddle equity curve stays <0.2 (not <0.4) across a full earnings cycle of real fills — the burden is to show the event-crush convexity, not just vega level, is hedged. For costs: prove on actual observed per-leg (ask-bid)/mid in the chain (no idealized spread) that restricting to the ~10 tightest mega-caps AND single net-priced spread execution flips median net P&L positive; the spec already states this is the ONLY viable regime, so it must be demonstrated, not asserted. For falsifiability: the falsifier as written cannot fire on the repo's data; it must be re-specified against a dataset that actually exists, or the strategy is untestable and should not be deployed.
redteam.residual: Thin and probably illusory at retail frictions. After the attack, the only conceivably-surviving residue is a tiny, low-capacity sliver: the ~10 most liquid mega-caps where the front-expiry event-richness R is in an extreme tail AND the four-leg structure is executed as a single net-priced spread order, where the slope re-pricing might marginally exceed the (now-minimized) crossing cost. But even there: (1) the gross edge is ~130 bps against a best-case ~330 bps cost by the author's own figures, so it needs the spread-order execution to cut crossing cost by >60% just to break even; (2) any surviving P&L is, by mechanism, the event variance risk premium concentrated into the front leg — i.e. it is short-vol return, so it does NOT deliver the diversification/orthogonality that is the entire stated reason for the sleeve to exist alongside the put book; and (3) it cannot be validated out-of-sample on the repo's data. Net: no durable, orthogonal, cost-clearing edge survives. The honest read is that the spec is best treated as a falsified hypothesis the author has already half-conceded (expectedEdge 130 < estCost 420), not a deployable strategy.

redteam.lens: REGIME & ROBUSTNESS (regime dependence, parameter sensitivity, crowding, tail risk; survival through 2018-Q4 / Mar-2020 / 2022 / Aug-2024)
redteam.kill: The spec neutralizes VEGA but is silent on GAMMA/gap convexity, and it deliberately holds the structure THROUGH a discrete catalyst while net-SHORT the near-dated event-spanning leg in the top-decile (rich-R) case. That is a structurally short-gamma, short-tail bet whose P&L is concave in the realized earnings move. The documented edge it harvests is exactly the 70-75% of events where implied > realized; the entire offsetting loss is paid in the 25-30% fat-tail gap events, which CLUSTER in the named crisis windows. Web evidence is explicit that a single large earnings gap 'can render the calendar nearly worthless even if IV crush works as expected' and 'a single 1.5% move can wipe the entire debit' due to front-month gamma. In Mar-2020 term structures inverted market-wide and single-name realized moves dwarfed pre-event implied (rich R was rich for a reason) -> the short-event leg is run over; in 2018-Q4 and 2022 outsized single-name earnings gaps (META -26%, NFLX -35%, etc.) hit the short-event trades at near-max loss; in Aug-2024 vol-of-vol spiked and both the 0.5-vol-pt early-close trigger and the Wikipedia dual-confirmation date layer degrade. The 1.5x-net-debit 'hard stop' and 'defined-risk' claim are illusory across an overnight earnings gap or crisis open — the mark gaps PAST the stop because the position is held into the catalyst by design, so worst-case loss is realized as a jump, not a catchable MTM. This is regime-conditional failure coincident with drawdowns: low correlation in calm markets, correlation->1 in the crisis, i.e. the picking-up-pennies signature, not robustness. Compounding it, the spec ITSELF concedes net P&L of -140 to -700 bps after costs, rescued only by restricting to ~10 mega-caps — the single most crowded corner of event-vol RV — whose slope edge compresses below cost in benign regimes and whose option spreads widen 2-4x (tripping the 12% liquidity gate at the worst marks) when the crowd de-grosses in a crisis. The cost model in the repo (DEFAULT_COSTS, equity 6bps) is calm-market-calibrated with no crisis-widening term, and m_event=sqrt(max(0,.)) silently floors away the inverted-term-structure crisis observations, biasing any backtest optimistic.
redteam.mitigation: To survive, the spec would need: (1) an explicit gamma/jump-risk budget and a realized-move stress test showing the top-minus-bottom decile NET P&L stays positive when the worst 10-20% of single-name earnings gaps (calibrated to 2018-Q4/Mar-2020/2022/Aug-2024 magnitudes) are forced into the sample — not just average behavior; (2) abandon the 'hard stop' as tail control and instead bound loss with an actual long wing (turn the short-event calendar into a defined-risk fly/condor so the gap cannot exceed a known cap), accepting the lower edge; (3) a crisis-state cost/liquidity model (option half-spreads at 2-4x, gate behavior when ≥12% is breached portfolio-wide) and proof the mega-cap-only restriction still clears cost net of that; (4) a regime gate that stands the sleeve DOWN when VIX/term-structure is inverted or VIX>35 (the strategy should not trade in exactly the regimes that produce its tail), with the backtest re-run to show the standdown does not eliminate the edge; (5) remove the max(0,.) floor or report how many observations it discards, since those ARE the tail; (6) fix the date feed (AMC/BMO disambiguation in yahoo-finance.ts) and quantify falsifier-4 wrong-date rate out-of-sample, because a wrong date inverts which leg is short and turns a hedged trade into a naked short-gamma one.
redteam.residual: Thin and regime-restricted. The orthogonality property (vega-neutral slope P&L being low-correlation to a short-vol/put-selling book) is the genuinely strongest claim and is plausibly real in CALM, low-vol regimes — but that is precisely the picking-up-pennies profile: diversifying most of the time, then correlating into the drawdown. After a hard regime standdown (no trading when term structure inverts / VIX>35) and a real long-wing cap on the gap, what remains is a small, capacity-tiny, calm-regime-only behavioral slope-mispricing harvest on ~10 mega-caps whose gross edge (~130 bps) is at or below realistic multi-leg options frictions (~330+ bps) unless executed as a single net-priced spread order — and even then crowding in that exact corner keeps it marginal. Net: a defensible research curiosity / tiny alongside sleeve at <=2-3% capital with a strict crisis kill-switch, NOT a robust standalone edge. As specified (held through the catalyst, stop-based tail control, calm-market costs), it does not clear the regime-and-robustness bar.

name: Regime-Gated SPY/QQQ ATM Put Calendar (Long-Vega Contango Harvest)
family: Volatility-risk-premium harvesting BEYOND naked puts (defined-risk + orthogonal-premium vol selling)
rationale: Three independent Fatal findings, all verified against the code: (1) the core orthogonality mechanism has a backwards sign — a short-front/long-back put calendar is negative-gamma and loses on vol-spike days (front IV explodes more than back in a front-loaded inversion), so it would fail its own 7-of-10-VIX-jump falsifier and is correlated to, not a hedge for, the CSP book exactly when it matters; (2) gross edge (~150-250 bps) sits inside the spec's own cost band (220-347 bps), negative on QQQ, with the spec conceding it fails as standalone alpha; (3) the falsifier is uninstrumentable here — data.ts is OHLCV-only with no historical option chains, engine.ts is a pure equity-share simulator with no option_spread/multi-leg P&L path (would undercharge option costs ~20x), and no incumbent CSP daily equity curve exists in the 5-strategy registry to run curveCorrelation against. The signal also miscites its sole reused feature: row[163] is the 5-day momentum of VIX3M/VIX, not the slope level the gate needs, and the 1.087/1.20/0.92 knobs are underived. Mitigations (build IV-surface history, multi-leg cost path, CSP curve; SPY-only; >$250k account; replace the structure with a positive-gamma overlay) are neither cheap nor real, and the last one is a different strategy with guaranteed-negative carry.
bestUseInEnsemble: Almost none as specified. The only salvageable kernel is the uncontested fact that VIX is in contango ~75-85% of the time, but the proposed vehicle captures that decayed/crowded premium non-orthogonally and bleeds in the tail it claims to hedge. If left-tail diversification of the CSP book is the real goal, the ensemble needs a genuinely positive-gamma AND positive-vega overlay (long front-dated puts / put backspread) that accepts negative theta in calm — a fundamentally different strategy from this theta-positive calendar — and even that should only be pursued after a real option-chain/IV-surface dataset and a multi-leg P&L path are built into the engine so the hedge value can actually be measured.

spec.name: Regime-Gated SPY/QQQ ATM Put Calendar (Long-Vega Contango Harvest)
spec.family: Volatility term-structure / roll-yield harvesting (defined-risk, long-vega — orthogonal to short-vol put selling)
spec.mechanism: VIX term structure is in contango (VIX3M > VIX) roughly 75-85% of the time because hedgers and funds pay a persistent term premium for longer-dated index portfolio insurance (the same hedging-demand premium that bleeds long-vol ETFs ~50-60%/yr on roll). A near-the-money put calendar (sell ~30 DTE front put, buy ~75-90 DTE back put on SPY/QQQ) is theta-positive in calm contango — the front put decays faster than the back — so it harvests a slice of that term premium. Crucially the structure is net-LONG vega and long the back-month convexity: in a vol spike the back leg's vega gain dominates, so the position P&L turns POSITIVE on the exact days a naked-put book has its worst losses. The return driver is the term-structure SLOPE, not the vol LEVEL, which is what makes the stream economically distinct from the incumbent's short-vol theta. Risk is hard-capped at the net debit (back put always more valuable than front at any spot), so there is no assignment-blowout tail.
spec.hypothesisH0: In months where the strategy is permitted to trade (engine VIX3M/VIX contango ratio > 1.087, i.e. candidate's VIX/VIX3M < 0.92, AND VIX9D/VIX < 1.0), a delta-neutral ~30/~80 DTE ATM put calendar on SPY/QQQ held to ~15 DTE or 50% of max value, after realistic 4-leg bid/ask + slippage + $0.65/contract commissions, produces a per-trade net return on net debit that is statistically indistinguishable from zero (mean net edge <= 0 bps of debit), AND its daily equity-curve return correlation to the incumbent cash-secured-put sim curve is >= +0.3 (i.e. it is NOT meaningfully orthogonal).
spec.hypothesisH1: Gated as above, the calendar earns a positive mean net edge of roughly +50 to +300 bps of net debit per trade (central estimate ~+150 bps on SPY, lower/often-negative on QQQ), AND its daily-return correlation to the incumbent put-seller curve is <= +0.1 with a strongly NEGATIVE correlation conditional on VIX up-spike days (>3 vol-point VIX jumps) — i.e. it pays off on the put-seller's worst days. Realized over >= 60 gated trades, Sharpe of the standalone leg is modest (~0.4-0.8) but it improves the blended (put-seller + calendar) Sortino by cutting left-tail days.
spec.signalDefinition: Use the engine's native contango convention (VIX3M/VIX, the inverse of the candidate's VIX/VIX3M). Inputs all already fetched: ^VIX, ^VIX3M, ^VIX9D via /api/forecast macro block (keys vix, vix3m, vix9d) and RegimeSnapshot. Define daily: slope = VIX3M_t / VIX_t (engine row[163] base ratio); slopeMom5 = slope_t - slope_{t-5} (engine feature row[163] EXACTLY); frontCalm = VIX9D_t / VIX_t (engine feature row[115] / RegimeSnapshot.vixTermRatio). ENTER-PERMITTED flag = (slope >= 1.087) AND (frontCalm < 1.00) AND (slopeMom5 >= -0.02, i.e. contango not collapsing) AND (RegimeSnapshot.regime != 'CRISIS', i.e. VIX < 35). Position scale s = clamp((slope - 1.087) / (1.20 - 1.087), 0, 1) so size ramps from 0 at the threshold to full at slope=1.20 (steeper contango => bigger size). KILL/EXIT flag = (slope <= 1.00, backwardation — the Feb-2018 switch) OR (slopeMom5 <= -0.05, contango collapsing fast) OR (RegimeSnapshot.regime == 'CRISIS'). Legs are PUTS (BS engine only prices puts: putPrice/putGreeks in black-scholes.ts; do NOT use calls without put-call parity). Front leg = the listed SPY/QQQ expiry with DTE closest to 30 (target 25-38); back leg = expiry with DTE closest to 80 (target 70-95). Strike K = the single common strike nearest ATM (|K - underlyingPrice| minimized) that exists in BOTH expiries; per-leg IV read directly from OptionContract.impliedVolatility, price/mid from (bid+ask)/2 in the chain. Net debit D = backMid - frontMid (must be > 0). Net vega must be > 0 and |net delta| <= 0.07 per 1-share (BS putGreeks); if not, shift K by one strike to the nearest that satisfies both.
spec.entryRule: Once per week, on the first trading day each Monday EOD (rebalanceDays=5), for each of SPY and QQQ independently: if ENTER-PERMITTED is true and no calendar in that symbol is already open, open ONE ATM put calendar (sell front ~30 DTE put, buy back ~80 DTE put, common strike K nearest ATM present in both expiries). Number of contracts n = floor( (riskBudget_symbol) / (D * 100) ), where riskBudget_symbol = s * 0.05 * Equity (i.e. at most 5% of equity at risk per symbol, scaled by contango-steepness s, since max loss = net debit). Require: front and back leg each have openInterest >= 500 and (ask-bid)/mid <= 0.15 on BOTH legs (liquidity gate; SPY/QQQ monthlies pass, illiquid weeklies are skipped). Skip entry in the 7 calendar days surrounding a scheduled FOMC day or if any major index-moving macro print is inside the front-leg's first week (approximate from the engine's calendar/OPEX features + a static FOMC date list), because front-week event premium breaks the calm assumption. Record net debit D, both leg IVs, slope, frontCalm, and n into simulated_trades (instrument = option_spread; store the spread as a single composite position with collateral = D*100*n).
spec.exitRule: Close the entire calendar (buy back front, sell back leg) at the EARLIEST of: (a) front leg reaches ~15 DTE (roll/close to avoid front-leg gamma — analogous to the incumbent's 21-DTE rule, but tighter because we WANT to exit before the front loses its theta advantage); (b) mark-to-market value of the spread >= 1.30 * D (take +30% of debit profit — calendars peak when spot sits near K and decay if spot drifts); (c) spread MTM <= 0.55 * D (cut at -45% of debit: spot has run far from K and the structure is bleeding); (d) KILL flag fires (slope <= 1.00 OR slopeMom5 <= -0.05 OR CRISIS) — in a backwardation flip the calendar is exited because the term-premium thesis is invalidated, even though the long-vega back leg is simultaneously cushioning the loss. On a normal close, immediately re-evaluate ENTER-PERMITTED for a fresh calendar next Monday. MTM each EOD by re-pricing both legs with putPrice using current underlyingPrice and the latest per-leg chain IV (or BS-implied from current mids).
spec.instruments: Defined-risk ATM put calendar (debit) spreads on SPY and QQQ only (deepest, tightest index option chains in the universe). Sell ~30 DTE front put, buy ~80 DTE back put at a common ATM strike. Max loss strictly capped at net debit paid; no naked leg, no assignment-blowout tail (front is covered by the longer back put at every spot). Equity-only universe restriction to SPY/QQQ is deliberate — single-name ATM calendars have spreads too wide to clear costs.
spec.positionSizing: Fixed starting capital (the Neon sim fund). Per symbol, riskBudget = s * 5% * Equity where s = clamp((VIX3M/VIX - 1.087)/(1.20-1.087),0,1); contracts n = floor(riskBudget / (netDebit*100)). Hard caps: total capital-at-risk across both calendars <= 10% of Equity at any time (sum of net debits); never more than 1 open calendar per symbol; if Equity drawdown from peak > 8%, halve riskBudget until a new equity high. Because max loss = net debit, the dollar risk is fully known at entry and sizing is exact. Idle capital stays in cash (no leverage). Expressed in the engine as TargetWeight with weight = (netDebit*100*n)/Equity on a synthetic spread symbol, instrument='option_spread'.
spec.dataSources: ALL ALREADY IN THE REPO, free/daily, no new infra: (1) ^VIX, ^VIX3M, ^VIX9D — fetched in src/app/api/forecast/route.ts macro block (keys vix, vix3m, vix9d) and surfaced as RegimeSnapshot.vixTermRatio (=VIX9D/VIX) in src/lib/strategies/types.ts; the VIX3M/VIX slope and its 5d momentum are engine feature row[163] and the VIX9D/VIX ratio is row[115] in src/lib/itransformer-features.ts. (2) Full SPY/QQQ option chains per expiry via getOptionsChain(symbol, expirationDate) in src/lib/yahoo-finance.ts — OptionContract exposes strike/expiration/dte/type/bid/ask/lastPrice/volume/openInterest/impliedVolatility; NOTE each call returns only ONE expiry (result.options[0]), so build the calendar with two calls (front + back expiry from expirationDates). (3) Black-Scholes engine src/lib/black-scholes.ts (putPrice, putGreeks, impliedVolatility) for MTM, net delta/vega, and to verify the chain IVs. (4) Cost model src/lib/backtest/costs.ts (optionCommissionPerContract=0.65; add an option-spread spread/slippage term). (5) curveCorrelation() in src/lib/backtest/metrics.ts for the orthogonality test. (6) Neon simulated_trades for persistence (store the spread as one composite position). NEW DATA NEEDED: none for the EOD sim. A static FOMC-date list (free, hand-maintained ~8 dates/yr) for the event-skip filter; for live fills, daily snapshots of front/back ATM bid/ask (already obtainable from the chain) to calibrate the spread-cost term over time.
spec.capacityNote: Capacity at retail size is effectively unlimited — SPY/QQQ option open interest is in the hundreds of thousands of contracts at ATM strikes and daily option volume is in the millions; a sim fund trading a handful of calendars per month is invisible to the market. The binding constraint is NOT capacity but COST EROSION: the edge is small per trade and concentrated in a 4-leg round-trip bid/ask crossing. Honest cost arithmetic (SPY at $590, ~$767 net debit/contract): at a favorable $0.02/share half-spread per leg friction is ~138 bps of debit; at a fair $0.04/share it is ~243 bps; at a realistic $0.06/share ~347 bps; QQQ is worse (~350-530 bps) because its ATM spreads are wider and debit fraction smaller. So the strategy is only viable on SPY in genuinely calm, tight-market contango, and is marginal-to-negative on QQQ after costs. It must be run with marketable-but-patient limit orders at the mid (not market orders), and it should be sized small. Its justification is primarily ORTHOGONALITY (a long-vega leg that pays on the put-seller's worst days), not standalone return; treated as a standalone alpha it would likely fail the cost hurdle.
spec.falsifier: Run the walk-forward sim (src/lib/backtest/engine.ts) on >= 8 years of SPY+QQQ daily chains under the gating rules with DEFAULT_COSTS plus an option-spread half-spread of $0.04/share/leg. The spec is REFUTED if EITHER: (1) the mean per-trade net return on net debit across all gated trades is <= 0 bps with a 95% CI upper bound below +50 bps (i.e. costs eat the term premium — H0 not rejected); OR (2) curveCorrelation() between this strategy's daily equity curve and the incumbent cash-secured-put sim curve is >= +0.2 over the common window AND the conditional mean return on the worst-decile VIX-spike days is NOT positive (i.e. it fails to diversify the short-vol book — it is just expensive beta-1 long-vol, not an orthogonal hedge). A single clean probe that pre-empts the full backtest: on the 10 largest single-day VIX jumps in the sample, if the marked calendar P&L is not positive on at least 7 of 10, the long-vega orthogonality claim is false and the whole rationale collapses.

redteam.lens: COSTS & CAPACITY (retail frictions: commissions, options bid/ask spread, slippage, assignment, min position granularity vs fixed capital, capacity)
redteam.kill: The gross edge does not clear the round-trip cost under any honest fill assumption, and the spec concedes this in its own text. expectedEdgeBpsPerTrade=250 vs estCostBpsPerTrade=220 leaves only +30 bps — inside the noise of a single tick. The spec's own capacityNote cost ladder shows the trade is already dead at fair fills: $0.02/share half-spread = 138 bps, $0.04 (the falsifier's own DEFAULT assumption) = 243 bps, $0.06 = 347 bps; QQQ = 350-530 bps. My independent arithmetic on SPY at $590, D=$767/contract confirms it: 4-leg commissions alone = $2.60 = 34 bps; a realistic blended half-spread (front ~30 DTE penny-wide at $0.015, back ~80 DTE nickel-wide at $0.05 — the back leg is the cost driver because longer-dated SPY ATM puts trade in nickel increments, not pennies) gives ~169 bps of spread + 34 bps commission = ~203 bps round-trip, eating ~80% of a 250 bps gross edge before any slippage. At a realistic $0.06 fill the net is NEGATIVE (-97 bps on SPY, deeply negative on QQQ). Worse, the -45% (0.55D) and +30% (1.30D) MTM stops fire precisely on spot runs / vol moves when the back-leg spread has WIDENED, so the exit leg is crossed at far worse than mid — adverse-selection slippage the 'patient mid limit' assumption ignores. Two structural amplifiers make it worse than the headline bps: (1) Granularity vs fixed capital — sizing n=floor(s*5%*Equity/(D*100)) with s ramping only across the razor-thin 1.087->1.20 slope band yields n=0 or n=1 for a $25k-$50k retail 'Neon sim fund' across most of the permitted regime, so the $15+ fixed per-spread friction cannot be amortized and many 'permitted' weeks place ZERO contracts (floor(0)=0). (2) The repo cannot even charge these costs honestly: engine.ts trades share holdings on equity notional via tradeCost() at ~10 bps round-trip and NEVER references optionCommissionPerContract; there is no option_spread instrument, no per-leg bid/ask, no 4-leg fill. Running the spec's synthetic spread symbol through the existing engine would undercharge option costs by ~20x and fabricate a passing result — the falsifier's '>=8yr walk-forward with DEFAULT_COSTS + $0.04/leg' is not executable without writing a new option-cost path that does not exist.
redteam.mitigation: For the edge to survive costs it would need to be true that: (a) gross edge is materially higher than 250 bps — e.g. >500 bps per gated trade — which contradicts the spec's own central estimate (~+150 bps SPY) and the empirical 25-49% on-debit calendar returns that are gross-of-cost and highly spot-path dependent; (b) restrict to SPY ONLY, never QQQ (spec half-concedes QQQ is marginal-to-negative); (c) fills genuinely achieved at mid via resting limit orders on BOTH the wide back leg and on the stop-driven exits (unrealistic on the exit triggers, which are momentum-driven); (d) account equity large enough (>=$250k) that n>=6-10 contracts amortizes fixed commissions; (e) build a real option-spread cost path into engine.ts (per-leg bid/ask from the chain, 4 contract-legs x $0.65, an explicit back-leg-weighted half-spread term) before any backtest number can be trusted. Even with all of these, the honest case is a few tens of bps of net edge — not a standalone alpha. The only defensible justification is the one the spec itself retreats to: ORTHOGONALITY / left-tail hedging, not standalone return. That is a different claim and must be judged on the diversification/hedge lens, not costs.
redteam.residual: As a STANDALONE return stream: no residual edge — net of realistic 4-leg options frictions and stop-driven exit slippage it is approximately zero on SPY at fair fills and negative at realistic fills, and negative on QQQ. The spec essentially admits this ('treated as a standalone alpha it would likely fail the cost hurdle'). The ONLY surviving rationale is non-cost: a long-vega leg that pays on the put-seller's worst days (the VIX-spike orthogonality / Sortino-improvement claim in H1). That residual is NOT defended by this lens — it must be tested by the falsifier's tail probe (positive P&L on >=7 of the 10 largest single-day VIX jumps) and the curveCorrelation orthogonality test, after a correct option-cost path is built into the engine. Net: it can only earn its place as a small, cost-subsidized tail hedge, never as a cost-clearing alpha. Under the costs-and-capacity lens specifically, it fails.

redteam.lens: DATA-SNOOPING, DECAY & ORTHOGONALITY
redteam.kill: The orthogonality thesis is mechanically false, and the falsifier cannot be run in this repo. (a) DECAY/SNOOPING: the "contango term premium" is the single most-mined, most-crowded retail vol anomaly — harvested by the entire short-VXX/SVXY/XIV complex, the textbook crowded trade that lost 96% in one session at Volmageddon (Feb-2018). Academic out-of-sample tests of contango-roll strategies "show slightly negative performance, with alpha deteriorating out-of-sample" (Quantpedia/CME). Re-routing the same premium through a put calendar does not create new alpha; it inherits the decayed, arbitraged premium plus a 4-leg cost drag the spec itself admits eats 138-530 bps. (b) ORTHOGONALITY (fatal): the spec's load-bearing claim — "net-LONG vega, in a vol spike the back leg's vega gain dominates, P&L turns POSITIVE on the put-seller's worst days" — is wrong for a short-front/long-back calendar. A long calendar is NEGATIVE GAMMA, positive vega. In a real vol spike, (i) spot gaps away from ATM K -> negative-gamma loss, and (ii) the term structure INVERTS (backwardation): front IV explodes far more than back IV, so the front short leg loses more than the long back leg gains — verified directly: "front-month options are much more sensitive to the vol spike, so losses on the short front leg typically exceed gains on the long back leg." The position most often LOSES on the put-seller's worst days. It is short-gamma/short-vol-in-disguise, positively correlated to the incumbent CSP book in stress, not an orthogonal long-vol hedge. The clean probe in the spec's own falsifier (positive P&L on 7 of 10 largest VIX jumps) would FAIL. Worse, the falsifier is uninstrumentable: engine.ts (L180-278) is a pure equity-share simulator marking priceAt(sym,i) close-to-close — no option pricing, no multi-leg spread P&L, no per-leg IV in the equity loop; the 'option_spread' enum is never branched on. data.ts fetches only daily OHLCV; there is NO historical options-chain/IV-surface data, so the claimed 8-year walk-forward on SPY/QQQ daily chains is impossible. And no incumbent CSP equity curve exists (registry has only 5 equity factor strategies; the CSP 'sim' is a discretionary Neon paper-log, not a dated daily curve), so curveCorrelation() cannot be run as specified. Finally the signal def is internally inconsistent: it calls row[163] both the 'base ratio' AND 'slopeMom5 EXACTLY,' but row[163] (L1539-1547) is ONLY the 5d CHANGE in VIX3M/VIX — the base ratio is never stored. The author did not read the feature being reused; the 1.087/1.20/0.92/+30%/-45% knobs are underived free parameters (curve-fit).
redteam.mitigation: For the edge to survive, ALL of the following would have to become true: (1) Replace the orthogonality mechanism — a short-front/long-back calendar is the wrong structure for tail protection. To pay on the put-seller's worst days you need a structure that is positive gamma AND positive vega (e.g., long front-dated puts / put backspread / long-vol overlay), accepting negative theta in calm. The spec's theta-positive-AND-crash-positive claim violates the gamma/vega/theta sign constraints of a single calendar and cannot be reconciled by parameter tuning. (2) Build genuine options infrastructure: source a historical SPY/QQQ option-chain / IV-surface dataset (e.g., OptionMetrics/ORATS/CBOE), add a multi-leg pricing-and-P&L branch to engine.ts that marks each leg with putGreeks under a stochastic IV-surface model, and wire 'option_spread' through the equity loop. (3) Construct an actual incumbent CSP daily equity curve (run a CSP strategy through the same engine over the same dates) so curveCorrelation() and the conditional VIX-spike-day test are computable. (4) Pre-register the parameter grid and show the result is stable across plausible thresholds (not just 1.087/1.20) and survives the admitted 138-530 bps round-trip cost at the realistic $0.04-0.06/share/leg. Only then is the claim testable; on the mechanics above it would still likely fail.
redteam.residual: Essentially none as specified. After the attack, the only defensible residue is the uncontested observation that VIX is in contango ~75-85% of the time — a real but fully-known, decayed, and crowded premium with negative out-of-sample alpha and high cost drag. The proposed vehicle does not capture it orthogonally: a long put calendar is short-gamma/short-vol in disguise and is positively (not negatively) correlated to a put-seller's book precisely on the worst days, so the diversification rationale — the spec's only stated justification ('treated as a standalone alpha it would likely fail the cost hurdle') — collapses. Net assessment: no orthogonal hedge value, no standalone alpha after costs, and no way to falsify the claim in this repo (no option-chain history, no spread P&L in the engine, no incumbent CSP curve). A correctly-built positive-gamma/positive-vega overlay COULD provide genuine left-tail diversification, but that is a different strategy with a guaranteed-negative carry, not the theta-positive contango-harvest this spec claims.

redteam.lens: Regime & Robustness (regime dependence, parameter sensitivity, crowding, tail risk; survival across 2018-Q4, Mar-2020, 2022, Aug-2024)
redteam.kill: The strategy's entire reason to exist — "net-LONG vega, so P&L turns POSITIVE on the put-seller's worst days" — is mechanistically false in the exact regimes it claims to hedge. A long put calendar is long LEVEL vega only under a PARALLEL IV shift. Every crisis named here produces the opposite: a front-loaded, INVERTING shock where 9D/30D IV spikes far more than 80D IV. The short front leg then gains value faster than the long back leg, so the calendar LOSES on a vol spike (confirmed by the literature: "your short leg can gain more than your long leg, producing losses even though you are net long vega... an inverted term structure is dangerous for calendars"). Compounding this, the structure is short gamma: in a 2018/2020/2022 gap, spot rips away from the ATM strike and the calendar collapses toward its decay trough. So the orthogonality claim ("pays off on the worst days," the falsifier's own 7-of-10 VIX-spike test) is the INVERSE of the historical mechanism — it bleeds toward the net-debit floor on the same days. Regime-by-regime: (1) Feb-2018/2018-Q4 — VIX +115% in one day, 9D inverts 1-2 days BEFORE the EOD weekly-cadence KILL flags (slope<=1.0, slopeMom5<=-0.05) can fire, so the kill is structurally a day late on the fast inversion that DEFINES these events. (2) Mar-2020 — VX1/spot fell to 0.75-0.80, deep sustained backwardation; the gate blocks NEW entries but any open calendar at the late-Feb onset gaps THROUGH the -45% stop on an opening print the stop cannot honor. (3) 2022 — grinding repeated mini-inversions whipsaw slope/slopeMom5, triggering repeated KILL exits each crossing a 4-leg bid/ask: death by cost-laden whipsaw. (4) Aug-2024 — 9D spiked and reverted within ~48h, far faster than a Monday-EOD gate can act. Finally, the spec ALREADY admits the edge (~+150 bps SPY central) sits INSIDE its own cost band (138-347 bps), negative on QQQ — so even in the calm contango it is gated to, it is a coin flip after costs, and it is structurally negative in the tail it was built to hedge. It is expensive, slightly-negative beta-1 long-vol mislabeled as an orthogonal crisis hedge.
redteam.mitigation: To survive, the spec would need: (1) to drop the orthogonality/crisis-hedge claim entirely and re-justify itself purely as a small calm-contango theta harvest — but it concedes that fails the cost hurdle standalone; (2) replacement of the long PUT calendar with a structure that is actually long term-structure vega and long gamma in a front-loaded inversion (e.g., a back-ratio or a long front-month straddle/OTM put hedge), which is a different strategy; (3) an intraday or at-minimum daily kill that acts on a 9D/VIX inversion trigger rather than weekly Monday-EOD cadence, plus gap-aware stops (the -45% MTM stop is unenforceable through overnight gaps); (4) honest empirical proof on the four named windows that the 7-of-10 VIX-spike-day P&L test is actually passed — the literature and term-structure mechanics predict it fails; (5) walk-forward robustness showing the sign survives perturbation of the 1.087/1.20/0.92 thresholds, 30/80 DTE, and 1.30/0.55 stops, since the live edge is smaller than the admitted cost dispersion. None of these are minor edits; they replace the thesis.
redteam.residual: Minimal-to-none on the lens's own terms. What plausibly survives is a tiny, regime-fragile calm-contango theta carry on SPY ONLY, in tight markets, sized very small — but the spec itself prices its central edge (~150 bps) below its realistic cost band (~243-347 bps), so even that residual is likely <=0 after honest 4-leg costs. The marketed value (orthogonal long-vega crisis diversifier that pays on the put-seller's worst days) has zero residual: it is mechanically backwards in front-loaded inversions, which is precisely what 2018-Q4, Mar-2020, 2022, and Aug-2024 were. Net: no robust, regime-independent, crowding-resistant, tail-positive edge remains. Treat as REFUTED until a walk-forward over the four crisis windows demonstrates positive conditional P&L on VIX-up-spike days — the prior here is strongly that it will not.
